description:
About the job

Role: Online (Remote)
Job Type: Part-Time Contract
Location: Remote (Work From Home)
Compensation: Up to $7.50 USD/hr, paid weekly




About the Role

We are looking for fluent language professionals to help train and evaluate AI language models. This is a flexible, part-time contract role you can do entirely from home, on your own schedule. If you work with language professionally - as a writer, teacher, translator, tutor, linguist, or language specialist - this role is a strong fit.




What You'll Do

Read and evaluate AI-generated text for quality, accuracy, and fluency
Write short prompts and creative responses to help train AI systems
Rank and compare AI outputs based on clear evaluation criteria
Provide written feedback on language quality




What We're Looking For

Fluency in your language and English
Bachelor's degree or higher, preferably in a humanities, language, or writing-related field
Professional experience working with language - writing, teaching, translating, editing, annotating, or related fields
Strong attention to detail and ability to follow evaluation guidelines
Background in linguistics, academic writing, data annotation, or AI content evaluation is a plus




What You Get

Up to $7.50 USD/hr, paid weekly
Fully remote - work from anywhere
Flexible hours - you set your own schedule
Part-time contract - ideal as a primary or supplemental income source




Equal Opportunity Employer:

We hire based on skills and expertise. All qualified candidates are welcome regardless of background, experience, or prior employment history. Applications are reviewed solely on demonstrated technical ability and qualifications.

Qualcomm is a company of inventors that unlocked 5G ushering in an age of rapid acceleration in connectivity and new possibilities that will transform industries, create jobs, and enrich lives. description:
About the job

Launch Your Next Successful Chapter with Veta Virtual! 🚀




💸 Competitive Salary in USD | 🌍 100% Remote | 🎯 Paid Search & Social Role | 🏆 Award-Winning Agency Culture




Veta Virtual is hiring a Paid Search & Social Specialist for a high-growth client in the Digital Marketing industry — an award-winning agency specializing in the automotive vertical, recognized as Best Large SEO Agency and winner of Best Low Budget PPC Campaign at the 2024 US Search Awards. This is a learning-forward role for someone curious, resourceful, and ready to run real campaigns that push what is possible.




🔑 Your Role:




You will manage and optimize paid search and social campaigns for automotive dealership clients — trained and mentored by top specialists while taking real ownership of results. Your day-to-day will involve:




Managing and optimizing Google Ads and Meta Ads campaigns — delivering higher ROI and lower cost per conversion for automotive dealership clients.
Conducting daily account audits, budget pacing, and campaign adjustments to keep performance on track and spend optimized.
Generating performance reports from Google Ads and Meta — and conducting client calls to present strategies and results clearly and confidently.
Managing OEM reimbursement processes — reviewing requirements, gathering materials, and submitting claims accurately and on time.
Using Google Analytics, Data Studio, Tag Manager, Merchant Center, and Meta Ads Manager to analyze data and drive campaign improvements.
Collaborating with SEOs, writers, and designers on new experiments and client work — contributing to a team that tests, learns, and pushes boundaries daily.




✅ What You'll Need:




1+ year of hands-on Google Ads experience — able to build, manage, and optimize paid search campaigns confidently.
1+ year of Meta Ads experience — proficient in Facebook and Instagram campaign setup, targeting, and optimization inside Ads Manager.
Working knowledge of Google Analytics, Google Data Studio, Google Ads Editor, Google Tag Manager, and Google Merchant Center.
Prior agency experience — you understand client management, reporting, and the pace of agency work.
Detail-oriented, research-driven, and a strong communicator in written and spoken English.
Bonus: automotive vertical experience, OEM reimbursement knowledge, or campaigns you can show us with real results.
To be considered for this role, you must be currently based in Latin America (LATAM) or Europe.




💼 What We Offer:




💸 Competitive salary in USD — paid consistently every month.
🌴 10 paid vacation days per year plus all U.S. federal holidays.
💻 100% remote — open to candidates based in LATAM or Europe.
📈 Direct mentorship from top paid media specialists — real room to grow and own your campaigns inside a fast-moving agency.
🏆 A high-performance, collaborative culture where testing, learning, and pushing boundaries are part of daily life.
🤝 A supportive environment through Veta Virtual and our client where curiosity is rewarded and your growth is taken seriously from day one.




Ready to Apply?




If you are ready to run real campaigns, learn from the best, and grow inside one of the most innovative digital marketing agencies in the automotive space — apply now and launch your next chapter with Veta Virtual!




Once you apply, you will complete a short 2-minute video question — come ready to tell us about campaigns you have run and results you have driven. We can't wait to meet you!

title: Email Marketing Account Manager
skills: You are proficient in Project management, Marketing strategy, Email marketing, Data analytics, Copywriting.
You have the potential to develop in Slack, Shopify, Notion, Figma.
English - Fully fluent
Minh Vo - Founder
description:
About the job

I’m helping Email Clout find a top candidate to join their team flexible for the role of Email Marketing Account Manager.




You'll drive revenue and client success by independently managing end-to-end email marketing campaigns.




Compensation:

USD 300 - 1.5K/month




Location:

Remote: Mexico




Mission of Email Clout:

"Empower online brands with high-impact, revenue-driving email campaigns. Fast, accountable, and world-class results."




What makes you a strong candidate:

You are proficient in Project management, Marketing strategy, Email marketing, Data analytics, Copywriting.
You have the potential to develop in Slack, Shopify, Notion, Figma.
English - Fully fluent




Responsibilities and more:

Email Clout is an email marketing agency that partners with online brands to create unique, engaging, and revenue-generating email campaigns and flows. We’re fully remote, small, and love to move fast. We hold ourselves accountable for delivering a world-class client experience and industry-shattering email performance. 




We are looking for the ideal candidate for the role of Email Marketing Account Manager, who will be responsible for the experience and performance of client accounts. In this role, you will manage and execute email marketing campaigns and flows from start to finish. This includes coming up with ideas, planning, writing copy, communicating with clients and internal teams, as well as implementation in Klaviyo. You will use Klaviyo and Figma while managing your tasks independently through Notion. Below are the key responsibilities for this role. 




- Create and manage multiple email content calendars. 

- Plan, write, and schedule email marketing campaigns and flows. 

- Collect, report, present, and analyze past data to make the best decisions for clients. 

- Conduct and lead client calls. 

- Handle difficult questions related to email performance. 

- Make design and copy changes in Figma. 

- Collaborate and manage designers and copywriters. 

- Stay up-to-date with industry trends and best practices in email marketing. 




Software stack you’ll be using: 

- Slack 

- Klaviyo 

- Figma 

- Shopify 

- Notion 




Requirements: 

- 1-2 years of experience in email marketing, preferably with experience using Klaviyo. 

- Proficiency in Figma or similar design software. 

- Excellent written and verbal communication skills. 

- Strong attention to detail and multitasking ability. 

- Familiarity with email marketing best practices and metrics. 

- Confident in communicating with clients. 

- Perfect English (spoken and written). 




Do Not Apply If: 

- You’re okay with delays or errors in client work. 

- You’re willing to work only during regular hours. 

- You can only write when assisted by ChatGPT. 

- You don’t take deadlines seriously. 

- You lack urgency in completing work. 

- You don’t have the resourcefulness to solve problems on your own first. 

- You don’t feel confident in your email marketing abilities.




Your potential leader(s):

Minh Vo - Founder



Desired Skills and Experience

Project management, Marketing strategy, Email marketing, Data analytics, Copywriting, Client communication, Slack, Shopify, Notion, Figma

description:
About the job

About Puig




At Puig, we are a family-owned company with a long-term commitment to creativity, innovation, and people development. Through our Graduate Program, we are looking for exceptional young talent ready to begin their professional journey in a dynamic, international, and purpose-driven environment.




This is a unique opportunity to accelerate your career, gain exposure to a leading global organization, and contribute to the growth of prestigious dermatological brands that improve people's quality of life through science-backed skincare solutions.




Your Mission




As a Marketing Graduate (Entry Level) within our Derma Division, you will support the Marketing team in the development and execution of brand strategies, product launches, and commercial initiatives while gaining hands-on experience across multiple areas of the business.




You will work closely with cross-functional teams including Medical Affairs, Sales, Trade Marketing, Digital, and Finance, contributing to projects that have a direct impact on business performance and patient outcomes.




What You'll Do




• Support the planning, execution, and optimization of digital marketing campaigns across social media platforms, with a strong focus on TikTok and emerging digital channels.

• Assist in the creation, coordination, and publishing of engaging content aligned with brand strategies, consumer trends, and business objectives.

• Collaborate closely with the Digital & Marketing team to manage community engagement, monitor social conversations, and identify opportunities to increase brand relevance and reach.

• Support influencer and content creator initiatives, including campaign coordination, performance tracking, and reporting.

• Monitor digital KPIs and campaign performance, providing insights and recommendations to improve engagement, visibility, and business impact.

• Contribute to the development and management of a dedicated social media project aimed at healthcare professionals, helping create educational and scientifically relevant content for derma brands.

• Coordinate with Medical Visit and cross-functional teams to ensure content accuracy, compliance, and alignment with brand objectives.

• Assist in the preparation of presentations, reports, and business reviews related to digital performance, marketing initiatives, and key projects.

• Participate in local and global marketing projects, gaining exposure to brand management, consumer engagement, and omnichannel strategies.

• Provide support on ad hoc marketing initiatives, product launches, events, and special projects across the Derma division.




Who You Are

Recent graduate (Bachelor's degree completed within the last 1–2 years) in Marketing, Business Administration, Communication, Pharmaceutical Business, or related fields.
Previous internship, trainee, student leadership, or extracurricular experience is highly valued.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
Excellent communication and presentation abilities.
Curious, proactive, and eager to learn.
Collaborative mindset and ability to work in diverse teams.
Passion for marketing, healthcare, skincare, and consumer behavior.
Comfortable working in a fast-paced and dynamic environment.




Requirements

Bachelor's degree completed by the start date.
Intermediate English proficiency (written and spoken).
Advanced knowledge of Microsoft Office (Excel and PowerPoint preferred).
Availability to work full-time.
Authorization to work in Mexico.




What We Offer

Participation in a global Graduate Program designed to accelerate your development.
Exposure to senior leaders and international projects.
Structured learning and development opportunities.
Mentorship and career guidance.
A collaborative, inclusive, and innovative culture.
Opportunity to build a long-term career within Puig.




Our Commitment

At Puig, we believe that diversity, equity, and inclusion drive innovation and creativity. We are committed to creating an environment where everyone can thrive, contribute, and grow.

Join us and help shape the future of dermatological skincare through science, innovation, and purpose. ✨

title: Content & SEO Specialist

AI-Assisted Blog Content → CMS Publishing
Use AI tools (ChatGPT, Claude, or similar) to generate first drafts from content briefs and keywords
Rewrite and refine AI output so it reads natural, human, and brand-consistent
Fact-check, improve clarity, and ensure editorial quality (no “AI-sounding” content)
Publish 2–4 blog posts per week into a CMS (proper formatting: H1/H2/H3, meta title, meta description, slug)
Source and integrate relevant visuals for every post (hero images, diagrams, screenshots where relevant)
Ensure images are contextually relevant, correctly sized, and include alt text
Add internal links following the hub-and-spoke structure
Implement FAQ schema markup where applicable (training provided)
Maintain tracking in the content calendar (status, publish date, URL, performance notes)
Weekly Newsletter

Curate 2–3 external insights (industry news, posts, tools, studies)
Write concise commentary and framing around each item
Include links to newly published content
Maintain a clear, non-promotional tone focused on value and relevance
Schedule, test, and send each issue
Monitor open and click-through rates weekly and flag performance trends
LinkedIn Content (1–2 Posts Weekly)
Write 1–2 posts per week for a company LinkedIn page
Avoid generic “corporate thought leadership” tone — keep it specific, practical, and grounded
Pull insights from blog content, product use cases, customer conversations, or sales insights
Create or source supporting visuals (screenshots, simple graphics, text-based visuals)
Schedule posts using a social scheduling tool or native platform
Monitor engagement and flag important comments or mentions for response
Template Pages & Gated Content
Write landing page copy for template downloads (based on provided briefs)
Format templates into usable assets (PDF, Slides, or Docs)
Publish gated landing pages in CMS with email capture integration
Ensure proper metadata, internal linking, and conversion flow
Maintain consistency between page copy and downloadable asset
Strong experience using AI writing tools effectively (knows the difference between edited output and raw AI content)
Ability to write clearly, directly, and without corporate or filler language
Experience publishing content in a CMS environment
Strong organizational skills and ability to manage workload independently
Ability to source relevant, context-aware visuals
Comfortable receiving editorial feedback and iterating quickly
Experience with CMS platforms (e.g., Webflow or similar)
Email marketing experience
Basic SEO understanding (metadata, structure, internal linking)
Experience writing for B2B audiences (sales, revenue, or SaaS context)
Familiarity with CRM ecosystems
Experience with LinkedIn content creation and distribution
description:
About the job

Hi there! We are South and our client is looking for a Content & SEO Specialist!

Note To Applicants


Eligibility: This position is open to candidates residing in Latin America. 
Application Language: Please submit your CV in English. Applications submitted in other languages will not be considered. 
Professional Presentation: We encourage you to showcase your professional experience by including a Loom video in the application form. While this is optional, candidates who provide a video presentation will be given priority. 
Interview Policy: The use of artificial intelligence (AI) tools during interviews are strictly prohibited. 
Note: As part of the final stages of our hiring process, candidates may be asked to provide professional references for a reference check. If hired, you may also be asked to update your LinkedIn profile :)


The Core Job (Non-Negotiable)

These Four Areas Define Success In This Role


 AI-Assisted Blog Content → CMS Publishing


You will execute a structured content strategy that includes keyword research, content briefs, and a hub-and-spoke architecture already in place.

Your responsibility is to turn briefs into high-quality, published content.


Use AI tools (ChatGPT, Claude, or similar) to generate first drafts from content briefs and keywords
Rewrite and refine AI output so it reads natural, human, and brand-consistent
Fact-check, improve clarity, and ensure editorial quality (no “AI-sounding” content)
Publish 2–4 blog posts per week into a CMS (proper formatting: H1/H2/H3, meta title, meta description, slug)
Source and integrate relevant visuals for every post (hero images, diagrams, screenshots where relevant)
Ensure images are contextually relevant, correctly sized, and include alt text
Add internal links following the hub-and-spoke structure
Implement FAQ schema markup where applicable (training provided)
Maintain tracking in the content calendar (status, publish date, URL, performance notes)
 Weekly Newsletter


Own a weekly email newsletter sent to a B2B audience (sales and revenue professionals).


Draft one newsletter per week in an email platform (e.g., Klaviyo or equivalent)
Curate 2–3 external insights (industry news, posts, tools, studies)
Write concise commentary and framing around each item
Include links to newly published content
Maintain a clear, non-promotional tone focused on value and relevance
Schedule, test, and send each issue
Monitor open and click-through rates weekly and flag performance trends
 LinkedIn Content (1–2 Posts Weekly)


Manage consistent organic presence on LinkedIn.


Write 1–2 posts per week for a company LinkedIn page
Avoid generic “corporate thought leadership” tone — keep it specific, practical, and grounded
Pull insights from blog content, product use cases, customer conversations, or sales insights
Create or source supporting visuals (screenshots, simple graphics, text-based visuals)
Schedule posts using a social scheduling tool or native platform
Monitor engagement and flag important comments or mentions for response
 Template Pages & Gated Content


Build and publish downloadable, conversion-focused content assets.


Write landing page copy for template downloads (based on provided briefs)
Format templates into usable assets (PDF, Slides, or Docs)
Publish gated landing pages in CMS with email capture integration
Ensure proper metadata, internal linking, and conversion flow
Maintain consistency between page copy and downloadable asset


Who This Role Is For

This role is for someone who is highly proficient in AI-assisted content production and can maintain quality at volume.

Required Skills


Strong experience using AI writing tools effectively (knows the difference between edited output and raw AI content)
Ability to write clearly, directly, and without corporate or filler language
Experience publishing content in a CMS environment
Strong organizational skills and ability to manage workload independently
Ability to source relevant, context-aware visuals
Comfortable receiving editorial feedback and iterating quickly


Strong Plus


Experience with CMS platforms (e.g., Webflow or similar)
Email marketing experience
Basic SEO understanding (metadata, structure, internal linking)
Experience writing for B2B audiences (sales, revenue, or SaaS context)
Familiarity with CRM ecosystems
Experience with LinkedIn content creation and distribution


Schedule: Monday to Friday, 9 AM – 6 PM ET

Compensation: USD salary

Location: 100% remote opportunity

If this opportunity sounds good to you, send us your resume!

title: Marketing Specialist
skills: Digital Marketing Strategy: Develop and execute comprehensive marketing strategies aimed at first responder agencies and healthcare professionals.
Content Creation: Craft compelling content including blog posts, newsletters, and social media updates that resonate with our target audience.
SEO Management: Optimize website content for search engines to enhance visibility for relevant keywords related to mental health services for first responders.
Social Media Engagement: Manage social media platforms, creating engaging posts that drive awareness and interaction among followers.
Analytics and Reporting: Analyze marketing performance metrics to assess the effectiveness of campaigns and suggest improvements.
Audience Segmentation: Identify and segment target audiences to tailor messaging specifically for different groups, including agencies and individual first responders.
Networking and Outreach: Develop relationships with potential referral sources, including healthcare providers and first responder agencies, to expand our reach.
Collaboration: Work closely with our team to align marketing efforts with overall practice goals and client needs.
Experience: Minimum 2+ years of experience marketing for American-based companies (Healthcare or Service Industry preferred).
Technical Savvy: Current with modern marketing technologies and social media algorithms; ability to use AI tools to optimize workflow (but not rely on them for core creativity).
Communication: Exceptional written and verbal English skills. While primarily a creative role, you must be comfortable making occasional outbound "warm" contacts to build referral relationships.
Mindset: A "shark" mentality—proactive, aggressive in pursuing visibility, and capable of working without constant micromanagement.
Education: Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Communications, or a related field is highly preferred.
Monitoring Tools: Open to using monitoring tools like Hubstaff.
description:
About the job

✨ IMPORTANT – WATCH THIS QUICK LOOM VIDEO ON HOW TO GET HIRED:

📽️ https://www.loom.com/share/345f776084e642ba99c24aabe40a67a0

🇬🇧 C1 ENGLISH LEVEL IS REQUIRED.

The selected candidate will be speaking strictly in English during their entire shift.

🎤 PLEASE SUBMIT YOUR RESUME AND AUDIO RECORDING IN ENGLISH




Job Details




JOB TITLE

Marketing Specialist

JOB ID

BYRTIA1

INDUSTRY

Medical

LOCATION

LATAM / South Africa / PH if needed

JOB STATUS

Part Time (to full time)

WORK SCHEDULE

Monday to Friday, availability during EST business hours

SALARY

$8 - $11 per hour

TARGET START DATE

ASAP




ROLE OVERVIEW




About the Client:

The Client is a growing psychological therapy and testing practice. We specialize in providing high-quality therapy and diagnostic testing (e.g., for first responders and police candidates). We are currently at a pivotal growth phase and are looking for a proactive, tech-savvy marketing professional to help us overcome our current financial bottleneck and scale our team of clinicians.




About the Role:

Our Client is seeking a high-energy Marketing Specialist (Virtual Assistant). This is not a passive task-taking role; they need a strategic thinker who understands the American healthcare landscape and can proactively drive lead generation. You will be responsible for translating the clinical expertise of the Client into a digital presence that attracts the "right fit" clients—specifically those seeking therapy and testing rather than medication management.




Key Responsibilities

Digital Marketing Strategy: Develop and execute comprehensive marketing strategies aimed at first responder agencies and healthcare professionals.
Content Creation: Craft compelling content including blog posts, newsletters, and social media updates that resonate with our target audience.
SEO Management: Optimize website content for search engines to enhance visibility for relevant keywords related to mental health services for first responders.
Social Media Engagement: Manage social media platforms, creating engaging posts that drive awareness and interaction among followers.
Analytics and Reporting: Analyze marketing performance metrics to assess the effectiveness of campaigns and suggest improvements.
Audience Segmentation: Identify and segment target audiences to tailor messaging specifically for different groups, including agencies and individual first responders.
Networking and Outreach: Develop relationships with potential referral sources, including healthcare providers and first responder agencies, to expand our reach.
Collaboration: Work closely with our team to align marketing efforts with overall practice goals and client needs.




Qualifications & Skills

Experience: Minimum 2+ years of experience marketing for American-based companies (Healthcare or Service Industry preferred).
Technical Savvy: Current with modern marketing technologies and social media algorithms; ability to use AI tools to optimize workflow (but not rely on them for core creativity).
Communication: Exceptional written and verbal English skills. While primarily a creative role, you must be comfortable making occasional outbound "warm" contacts to build referral relationships.
Mindset: A "shark" mentality—proactive, aggressive in pursuing visibility, and capable of working without constant micromanagement.
Education: Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Communications, or a related field is highly preferred.
Monitoring Tools: Open to using monitoring tools like Hubstaff.

Monthly stipend

Opportunity to work closely with an award-winning global startup

description:
About the job

About Greether

Greether is an award-winning travel startup that empowers women to travel safer by connecting female travelers with verified, local women Greeters for safe, authentic, and sustainable travel experiences.

Operating in over 100 countries and 1,000+ cities, Greether is aligned with key Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs), working to reduce safety risks for women while creating economic opportunities through technology and tourism.

Greether was named Startup of the Year at WTM London, selected as one of PhocusWire's Hot 25 Travel Startups, and recognized by the UNWTO Awake Tourism Challenge. The company has been featured in Forbes, USA Today, and Fast Company and is backed by leading organizations including a16z, Expedia Group, Mercury, and the Untours Foundation.




About the Role

We're looking for a highly organized, proactive, and curious Growth & Partnerships Intern to join our team.

This is a unique opportunity to gain hands-on experience across partnerships, marketing, communications, community building, and startup operations. You will work closely with our leadership team and support initiatives that help grow Greether's global network of travelers, Greeters, ambassadors, and partners.

If you're passionate about travel, women's empowerment, relationship building, and learning how a fast-growing startup operates behind the scenes, we'd love to hear from you.

📌 Please note: This is a full-time internship (Monday to Friday, 9 AM–6 PM PST). We prioritize candidates based in Latin America and recent graduates or individuals seeking full-time professional experience.




Key Responsibilities

Support outreach and relationship-building efforts with potential partners, organizations, brands, tourism boards, and communities
Assist in managing and growing Greether's Brand Ambassador Program
Conduct research on potential partnership opportunities, events, destinations, and industry trends
Support the planning and execution of marketing and community-building campaigns
Assist with onboarding and engagement initiatives for Greeters and travelers
Help coordinate virtual and in-person events, and partnership activations
Support communications and follow-up with prospective collaborators and stakeholders
Contribute to content creation, storytelling, and community engagement initiatives
Assist with project coordination and operational tasks 




What We're Looking For

Recent graduate in marketing, communications, tourism, international relations, business, or a related field 
Excellent written and verbal communication skills in English
Strong organizational skills and attention to detail
Ability to manage multiple projects and priorities in a fast-paced startup environment
Comfortable conducting research and presenting findings clearly
Proactive, resourceful, and eager to learn
Interest in partnerships, marketing, community building, tourism, and social impact
Passion for women's empowerment, sustainable travel, and entrepreneurship
Available Monday to Friday, from 9 AM to 6 PM PST




Perks

Monthly stipend
Direct exposure to startup growth, partnerships, marketing, and operations
Opportunity to work closely with an award-winning global startup
Meaningful work that contributes to making travel safer and more inclusive
Networking opportunities with tourism leaders, partners, and communities worldwide
Potential for long-term growth within the company




📩 Interested?

Apply directly on LinkedIn AND send your CV to info@greether.com

We can't wait to hear from you!

description:
About the job

We are PMG

Named Ad Age Best Places to Work for 11 years in a row and Fast Company Best Workplaces for Innovators since 2022, PMG is a global independent marketing services and technology company that seeks to inspire people and brands that anything is possible. Driven by shared success, PMG leverages business strategy and transformation, creative, media, and insights—as well as its proprietary operating system, Alli—to deliver cutting-edge digital solutions and innovative media strategies.

Our team comprises over 900 employees globally, and our work for ambitious brands like Apple, Nike, Sephora, Experian, Therabody, Best Western Hotels & Resorts, Creed Fragrances, BNY, Intuit, CKE Restaurants, Travelex, and Whole Foods spans 85+ countries.

Professional proficiency in written and spoken English is required, including for resume submission and interviews.

Who You Will Be Working With

At PMG, our Influencer & Branded Content team is at the intersection of culture, storytelling, and strategy, helping brands connect with audiences in authentic, meaningful ways. We craft high-impact influencer campaigns that go beyond traditional advertising - driving engagement, building brand affinity, and delivering measurable results across social, digital, and experiential platforms.

You’ll collaborate with creators, talent agencies, media strategists, content teams, and brand partners to develop and execute influencer marketing programs that seamlessly align storytelling with business objectives. Whether it’s identifying the right talent, negotiating partnerships, overseeing content production, or optimizing campaign performance, your role will be instrumental in shaping how brands foster meaningful connections with their audiences.

Beyond execution, you’ll play a role in exploring new content formats, leveraging emerging platforms, and staying ahead of industry trends to ensure influencer strategies are not only relevant but also innovative and impactful. At PMG, we don’t just follow trends - we create them.

As an Influencer & Branded Content (title level), if you’re passionate about shaping culture through creator-driven storytelling, using data to drive real impact, and redefining what’s possible in influencer marketing, this is the place to make your mark.

What You Will Be Doing


Support the execution of influencer marketing campaigns from start to finish, helping to deliver outstanding, creative work for global brands. 
Serve as a reliable day-to-day contact for clients—managing updates, timelines, and deliverables to ensure smooth communication and collaboration. 
Partner with Account Managers and Directors to develop campaign briefs, creator contracts, and proposals, ensuring clarity and accuracy. 
Contribute to pitches and presentations, helping bring creative ideas to life through data, insights, and storytelling. 
Collaborate across influencer, creative, and strategy teams to deliver cohesive, effective client campaigns. 
Track campaign performance and analyse data to identify key insights and learnings. 
Maintain organized campaign folders, documentation, and reports, ensuring all information is accurate and easy to access. 
Stay current on creator trends, platform updates, and brand partnerships, identifying new opportunities to elevate client work. 
Monitor budgets, timelines, and performance targets to support the achievement of campaign and revenue goals. 
Demonstrate curiosity and initiative—actively seeking opportunities to improve processes and contribute to the team’s success. 


What You Will Bring


1-2+ years of experience in Influencer & Branded Content, with a bachelor’s degree or equivalent work experience. 
Understanding of content creation and influencer marketing strategy, supporting campaign execution and brand partnerships. 
Strong attention to detail orientation, ensuring accuracy and consistency in influencer and branded content initiatives. 
Experience in project implementation, helping coordinate and manage influencer collaborations. 
Basic proficiency in presentation skills, effectively communicating campaign results and insights. 
Familiarity with advertising standards, client service, and relationship management, ensuring seamless influencer partnerships. 
Exposure to influencer marketing dashboards and social media platforms, optimizing campaign tracking and performance analysis. 
Experience in networking and influencer management, fostering strong industry relationships for impactful collaborations. 
A commitment to curiosity and adaptability as a Continual Learner, staying at the forefront of media innovation while prioritizing client success with a Customer-Focused mindset that seeks opportunities to deliver meaningful value. 
A dedication to People-Focused leadership, fostering collaboration, innovation, and professional growth by Leading by Example and nurturing strong relationships that empower others to excel. 


What We Offer


Professional Development: Take advantage of our learning and development programs, mentorship opportunities, and career advancement support. 
Generous Time Off: Enjoy generous paid time off and holiday allowances to recharge and spend time with loved ones. 
Parental Leave: We provide paid parental leave to support your family during important life events. 
Retirement & Pension Plans: Plan for your future with competitive retirement or pension programs, including contribution matching. 
Fertility and Family Support: Access fertility benefits for all team members and their spouses. 
Healthcare: Coverage and support for everyday medical expenses and routine care, tailored by geography. 
Pet Insurance: Protect your pet's health and your finances. 
Lifestyle Spending Accounts: Enjoy 100% company-funded accounts to promote healthy habits and well-being. 
Commuter Benefits: Access support for travel and commuting needs, where available. 
Annual Bonus: All employees are eligible for an annual bonus. 
Volunteering Opportunities: Receive 8 give-back hours to volunteer in your local communities. 
AI Enterprise License: Access AI Enterprise accounts and participate in weekly AI training sessions to empower and ensure AI safety. 


Benefits vary by country, location, and geography to reflect local laws, norms, and expectations.

What Sets Us Apart

Being part of PMG means joining a company culture that’s unmatched in digital. We're dedicated to working hard to serve our employees and clients, delivering value, results, and innovation—which often requires true grit and agility. We believe in taking care of ourselves and each other to continuously improve in every way.

In alignment with our core values to be inclusive and always change for the better, PMG is committed to creating a more diverse and inclusive culture, and we are proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We believe that we only change for the better by bringing diverse perspectives to our company. PMG recruits, employs, trains, compensates, and promotes regardless of race, color, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, genetics, disability, age, or veteran status.

title: Volunteer Marketing and Communications Lead to Connect Stakeholders Working to End Deep Polarization with Colombia Construye Confianza
description:
About the job

This is an opportunity to work with an Ashoka Fellow 

Ashoka Fellows are leading social entrepreneurs that have been taken through a rigorous selection process, testing the quality of their ideas and character, to become part of the Ashoka Fellowship and Network. Ashoka Fellows take on the challenges of building innovative system change ideas to make and spread social impact in a world wrought with challenges. They refuse to let the ‘impossible’ stand in their way to creating a better world.

This is an unpaid, volunteer opportunity

About The Affiliated Organization

Colombia Construye Confianza (CCC) was founded to address one of Colombia's most urgent social challenges: deep polarization among citizens who have lost the ability to trust one another. We work with communities, organizations, and institutions to transform the way people relate, building the social trust that democracy and coexistence require. Our flagship methodology, BAKONDO, has reached over 3,200 people over more than 20 years, earning recognition from the World Economic Forum, Coca-Cola, and One Young World. Through experiential education, play, and art, we bring together volunteers and vulnerable communities to form empathetic leaders. Our most powerful program, BAKONDO Paz, unites ex-combatants, victims of the armed conflict, and social leaders to run a camp for children in vulnerable situations, turning former adversaries into builders of peace.

For more information, please visit www.colombiaconstruyeconfianza.org

Job Description

Colombia Construye Confianza is seeking a strategic marketing volunteer to help us design and deploy a comprehensive communications strategy that connects us with all our key stakeholders. We recently developed our brand concept and initial materials with a creative agency, and now need someone to take that foundation and turn it into an active, results-driven marketing operation. The volunteer will design the overall communications and marketing strategy for CCC and its initiatives, including our BAKONDO bootcamps. They will define the right channels, messages, and tactics to attract volunteers, corporate partners, donors, universities, and impact investors. They will also help manage and optimize our social media presence across Instagram, Facebook, LinkedIn, and WhatsApp, and support the team in executing campaigns consistently. Their work will directly determine how many people discover, trust, and join our mission, making it essential to the growth and sustainability of the organization. Please let us know today if you can help!

Volunteer Logistics

Estimated hours required per week: 5

The estimated duration of the project is: 3-6 months

This position is Virtual (Remote)

Language Requirements

Spanish

Desired Skills And Experience

We are looking for a professional with demonstrated experience in marketing strategy and digital communications, ideally within the social impact, NGO, or purpose-driven brand space. The ideal candidate has strong skills in content strategy, social media management, and audience segmentation, and knows how to translate a compelling mission into messages that move people to act. Experience working with multiple stakeholder audiences (corporate, institutional, and grassroots) is highly valued. Proficiency in Spanish is required; English is a plus. A background in impact communications, social entrepreneurship, or international development will help the volunteer hit the ground running.

title: Founding Growth Lead
skills: If you've taken an app from quiet to growing and want to do it again as an owner, not an employee — message me.
description:
About the job

I'm hiring a Founding Growth Lead for TalkPilot.




TalkPilot is an AI conversation co-pilot for iOS, Mac, and Apple Watch. The product is live and it's good. Distribution is the problem — and that's the entire job.




I'm a strong builder and a weak distributor. I need the opposite of me: someone who wakes up thinking about acquisition channels, activation, and retention curves, and knows how to turn a launched app into a growing one.




What you'd own:

 → The full growth function — paid (Apple Search Ads, Meta), ASO, organic, content, lifecycle

 → Finding the one channel that actually works and pouring fuel on it

 → The numbers: CAC, install→paid conversion, retention




This is a founding role. The equity is real and meaningful. The salary is early-stage and below what a funded company pays — that's the trade for ownership. Mexico City preferred, remote possible for the right person.




If you've taken an app from quiet to growing and want to do it again as an owner, not an employee — message me.

description:
About the job

I’m helping Equity Medical find a top candidate to join their team full-time for the role of Patient Recruiter & Retention Specialist.




You'll expand access to clinical trials by engaging diverse communities and advancing medical research.




Compensation:

USD 18K - 22K/year




Location:

Remote: Mexico




Mission of Equity Medical:

"Advance clinical research by providing patient-centered trials and expanding access to innovative treatments across diverse communities."




What makes you a strong candidate:

You are proficient in Scheduling, Retention management, Documentation.
You have the potential to develop in Clinical research, CRM tools.
Spanish - Fully fluent
English - Fully fluent




Responsibilities and more:

About Equity Medical

Equity Medical is a rapidly growing clinical research organization dedicated to expanding access to clinical trials in underserved communities. We are seeking a bilingual Patient Recruiter & Retention Specialist to support participant recruitment and enrollment efforts across multiple clinical research sites.




Position Overview

The Patient Recruiter & Retention Specialist will be responsible for engaging prospective study participants, conducting pre-screening activities, supporting enrollment efforts, and maintaining ongoing communication to improve study participation and retention.

This is a remote independent contractor position supporting U.S.-based clinical research operations.




Key Responsibilities

Contact and pre-screen warm digital leads through the CRM to assess preliminary study eligibility.

Follow up consistently with prospective participants to reduce no-shows and increase enrollment conversion rates.

Schedule and coordinate participant appointments as needed.

Maintain accurate and timely documentation within the CRM and related systems.

Collaborate closely with marketing, recruitment, and clinical operations teams to achieve study enrollment goals.

Attend weekly meetings with on-site clinical teams.

Attend weekly meetings with the marketing and recruitment departments.

Conduct outreach to potential participants within the community in a professional, ethical, and compliant manner.

Communicate effectively with both English-speaking and Spanish-speaking participants.

Assist with retention initiatives to improve participant engagement and study completion.

Perform additional administrative and recruitment-related duties as assigned.




Qualifications

Fluent in both English and Spanish (written and verbal) – required.

Experience in patient recruitment, customer service, sales, call center operations, healthcare, clinical research, or a related field preferred.

Strong phone presence and interpersonal skills.

Comfortable making a high volume of outbound and follow-up calls.

Ability to build rapport and communicate effectively with diverse populations.

Strong organizational and time management skills.

Experience using CRM systems is preferred.

Clinical research experience is a plus but not required.

Schedule Requirements

Required schedule: Saturday through Wednesday.

Working hours: 10:00 AM – 7:00 PM Pacific Time (San Diego, California).

Must be available to attend scheduled team meetings and collaborate with U.S.-based teams during business hours.

Must maintain reliable internet connectivity and a professional remote work environment.

Preferred Experience

Clinical research recruitment.

Patient engagement and retention.

Healthcare scheduling or patient coordination.

CRM platforms and lead management.

Experience supporting U.S.-based organizations.




Independent Contractor Status

This position is offered as an independent contractor engagement. Contractors are responsible for their own taxes, insurance, equipment, and compliance with applicable laws in their country of residence.




Your potential leader(s):

Grace Ulerio -



Desired Skills and Experience

Scheduling, Retention management, Documentation, Clinical research, CRM tools

description:
About the job

About Flipp

Flipp partners with the largest North American retailers and brands to deliver local promotions and savings to millions of engaged shoppers daily, driving superior returns on investments.

We help people make smarter shopping decisions with autonomy and accountability. With rising living costs, Flipp's mission is crucial. Our Shopper Consideration Platform allows retailers and manufacturers to create digital experiences from their savings & deals content, aiding shoppers in deciding what to buy and where to buy it. Together, we make a difference.

Our five principles, Progress Over Perfection, Clarity Through Transparency, Learn Loudly, Challenge with Empathy, and Always Build Better, bring a relentless progress mindset to life. They're not just slogans, but they're the behaviours we expect, reward, and hold ourselves accountable to. You'll be equipped to make an impact, realize your potential, and stay inspired every step of the way.

As a Senior Content & Social Specialist, you will own Flipp's B2B storytelling across the Americas, shaping how we show up as a trusted voice in the retail industry. Your focus will be on bringing global narratives to life at the regional level, translating business priorities, research, and market insights into compelling, high-impact content across key markets: the United States, Canada, Mexico, Colombia, and Brazil.

Beyond the regional scope, you will also play a key role globally. You will work closely with the People & Culture team on employer branding content for both Flipp and Shopfully, crafting narratives that strengthen our positioning as a global, integrated company and support talent attraction across markets. Employer branding is embedded in business storytelling, showcasing people, expertise, and impact through credible, narrative-driven content. This work naturally connects with internal communications, where you will strengthen knowledge sharing and engagement across the organization.

What you'll get the opportunity to do:


Own and drive the Social Media and Content Strategy across the United States, Canada, and Latin America, ensuring strong brand positioning and consistency while translating global narratives into relevant regional storytelling.
Drive the creation and delivery of global employer branding content for both Flipp and Shopfully, developing assets that strengthen our positioning as a global, integrated company across all markets.
Define and execute content plans across all owned channels — including LinkedIn (primary), Instagram, newsletters, and corporate websites — ensuring alignment between regional initiatives and global priorities.
Create and optimize high-impact content (LinkedIn and Instagram posts, videos, ebooks, case studies), working closely with content specialists, designers, and videomakers from concept to publishing and performance analysis.
Shape and amplify executive presence on LinkedIn, elevating leadership visibility, and drive employee advocacy programs, to extend the reach of our content.
Contribute to internal communication initiatives at a global level, managing internal newsletters and the company intranet to deliver clear, consistent messaging.
Own and manage corporate websites (WordPress CMS), overseeing content updates, publishing, and coordination with external agencies.


What you'll bring to the team:


3+ years of experience in B2B content creation and social media management, with a strong editorial mindset and a sharp eye for tone of voice and brand consistency.
Excellent copywriting skills, with the ability to craft clear, engaging, and strategic messaging for both external and internal audiences.
Ability to connect content strategy to business objectives and translate complex topics and business priorities into high-impact content.
Deep understanding of LinkedIn and Instagram, including formats, algorithms, and performance levers.
Professional-level fluency in spoken and written Spanish & English is required.


Our promise to each other:

What we bring:

What you bring:


An inclusive, collaborative environment where your voice matters 
A remote-first environment 
The tools and opportunities you need to grow and develop 
Every opportunity to own your success
Your authentic self, every day 
Your best work, from anywhere 
An appetite for learning, curiosity, and a growth mindset 
Motivation, initiative and the mind of an entrepreneur


Here's how to apply:

This position is currently vacant and open for applications. If you're interested in working with us on the future of shopping, fill out the fields below and submit your application. While experience and skill sets are valuable, growth potential and attitudes are equally important. If you're prepared to grow dramatically with your team at a world-class learning organization, consider applying. We understand that the most creative solutions require diversity in thought and life experiences.

Flipp is an equal opportunity employer. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, color, ancestry, religion, creed, sex, national or ethnic origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, family status, disability, or gender identity or expression or any other protected grounds. We are proud to be a welcoming space for employees, of every background, to bring their whole selves to work with confidence. Flipp is committed to providing appropriate accommodations to ensure our selection process is equitable, and such accommodations can be made available on request. If you require an accommodation, please contact your dedicated recruiter directly.

Hiring is a deeply human process; therefore, we use AI in limited, administrative ways to help streamline our hiring process. An AI-powered scheduling tool assists with coordinating interviews, and our applicant tracking system may use AI to identify relevant keywords within applications. We do not use AI to assess your application, make hiring decisions, or conduct interviews. All evaluations are completed by real people.

A member of our recruitment team will respond to you in the coming weeks.

title: SEO Specialist
skills: Drive organic growth across multiple client websites through on-page SEO, technical optimization, keyword strategy, and site structure improvements.
Conduct in-depth keyword research, competitor analysis, and SEO audits to identify growth opportunities and technical issues.
Analyze performance data using tools such as Google Analytics, Google Search Console, Ahrefs, SEMrush, Screaming Frog, and Google Business Profile to generate actionable insights.
Develop and implement internal linking strategies, resolve keyword cannibalization issues, and optimize content architecture.
Design and execute Local SEO strategies, including Google Maps optimization and visibility improvements across local and AI-driven search surfaces.
Collaborate with writers, designers, developers, and SEO peers to test new ideas, improve deliverables, and optimize campaign performance.
Work directly with clients to understand business goals, communicate SEO insights clearly, and translate requirements into effective strategies.
Continuously test new SEO approaches, evaluate algorithm updates, and contribute insights that improve overall team performance.
Stay up to date with industry trends, attend webinars or conferences, and proactively bring new learnings into client strategies.
3+ years of hands-on SEO experience with proven results in improving rankings and organic traffic.
At least 2 years of experience in a client-facing SEO role within an agency environment.
Strong proficiency with SEO and analytics tools including Google Analytics, Google Search Console, and Google Business Profile.
Solid working knowledge of tools such as Ahrefs, SEMrush, Screaming Frog, or similar platforms.
Proven ability to design and execute both short-term and long-term SEO strategies that balance quick wins and sustainable growth.
Strong analytical mindset with the ability to interpret data, identify patterns, and adapt strategies under algorithm changes.
Ability to manage multiple client accounts independently in a fast-paced, fully remote environment.
Excellent communication skills with the ability to simplify complex SEO concepts for clients and stakeholders.
Experience with WordPress or other CMS platforms and basic understanding of HTML/CSS is a plus.
Exposure to digital PR, link building, or off-site SEO strategies is a strong advantage.
Experience in automotive SEO, local SEO, or multi-location businesses is considered a plus.
Bonus: Experience building and growing your own website with measurable SEO results.
Competitive salary paid in USD on a consistent monthly basis.
Fully remote role with flexibility to work from anywhere in Latin America or Europe.
10 paid vacation days annually plus U.S. federal holidays.
High level of ownership over SEO strategy and execution with freedom to test and innovate.
Collaborative, high-performance agency culture focused on experimentation and continuous improvement.
Access to SEO conferences, webinars, and ongoing professional development opportunities.
Supportive environment with strong mentorship and clear growth pathways.
Opportunity to work on impactful, real-world SEO projects in a specialized automotive niche.
Culture that values curiosity, data-driven decisions, and measurable results.
description:
About the job

This position is listed on behalf of a partner company, who manages all applications and next steps. Our partner is looking for a SEO Specialist based in Mexico.

As an SEO Specialist, you will take full ownership of organic search performance for a portfolio of high-growth automotive clients in a fully remote, performance-driven environment. This role goes beyond execution — it is designed for someone who thinks strategically, experiments continuously, and uses data to drive real ranking improvements. You will work closely with cross-functional teams including content creators, designers, and developers to deliver measurable SEO impact across multiple websites. Your work will directly influence organic traffic growth, local search visibility, and long-term digital performance. The environment encourages curiosity, testing, and evidence-based decision-making, where ideas are valued based on results rather than assumptions. This is an opportunity to shape SEO strategy end-to-end while working in a collaborative, fast-moving agency setting focused on real outcomes.

Accountabilities


Drive organic growth across multiple client websites through on-page SEO, technical optimization, keyword strategy, and site structure improvements.
Conduct in-depth keyword research, competitor analysis, and SEO audits to identify growth opportunities and technical issues.
Analyze performance data using tools such as Google Analytics, Google Search Console, Ahrefs, SEMrush, Screaming Frog, and Google Business Profile to generate actionable insights.
Develop and implement internal linking strategies, resolve keyword cannibalization issues, and optimize content architecture.
Design and execute Local SEO strategies, including Google Maps optimization and visibility improvements across local and AI-driven search surfaces.
Collaborate with writers, designers, developers, and SEO peers to test new ideas, improve deliverables, and optimize campaign performance.
Work directly with clients to understand business goals, communicate SEO insights clearly, and translate requirements into effective strategies.
Continuously test new SEO approaches, evaluate algorithm updates, and contribute insights that improve overall team performance.
Stay up to date with industry trends, attend webinars or conferences, and proactively bring new learnings into client strategies.


Requirements


3+ years of hands-on SEO experience with proven results in improving rankings and organic traffic.
At least 2 years of experience in a client-facing SEO role within an agency environment.
Strong proficiency with SEO and analytics tools including Google Analytics, Google Search Console, and Google Business Profile.
Solid working knowledge of tools such as Ahrefs, SEMrush, Screaming Frog, or similar platforms.
Proven ability to design and execute both short-term and long-term SEO strategies that balance quick wins and sustainable growth.
Strong analytical mindset with the ability to interpret data, identify patterns, and adapt strategies under algorithm changes.
Ability to manage multiple client accounts independently in a fast-paced, fully remote environment.
Excellent communication skills with the ability to simplify complex SEO concepts for clients and stakeholders.
Experience with WordPress or other CMS platforms and basic understanding of HTML/CSS is a plus.
Exposure to digital PR, link building, or off-site SEO strategies is a strong advantage.
Experience in automotive SEO, local SEO, or multi-location businesses is considered a plus.
Bonus: Experience building and growing your own website with measurable SEO results.


Benefits


Competitive salary paid in USD on a consistent monthly basis.
Fully remote role with flexibility to work from anywhere in Latin America or Europe.
10 paid vacation days annually plus U.S. federal holidays.
High level of ownership over SEO strategy and execution with freedom to test and innovate.
Collaborative, high-performance agency culture focused on experimentation and continuous improvement.
Access to SEO conferences, webinars, and ongoing professional development opportunities.
Supportive environment with strong mentorship and clear growth pathways.
Opportunity to work on impactful, real-world SEO projects in a specialized automotive niche.
Culture that values curiosity, data-driven decisions, and measurable results.


How Jobgether Works

We use an AI-powered matching process to ensure your application is reviewed quickly, objectively, and fairly against the role's core requirements. Our system identifies the top-fitting candidates, and this shortlist is then shared directly with the hiring company. The final decision and next steps (interviews, assessments) are managed by their internal team.

We appreciate your interest and wish you the best!

 Why Apply Through Jobgether?

Data Privacy Notice: By submitting your application, you acknowledge that Jobgether will process your personal data to evaluate your candidacy and share relevant information with the hiring employer. This processing is based on legitimate interest and pre-contractual measures under applicable data protection laws (including GDPR). You may exercise your rights (access, rectification, erasure, objection) at any time.

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses and identifying potential inconsistencies or verification signals in application materials based on available information. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

title: Head of Retention Marketing
skills: Constant reinforcement of value: Work with Product Marketing on the messaging and own the program design that consistently reminds customers why they chose Superside.
Renewal behavior: Build the touchpoints and content that drive account usage and create the psychological and commercial conditions for renewal.
Churn mitigation: Work with data to identify customers at risk and activate account teams and automated programs to re-engage them before the renewal conversation.

Customer Advocacy Board: Partner with ABM to operationalize a Superside CAB. And PMM and TPD to drive customer reviews and help identify opportunities for customers to participate in marketing activities.
Subscription utilization programs: Build the systems that move customers from passive subscribers to active users — driving toward full utilization of their plan each month.
New feature education: Every major product release that Product Marketing launches— needs a customer-facing campaign that explains the what, why, and how.
Usage-triggered journeys: Partner with the lifecycle team to design automated journeys triggered by utilization signals.

AM & GAD toolkits: Build the playbooks and asset libraries that give Account Managers and Group Account Directors what they need to proactively manage customer health - email templates, outreach guides, objection handling - without requiring a custom campaign for every account.
Expansion enablement: Create the materials that help AMs and GADs articulate and sell new Superside offerings, cross-sell services, and grow accounts.

Subscription or retention marketing ownership: You have owned renewal, churn reduction, or subscription growth as a primary mandate - not as a byproduct of a broader marketing role. This was your job.

Utilization or product usage as a core KPI: In at least one role, you were accountable for driving customers to actively use a product or service. You built programs around that metric. You know what moves it and what does not.

Programmatic lifecycle infrastructure: You have built or substantially redesigned a customer lifecycle program — segmentation logic, trigger design, journey mapping, A/B frameworks — not just executed campaigns someone else designed.

Account team or CSM enablement: You have built toolkits, playbooks, and resources for customer-facing commercial teams - and you know the difference between an asset they will actually use and one that sits in a folder.

Data-driven cohort thinking: You do not wait for a report to tell you something is wrong. You know how to segment an account base by health, identify risk patterns, and design interventions for specific cohorts.
A builder, not a maintainer: This function does not have the infrastructure yet. You are energized by that, not intimidated. You have built from near-zero before.
Commercially minded: You think in terms of revenue impact. You know that renewal rate and utilization are lagging indicators of the work you do upstream, and you build toward those numbers.
A relationship investor: Cross functional leaders who work with customers daily are your distribution channel and your intelligence network.
Operationally rigorous: You produce documentation, playbooks, and toolkits that outlast you. You build systems that account teams can run, not campaigns that require you to run them.
A senior IC ready to become a people leader: You will not manage a team on day one. In 12–24 months you will build one. You already think and operate at that level.
description:
About the job

Superside is hiring a Head of Retention Marketing reporting to the Sr. Director of ABM and Field Marketing. The mandate is straightforward: keep customers, grow accounts, and make sure every customer understands the value they're getting from Superside before they're ever asked to renew. That means owning the retention program design that reinforces value at every stage of the customer journey — from onboarding through adoption through renewal. It means building utilization programs and equipping account teams with the playbooks, toolkits, and intelligence they need to proactively manage account health without waiting for a crisis. And it means working with data to spot churn risk early and activate the right interventions before the conversation gets hard.




What you’ll own




Retention Programs

Constant reinforcement of value: Work with Product Marketing on the messaging and own the program design that consistently reminds customers why they chose Superside.
Renewal behavior: Build the touchpoints and content that drive account usage and create the psychological and commercial conditions for renewal.
Churn mitigation: Work with data to identify customers at risk and activate account teams and automated programs to re-engage them before the renewal conversation.
Customer Advocacy Board: Partner with ABM to operationalize a Superside CAB. And PMM and TPD to drive customer reviews and help identify opportunities for customers to participate in marketing activities.




Adoption & Utilization

Subscription utilization programs: Build the systems that move customers from passive subscribers to active users — driving toward full utilization of their plan each month.
New feature education: Every major product release that Product Marketing launches— needs a customer-facing campaign that explains the what, why, and how.
Usage-triggered journeys: Partner with the lifecycle team to design automated journeys triggered by utilization signals.




Account Team Enablement

AM & GAD toolkits: Build the playbooks and asset libraries that give Account Managers and Group Account Directors what they need to proactively manage customer health - email templates, outreach guides, objection handling - without requiring a custom campaign for every account.
Expansion enablement: Create the materials that help AMs and GADs articulate and sell new Superside offerings, cross-sell services, and grow accounts.







Must-Have Experience




Subscription or retention marketing ownership: You have owned renewal, churn reduction, or subscription growth as a primary mandate - not as a byproduct of a broader marketing role. This was your job.
Utilization or product usage as a core KPI: In at least one role, you were accountable for driving customers to actively use a product or service. You built programs around that metric. You know what moves it and what does not.
Programmatic lifecycle infrastructure: You have built or substantially redesigned a customer lifecycle program — segmentation logic, trigger design, journey mapping, A/B frameworks — not just executed campaigns someone else designed.
Account team or CSM enablement: You have built toolkits, playbooks, and resources for customer-facing commercial teams - and you know the difference between an asset they will actually use and one that sits in a folder.
Data-driven cohort thinking: You do not wait for a report to tell you something is wrong. You know how to segment an account base by health, identify risk patterns, and design interventions for specific cohorts.







Who you are




A builder, not a maintainer: This function does not have the infrastructure yet. You are energized by that, not intimidated. You have built from near-zero before.
Commercially minded: You think in terms of revenue impact. You know that renewal rate and utilization are lagging indicators of the work you do upstream, and you build toward those numbers.
A relationship investor: Cross functional leaders who work with customers daily are your distribution channel and your intelligence network.
Operationally rigorous: You produce documentation, playbooks, and toolkits that outlast you. You build systems that account teams can run, not campaigns that require you to run them.
A senior IC ready to become a people leader: You will not manage a team on day one. In 12–24 months you will build one. You already think and operate at that level.

title: Electrical Product Marketing Specialist for Medium Voltage
location: San Luis Potosí, San Luis Potosí, Mexico (On-site)
skills: Collaborate with management to define strategic marketing plans and support relevant implementation, including communication and support to the business units to translate it into a relevant marketing strategy. Collects data and provides sound market and competitor intelligence analyses related to global markets and trends. Tracks and provides global market research and analysis results, identifying existing gaps or new growth opportunities.

Monitor market trends and accordingly recommends improvements in the marketing strategy and plans to meet changing market and competitive landscapes. Provides appropriate market information to support long- to mid-term decision-making on the marketing strategy.
Provide support in defining the end-user value proposition and communicates it to the target segment, both internal and external.
Drive sales efficiency by ensuring every salesperson possesses appropriate product and tool knowledge, including both technical and sales aspects.
Visit customers to understand their needs and ensures the resolution of customer issues.
Support proposals team, by suggesting and providing best available product(s)/solution(s) while appropriately addressing technical and commercial considerations for each individual opportunity.

Bachelor’s degree in Electrical, Electromechanical, or Mechatronics Engineering, or a related field. Candidates must provide official documentation certifying completion of their degree (e.g., degree certificate, professional license, or official graduation letter).

5+ years of experience in Marketing, Sales, Pricing, Business Development or Engineering, working as a Business Development Manager, Sales Engineer, Proposals Specialist, or similar, with Electrification products.
Demonstrated experience working with Enterprise Resource Planning and Standard Operating Procedures.
Sound knowledge of quotations and proposals, as well as understanding of exports and the international market.
Advanced English Level is a must.
Travel up to 70%. Candidates must already have a work authorization that would permit them to work for ABB in the US.
description:
About the job

En ABB, ayudamos a las industrias a funcionar de forma más eficiente y limpia, y cada una de las personas que trabajan aquí lo hace posible. Estarás capacitado para liderar, apoyado para crecer y orgulloso del impacto que creamos juntos. Únete a nosotros y ayuda a que funcione lo que hace funcionar el mundo.

Este Puesto Reporta a

Product Market Manager

__

Your Role And Responsibilities

In this role, you will coordinate sales activities, events, proposals, and market research to meet business goals and increase market share. This role will be focused on International Markets (Canada, CA&C, Europe, Middle East, and Asia Pacific regions). Working within the Grid Components group of Electrification Distribution Solutions in Mexico and providing support to Pinetops, North Carolina, you will be responsible for implementing product marketing strategies aligned with the global product strategy.

The work model for the role is: - Onsite

This role is contributing to Electrification - Distribution Solutions Division in Mexico and providing support to Pinetops, North Carolina.

You Will Be Mainly Accountable To


Collaborate with management to define strategic marketing plans and support relevant implementation, including communication and support to the business units to translate it into a relevant marketing strategy. Collects data and provides sound market and competitor intelligence analyses related to global markets and trends. Tracks and provides global market research and analysis results, identifying existing gaps or new growth opportunities.
Monitor market trends and accordingly recommends improvements in the marketing strategy and plans to meet changing market and competitive landscapes. Provides appropriate market information to support long- to mid-term decision-making on the marketing strategy.
Provide support in defining the end-user value proposition and communicates it to the target segment, both internal and external.
Drive sales efficiency by ensuring every salesperson possesses appropriate product and tool knowledge, including both technical and sales aspects.
Visit customers to understand their needs and ensures the resolution of customer issues.
Support proposals team, by suggesting and providing best available product(s)/solution(s) while appropriately addressing technical and commercial considerations for each individual opportunity.


Our team dynamics

You will join a dynamic, talented, and high performing team, where you will be able to thrive.

Qualifications For The Role


Bachelor’s degree in Electrical, Electromechanical, or Mechatronics Engineering, or a related field. Candidates must provide official documentation certifying completion of their degree (e.g., degree certificate, professional license, or official graduation letter). 
5+ years of experience in Marketing, Sales, Pricing, Business Development or Engineering, working as a Business Development Manager, Sales Engineer, Proposals Specialist, or similar, with Electrification products.
Demonstrated experience working with Enterprise Resource Planning and Standard Operating Procedures.
Sound knowledge of quotations and proposals, as well as understanding of exports and the international market.
Advanced English Level is a must.
Travel up to 70%. Candidates must already have a work authorization that would permit them to work for ABB in the US.


More About Us

ABB's Distribution Solutions Division facilitates the efficient and reliable distribution, protection and control of power by improving electric power quality while strengthening the resilience of the grid.

Construir un futuro más limpio e inteligente requiere todo tipo de mentes: las curiosas, las valientes y las creativas. Por eso acogemos a personas de todos los orígenes y experiencias.

¿Listo para causar impacto?

Presenta tu candidatura hoy mismo o visita https://www.abb.com para saber más sobre el impacto de nuestras soluciones en todo el mundo.

Modelo de Trabalho Híbrido: Adotamos um ambiente de trabalho híbrido e flexível (2-3 dias por semana no escritório, dependendo do papel) para funções baseadas em escritório, garantindo uma experiência integrada e coerente tanto no âmbito digital quanto presencial.

Flexibilidade e Equilíbrio entre Trabalho e Vida Pessoal: ""Flex My Way"" consiste em um conjunto de políticas de projetadas para facilitar a gestão das responsabilidades pessoais e profissionais, seja cuidando da família, participando de atividades comunitárias ou dedicando tempo para descanso e renovação pessoal. Essas políticas se baseiam em nossa abordagem flexível de trabalho, permitindo que os funcionários trabalhem de qualquer lugar por até 8 semanas por ano, promovendo assim um equilíbrio ideal entre seus compromissos de trabalho e pessoais.

Desenvolvimento e Crescimento Profissional: Promovemos uma cultura de aprendizado contínuo e desenvolvimento de habilidades, preparando nossos talentos para enfrentar os desafios do futuro e oferecer soluções reais. Nosso programa ""Grow My Way"" e a abordagem focada em habilidades garantem que você tenha as ferramentas e conhecimentos para crescer, liderar e prosperar em um futuro habilitado pela inteligência artificial.

Benefícios Competitivos do Setor: Oferecemos planos de benefícios abrangentes que incluem assistência médica e odontológica, dois dias de feriado corporativo para saúde mental, programas de incentivo por performance e recursos para o bem-estar mental, físico e financeiro.

Cultura: Reconhecida globalmente por inclusão e pertencimento, flexibilidade, equilíbrio entre trabalho e vida pessoal. Vivemos nossos valores: obsessão pelos nossos clientes, competir para ganhar, desafiar o (seu) nosso pensamento, agir rápido e aprender rápido e juntos somos mais fortes.

Impacto Social: Gere um impacto em sua comunidade com nosso Instituto de Impacto Social. Oferecemos a todos colaboradores dois dias de voluntariado pagos anualmente e oportunidades para se envolver em projetos de consultoria pro bono e iniciativas de Meio Ambiente, Sociais e de Governança (ESG).

Fazendo um Impacto Real no Mundo: Somos uma das poucas empresas globalmente que auxilia seus clientes na busca por justiça, verdade e transparência. Trabalhando em conjunto com os profissionais e instituições que servimos, contribuímos para sustentar o estado de direito, fomentar o comércio, detectar infratores, relatar os fatos e oferecer informações confiáveis e imparciais para pessoas em todo o mundo.
