Fazendo um Impacto Real no Mundo: Somos uma das poucas empresas globalmente que auxilia seus clientes na busca por justiça, verdade e transparência. Trabalhando em conjunto com os profissionais e instituições que servimos, contribuímos para sustentar o estado de direito, fomentar o comércio, detectar infratores, relatar os fatos e oferecer informações confiáveis e imparciais para pessoas em todo o mundo.


Quem somos 

A Thomson Reuters informa o caminho adiante ao reunir conteúdo confiável e a tecnologia que pessoas e organizações precisam para tomar decisões corretas. Servimos profissionais nos setores jurídico, fiscal, contábil, de conformidade, governamental e de mídia. Nossos produtos combinam software altamente especializado e informações para capacitar profissionais com os dados, inteligência e soluções necessárias para tomar decisões informadas e ajudar as instituições em sua busca por justiça, verdade e transparência. A agência de notícias Reuters, parte da Thomson Reuters, é líder mundial na área de jornalismo e notícias confiáveis.

Contamos com o talento de 26.000 colaboradores em mais de 70 países, onde todos têm a oportunidade de contribuir e crescer profissionalmente em ambientes de trabalho flexíveis. Em um momento em que objetividade, precisão, equidade e transparência estão sob ataque, estamos comprometidos em buscá-los. Ficou interessado? Faça parte da nossa equipe e contribua para moldar as indústrias que impulsionam o progresso da sociedade.

Como uma empresa global, confiamos no conhecimento único, perspectivas e experiências de todos os colaboradores para cumprir nossos objetivos comerciais. Para garantir que possamos fazer isso, buscamos colaboradores talentosos e qualificados em todas as nossas operações ao redor do mundo, independentemente de raça, cor, sexo/gênero, incluindo gestantes, identidade e expressão de gênero, origem nacional, religião, orientação afetivo-sexual, deficiência, idade, estado civil, status de cidadania, ou outra classificação protegida pela lei aplicável.

Além disso, oferecemos acessibilidade para pessoas com deficiências e para aquelas com crenças religiosas sinceras, conforme a legislação vigente. Você pode encontrar mais informações sobre como solicitar uma adaptação aqui.

title: Senior Marketing Tech and CRM Administrator
skills: Serve as the primary SFMC Admin across all business units, managing user accounts, role-based permissions, and security settings.
Oversee multi-Business Unit configurations and maintain overall platform health.
Design, build, and optimize complex Journey Builder flows, Automation Studio workflows, and triggered sends.
Own the troubleshooting of data integrations between SFMC, Salesforce CRM (via Marketing Cloud Connect), and upstream data systems using REST/SOAP APIs.
Oversee the SFMC data model, including Data Extensions, SQL query activities, and Contact Builder relationships.
Ensure strict data hygiene, suppression governance, and accurate targeting for all global sends.
Lead Content Builder strategy, providing advanced support for AMPscript-driven personalization and dynamic email design systems.
Act as the principal technical point of contact for our global user community, mentoring junior administrators and providing platform best practices.
Partner with leadership to evaluate emerging capabilities such as Marketing Cloud Next, Personalization Builder, and Einstein AI.
Develop and execute Proof of Concepts (POCs) to inform build-vs-buy decisions and platform roadmaps.
Experience: 10–15 years in Marketing Automation/Operations, with at least 5 years specifically administering SFMC at an enterprise scale.
Product Owner Mindset: You don't just react to tickets; you proactively drive platform sustainability and operational efficiency.
Communication: Professional-level English proficiency is required for global collaboration and technical documentation.
description:
About the job

Company Description

At Nielsen, we are passionate about our work to power a better media future for all people by providing powerful insights that drive client decisions and deliver extraordinary results. Our talented, global workforce is dedicated to capturing audience engagement with content - wherever and whenever it’s consumed. Together, we are proudly rooted in our deep legacy as we stand at the forefront of the media revolution. When you join Nielsen, you will join a dynamic team committed to excellence, perseverance, and the ambition to make an impact together. We champion you, because when you succeed, we do too. We enable your best to power our future.

Job Description

We are seeking a Senior Marketing Automation Administrator with deep Salesforce Marketing Cloud (SFMC) expertise to lead the day-to-day management, configuration, and strategic optimization of our marketing automation ecosystem.

As our SFMC technical authority, you will partner with marketing leadership to architect scalable journeys, harden platform governance, and drive continuous improvement across our MarTech stack. You will ensure platform stability, own complex data integrations, and mentor a global team of administrators to execute high-impact campaigns.

What You’ll Do

System Administration & Governance


Serve as the primary SFMC Admin across all business units, managing user accounts, role-based permissions, and security settings.
Oversee multi-Business Unit configurations and maintain overall platform health.


Architecting Workflows & Integrations


Design, build, and optimize complex Journey Builder flows, Automation Studio workflows, and triggered sends.
Own the troubleshooting of data integrations between SFMC, Salesforce CRM (via Marketing Cloud Connect), and upstream data systems using REST/SOAP APIs.


Data & Audience Management


Oversee the SFMC data model, including Data Extensions, SQL query activities, and Contact Builder relationships.
Ensure strict data hygiene, suppression governance, and accurate targeting for all global sends.


Advanced Support & Enablement


Lead Content Builder strategy, providing advanced support for AMPscript-driven personalization and dynamic email design systems.
Act as the principal technical point of contact for our global user community, mentoring junior administrators and providing platform best practices.


Innovation & Strategy


Partner with leadership to evaluate emerging capabilities such as Marketing Cloud Next, Personalization Builder, and Einstein AI.
Develop and execute Proof of Concepts (POCs) to inform build-vs-buy decisions and platform roadmaps.


Qualifications

Professional Background


Experience: 10–15 years in Marketing Automation/Operations, with at least 5 years specifically administering SFMC at an enterprise scale.
Product Owner Mindset: You don't just react to tickets; you proactively drive platform sustainability and operational efficiency.
Communication: Professional-level English proficiency is required for global collaboration and technical documentation.


Additional Information

Please be aware that job-seekers may be at risk of targeting by scammers seeking personal data or money. Nielsen recruiters will only contact you through official job boards, LinkedIn, or email with a nielsen.com domain. Be cautious of any outreach claiming to be from Nielsen via other messaging platforms or personal email addresses. Always verify that email communications come from an @nielsen.com address. If you're unsure about the authenticity of a job offer or communication, please contact Nielsen directly through our official website or verified social media channels.

title: Electrical Product Marketing Specialist for Medium Voltage
skills: Collaborate with management to define strategic marketing plans and support relevant implementation, including communication and support to the business units to translate it into a relevant marketing strategy. Collects data and provides sound market and competitor intelligence analyses related to global markets and trends. Tracks and provides global market research and analysis results, identifying existing gaps or new growth opportunities.

Monitor market trends and accordingly recommends improvements in the marketing strategy and plans to meet changing market and competitive landscapes. Provides appropriate market information to support long- to mid-term decision-making on the marketing strategy.
Provide support in defining the end-user value proposition and communicates it to the target segment, both internal and external.
Drive sales efficiency by ensuring every salesperson possesses appropriate product and tool knowledge, including both technical and sales aspects.
Visit customers to understand their needs and ensures the resolution of customer issues.
Support proposals team, by suggesting and providing best available product(s)/solution(s) while appropriately addressing technical and commercial considerations for each individual opportunity.

Bachelor’s degree in Electrical, Electromechanical, or Mechatronics Engineering, or a related field. Candidates must provide official documentation certifying completion of their degree (e.g., degree certificate, professional license, or official graduation letter).

5+ years of experience in Marketing, Sales, Pricing, Business Development or Engineering, working as a Business Development Manager, Sales Engineer, Proposals Specialist, or similar, with Electrification products.
Demonstrated experience working with Enterprise Resource Planning and Standard Operating Procedures.
Sound knowledge of quotations and proposals, as well as understanding of exports and the international market.
Advanced English Level is a must.
Travel up to 70%. Candidates must already have a work authorization that would permit them to work for ABB in the US.
description:
About the job

At ABB, we help industries run leaner and cleaner—and every person here makes that happen. You’ll be empowered to lead, supported to grow, and proud of the impact we create together. Join us and help run what runs the world.

This position reports to:

Product Market Manager

__


Your role and responsibilities:

In this role, you will coordinate sales activities, events, proposals, and market research to meet business goals and increase market share. This role will be focused on International Markets (Canada, CA&C, Europe, Middle East, and Asia Pacific regions). Working within the Grid Components group of Electrification Distribution Solutions in Mexico and providing support to Pinetops, North Carolina, you will be responsible for implementing product marketing strategies aligned with the global product strategy.

The work model for the role is: - Onsite

This role is contributing to Electrification - Distribution Solutions Division in Mexico and providing support to Pinetops, North Carolina.

You will be mainly accountable to:


Collaborate with management to define strategic marketing plans and support relevant implementation, including communication and support to the business units to translate it into a relevant marketing strategy. Collects data and provides sound market and competitor intelligence analyses related to global markets and trends. Tracks and provides global market research and analysis results, identifying existing gaps or new growth opportunities.
Monitor market trends and accordingly recommends improvements in the marketing strategy and plans to meet changing market and competitive landscapes. Provides appropriate market information to support long- to mid-term decision-making on the marketing strategy.
Provide support in defining the end-user value proposition and communicates it to the target segment, both internal and external.
Drive sales efficiency by ensuring every salesperson possesses appropriate product and tool knowledge, including both technical and sales aspects.
Visit customers to understand their needs and ensures the resolution of customer issues.
Support proposals team, by suggesting and providing best available product(s)/solution(s) while appropriately addressing technical and commercial considerations for each individual opportunity.


Our team dynamics

You will join a dynamic, talented, and high performing team, where you will be able to thrive.


Qualifications for the role:


Bachelor’s degree in Electrical, Electromechanical, or Mechatronics Engineering, or a related field. Candidates must provide official documentation certifying completion of their degree (e.g., degree certificate, professional license, or official graduation letter). 
5+ years of experience in Marketing, Sales, Pricing, Business Development or Engineering, working as a Business Development Manager, Sales Engineer, Proposals Specialist, or similar, with Electrification products.
Demonstrated experience working with Enterprise Resource Planning and Standard Operating Procedures.
Sound knowledge of quotations and proposals, as well as understanding of exports and the international market.
Advanced English Level is a must.
Travel up to 70%. Candidates must already have a work authorization that would permit them to work for ABB in the US.



More about us:

ABB's Distribution Solutions Division facilitates the efficient and reliable distribution, protection and control of power by improving electric power quality while strengthening the resilience of the grid.

Building a cleaner, smarter future takes all kinds of minds: the curious, the courageous, and the creative. That's why we welcome people from all backgrounds and experiences.

Ready to make an impact?

Apply today or visit https://www.abb.com to learn more about the impact of our solutions across the globe.

skills: REPORTS TO: VP LATAM and VP MKT (US-based)

description:
About the job

ON.energy is building the power infrastructure that makes the AI era possible. As AI demand surges past what the grid and traditional data centers can support, ON.energy provides a new class of power technology proven at gigawatt scale and trusted by the world’s leading cloud and AI companies. Our systems are already deployed across 2.5 GW of hyper-scale campuses, validated by top U.S. national labs, and certified for grid-safe operation by major utilities. With real products in the field, we’re scaling faster than the grid can, transforming power from a bottleneck into a competitive advantage for the companies building the future.

We are looking for a Marketing manager to design, execute, and lead the marketing strategy for ON.energy in the LATAM region, positioning the brand and its UPS solutions portfolio for Artificial Intelligence applications in markets with high demand for mission-critical infrastructure. Generate awareness, credibility, and qualified demand in a market where brand recognition is still emerging, reporting to the VP for LATAM and acting as the direct liaison between the region and the Corporate Marketing team in the U.S., ensuring global brand consistency with local relevance.


REPORTS TO: VP LATAM and VP MKT (US-based)
LOCATION: Mexico City, Mexico
MODALITY: In Person (travel availability required)
FUNCTIONAL LINK: Corporate Marketing (U.S.) – brand alignment, global guidelines, approval of regional materials and campaigns


Requirements


Bachelor's degree in Marketing, Communications, Business Administration, or related field. Master's degree or specialization in B2B Marketing or Commercial Management desirable.
Minimum 3 years in B2B marketing in technology, energy, infrastructure, or related sectors. Demonstrable experience in brand positioning in LATAM markets. Experience working in matrix structures or reporting to U.S.-based leadership is a plus.
Native Spanish. Advanced English (C1 or higher) – daily communication with corporate team in the U.S. Portuguese desirable.
B2B marketing and complex sales cycles (technical / capital-intensive solutions).
Content strategy and brand positioning on LinkedIn and B2B digital channels.
Lead generation and nurturing (HubSpot, Salesforce, or equivalent).
Event management, trade shows, and webinars in the LATAM region.
Marketing budget management and ROI metrics.
Tools: Adobe Suite / Canva Pro, Google Analytics, CRM and marketing automation platforms.


Job Responsibilities & Authority

Brand Strategy & Positioning


Develop and execute the annual marketing plan for LATAM, aligned with the VP's commercial objectives and U.S. corporate guidelines.
Build ON.energy's positioning as a reference in UPS solutions for AI infrastructure in the region.
Adapt corporate messages and materials to the cultural and regulatory context of each LATAM market.


Demand Generation & Lead Management


Design and run digital campaigns (LinkedIn Ads, email marketing, SEO/SEM) targeting decision-makers in datacenter, infrastructure, and IT.
Manage ON.energy's presence at trade shows, conferences, and industry events (Data Center Dynamics, Datacenter Forum LATAM, etc.).
Coordinate with the sales team on lead generation, qualification, and follow-up through CRM.


Content & Communications


Produce and manage technical-commercial content: case studies, whitepapers, product sheets, videos, and webinars on UPS for AI.
Manage ON.energy's digital channels in LATAM: LinkedIn, regional website, and sales materials.
Coordinate with local agencies in design, public relations, and specialized media.


Internal Coordination & Reporting


Report weekly to the VP for LATAM on campaign progress, performance metrics (KPIs), and ROI of activities.
Act as the formal liaison between LATAM and Corporate Marketing (U.S.): align campaign calendars, request and adapt global assets, submit regional materials for approval, and ensure compliance with corporate brand guidelines.
Participate in periodic global marketing team meetings, representing the needs and particularities of the LATAM market.
Manage the LATAM marketing budget efficiently and transparently.
Identify strategic partnerships with industry associations, integrators, and distribution channels in the region.

Competitive salary + annual performance-based bonus eligibility

For All Roles

title: Talent Account Executive 1
description:
About the job

LinkedIn is the world's largest professional network, built to create economic opportunity for every member of the global workforce. Our products help people make powerful connections, discover exciting opportunities, build necessary skills, and gain valuable insights every day. We're also committed to providing transformational opportunities for our own employees by investing in their growth. We aspire to create a culture that's built on trust, care, inclusion, and fun – where everyone can succeed.



Join us to transform the way the world works.





Job Description



This role will be based in Mexico City.



At LinkedIn, our approach to flexible work is centered on trust and optimized for culture, connection, clarity, and the evolving needs of our business. The work location of this role is hybrid, meaning it will be performed both from home and from a LinkedIn office on select days, as determined by the business needs of the team. 



LinkedIn is looking for a Talent Solutions Account Executive I (TAE1) to join our New Business team in Mexico City. This role is focused on driving new customer acquisition across a high-volume SMB territory, building strong pipeline through outbound prospecting, and closing new business opportunities across Hiring and Learning solutions. 



Success in this role requires strong discipline in pipeline management, excellent time management, and the ability to prioritize effectively across a large number of accounts and opportunities. The ideal candidate is highly organized, action-oriented, collaborative, leverages AI and data to increase productivity, and thrives in a fast-paced, high-activity sales environment. While the territory is primarily SMB-focused, the ability to identify and close larger strategic opportunities is considered a strong differentiator. 



Responsibilities:



• Own new business acquisition within a defined SMB territory, ensuring strong account coverage and prioritization of the highest-potential opportunities 

• Develop and execute a territory strategy focused on scale, efficiency, and consistent pipeline generation 

• Generate pipeline through proactive outbound prospecting (self-prospection and in partnership with SDs + partners) and effectively manage inbound opportunities 

• Build and maintain a strong, healthy pipeline across all funnel stages, ensuring high activity, progression, and conversion 

• Manage a high volume of opportunities simultaneously, with strong organization, time management, and prioritization discipline 

• Run effective discovery conversations to understand customer needs and position Hiring and Learning solutions accordingly 

• Execute pilot-led sales motions, including onboarding, engagement, and conversion into long-term partnerships 

• Maintain accurate forecasting and high standards of CRM hygiene 

• Consistently meet or exceed quota targets through strong execution and disciplined funnel management 

• Identify and drive cross-sell opportunities, especially expanding into Learning solutions 

• Identify larger strategic opportunities within the territory and navigate them effectively when present 

• Leverage AI tools and data insights to improve productivity, prospecting quality, and deal execution 

• Collaborate closely with cross-functional teams (SDs, ADs, Marketing, Partners) to maximize opportunity creation and success 

• Share market insights and contribute to the development of team best practices





Qualifications



Basic Qualifications:



• Fluency in Spanish and English 

• 3+ years of sales experience 



Preferred Qualifications:



• Proven track record of closing new business in B2B/SaaS environments 

• Experience with HR tech, recruiting solutions, or software sales 

• Strong prospecting, organization, and pipeline management skills 

• Experience managing high-volume sales cycles with efficiency and consistency 

• Data-driven mindset with ability to prioritize and make informed decisions 



Suggested Skills 



• Pipeline Management 

• Territory Planning & Prioritization 

• Time & Volume Management 

• Prospecting Excellence 

• Pilot Management 

• AI Usage for Sales Productivity 

• Consultative / Value-Based Selling 

• Forecasting 

• Cross-functional Collaboration 

• Opportunity Qualification 





Additional Information



Global Data Privacy Notice and Compliance Posters for Job Candidates 



Please use this link to access documents that provide information about how LinkedIn handles the personal data of employees and job applicants, as well as the E-Verify Participation Notice and the Department of Justice Immigrant and Employee Rights Section Right to Work posters: https://www.linkedin.com/legal/candidate-portal.

title: Financial Analyst, Lyft Ads
skills: Financial Close: Own month end processes and actualization of the Ads P&L, including revenue, expenses, and headcount, and ensure all data is accurate and organized efficiently.

Ads Reporting: Provide reporting for campaign margins, account growth, and sales rep efficiency routinely. Build relationships with cross functional teams to deliver well-rounded, thoughtful insights.

description:
About the job

At Lyft, our purpose is to serve and connect. We aim to achieve this by cultivating a work environment where all team members belong and have the opportunity to thrive.

Lyft Ads is one of the fastest-growing businesses within Lyft, building a first-of-its-kind mobility advertising platform. As the Financial Analyst, you'll be a core finance partner to a high-velocity, scaling business by translating complex commercial activity into clean financials, sharp insights, and executive-ready narratives. This role sits at the intersection of accounting rigor and strategic finance.

Responsibilities:


Financial Close: Own month end processes and actualization of the Ads P&L, including revenue, expenses, and headcount, and ensure all data is accurate and organized efficiently.
FP&A Reporting: Drive variance analyses and forecasting for Finance and Ads leadership on a weekly basis. Own all budget, actuals, and forecast variance analyses and provide forward-looking insights on revenue/expense trends.
Budget Management: Approve and own spend across the rapidly growing business. Collaborate with procurement and business operations to understand vendor usage and ROI to the business. 
Ads Reporting: Provide reporting for campaign margins, account growth, and sales rep efficiency routinely. Build relationships with cross functional teams to deliver well-rounded, thoughtful insights.
Executive Presentation: Translate dense data into clean, executive-ready Google Slides for VP- and C-suite reviews.


Experience:


Bachelor's degree in Accounting, Finance, or a related quantitative field.
Strong accounting background preferred. FP&A and/or advertising experience is helpful but not required.
Advanced Excel/Google Sheets knowledge: you are able to comfortably dig through spreadsheets, even ones you have not built.
Curiosity and comfortability with ambiguity: you possess the ability to research and present your findings with little historicals and guidance.
Attention to detail: you are extremely thorough with your reviews and can understand the bigger picture from the data.
Professional fluency in English is required.


Please submit your resume in English.

title: Senior Commercial Market Specialist (m/f/d)
skills: Monitor and analyze regional performance, competitor dynamics, and demand patterns for the North America market, turning data into strategic recommendations.
Own fare management for the region: define regional pricing settings, fallback logic, and verify implementation across the network.
Support the design and execution of commercial activities — promotions, marketing campaigns, A/B tests, and sales initiatives — aligned with regional strategy.
Build and maintain a country-specific campaign calendar (email, social media, and other channels).
Develop and manage briefings for Marketing & Sales channels, including budget tracking and bi-weekly syncs.
Identify revenue and customer flow opportunities: social discounts, special products, customer segments, and willingness-to-pay insights.
Stay ahead of market trends: track the competitive landscape, macroeconomic shifts, regulatory changes, and network dynamics across the region.
Contribute to topline forecasts (weekly and monthly updates) and propose data-driven improvements.
Collect North America-specific e-commerce needs, build business cases, and present recommendations to leadership.
Lead or contribute to cross-functional projects, acting as subject matter expert or project manager on specific commercial topics.
Leverage AI tools and automation to streamline processes and improve commercial efficiency.
Bachelor's degree in Economics, Finance, Mathematics, Business, or a related field.
2–4 years of experience in commercial analysis, revenue management, or business development — ideally in transportation, airlines, hospitality, beverages, or a similarly dynamic industry.
Strong analytical skills with the ability to go beyond the numbers: you design solutions, not just reports.
A natural connector who actively seeks input from stakeholders, builds relationships across teams, and is not afraid to knock on doors to get things done.
Strategic thinker who can navigate fast-changing macro environments (cost shifts, demand fluctuations, competitive moves) with adaptability and clarity.
Proactive and ownership-driven: you set your own goals, follow through on commitments, and hold yourself accountable to financial targets and project deliverables.
Curious about markets — you want to understand North America's geography, network, and customer dynamics, not just analyze spreadsheets.
Collaborative and a strong team player, with excellent communication and presentation skills for senior stakeholders.
Comfortable with ambiguity and freedom; you do not need constant guidance to deliver results.
Fluent in English (additional languages are a plus).
Interest or experience in AI-powered tools and process automation is a strong advantage.
Travel perks: 12 free Flix vouchers + 12 discount vouchers for friends & family.
Work from (M)Anywhere: Depending on your role, work from another location for up to 60 days per year.
Hybrid work model: We are an office-first company, but we offer flexibility to balance work and life.
Wellbeing support: Access confidential 1:1 counselling, courses, and stress management for yourself and up to four family members.
Learning & Development: Take advantage of language classes, training courses, and expert-led sessions to grow your skills.
Mentoring Program: Connect with experienced colleagues to gain insights and accelerate your career.
description:
About the job

At Flix, we offer a dynamic work environment with competitive pay, strong growth opportunities, and a tech-driven approach to making travel more accessible, sustainable, and affordable

We are looking for a Senior Commercial Market Specialist (m/f/d) to join our North America commercial team. This is not a purely analytical role — we need someone who combines strong analytical thinking with the ability to design and implement solutions, build bridges across stakeholders, and take ownership of results. You will interact directly with senior leadership, including the CEO for North America, and shape the commercial strategy of one of the world's fastest-growing mobility networks.

About The Role


Monitor and analyze regional performance, competitor dynamics, and demand patterns for the North America market, turning data into strategic recommendations.
Own fare management for the region: define regional pricing settings, fallback logic, and verify implementation across the network.
Support the design and execution of commercial activities — promotions, marketing campaigns, A/B tests, and sales initiatives — aligned with regional strategy.
Build and maintain a country-specific campaign calendar (email, social media, and other channels).
Develop and manage briefings for Marketing & Sales channels, including budget tracking and bi-weekly syncs.
Identify revenue and customer flow opportunities: social discounts, special products, customer segments, and willingness-to-pay insights.
Stay ahead of market trends: track the competitive landscape, macroeconomic shifts, regulatory changes, and network dynamics across the region.
Contribute to topline forecasts (weekly and monthly updates) and propose data-driven improvements.
Collect North America-specific e-commerce needs, build business cases, and present recommendations to leadership.
Lead or contribute to cross-functional projects, acting as subject matter expert or project manager on specific commercial topics.
Leverage AI tools and automation to streamline processes and improve commercial efficiency.


About You


Bachelor's degree in Economics, Finance, Mathematics, Business, or a related field.
2–4 years of experience in commercial analysis, revenue management, or business development — ideally in transportation, airlines, hospitality, beverages, or a similarly dynamic industry.
Strong analytical skills with the ability to go beyond the numbers: you design solutions, not just reports.
A natural connector who actively seeks input from stakeholders, builds relationships across teams, and is not afraid to knock on doors to get things done.
Strategic thinker who can navigate fast-changing macro environments (cost shifts, demand fluctuations, competitive moves) with adaptability and clarity.
Proactive and ownership-driven: you set your own goals, follow through on commitments, and hold yourself accountable to financial targets and project deliverables.
Curious about markets — you want to understand North America's geography, network, and customer dynamics, not just analyze spreadsheets.
Collaborative and a strong team player, with excellent communication and presentation skills for senior stakeholders.
Comfortable with ambiguity and freedom; you do not need constant guidance to deliver results.
Fluent in English (additional languages are a plus).
Interest or experience in AI-powered tools and process automation is a strong advantage.


We recognize that everyone carries a unique set of valuable skills and experiences. If you think you could have an impact even though you don't meet 100% of the requirements, we still encourage you to apply. We want to hear from you! 

What We Offer


Travel perks: 12 free Flix vouchers + 12 discount vouchers for friends & family. 
Work from (M)Anywhere: Depending on your role, work from another location for up to 60 days per year. 
Hybrid work model: We are an office-first company, but we offer flexibility to balance work and life. 
Wellbeing support: Access confidential 1:1 counselling, courses, and stress management for yourself and up to four family members. 
Learning & Development: Take advantage of language classes, training courses, and expert-led sessions to grow your skills. 
Mentoring Program: Connect with experienced colleagues to gain insights and accelerate your career. 


To view more local benefits specific to each office location, please check out this link: Locations - Flix Career

Why Join Flix?

At Flix, you’ll find teams that rally together to overcome challenges and spark creativity. We believe in ownership culture - giving you the freedom to take initiative, make an impact, and shape your own career path.

As we continue to expand across the globe, you can make a real difference in how we work.

If you’re ready to grow and lead your journey, Flix is the place for you!

title: Junior Media Buyer for a US Performance Marketing Agency (Remote)
skills: Building, launching, and managing Meta campaigns (CBO/ABO structures, audiences, placements, creative testing).
Supporting and growing into independent buying on AppLovin / Axon.
Monitoring daily performance and flagging scaling opportunities, underperformers, and anomalies.
Running structured creative and audience tests, and documenting the results.
Pulling, organizing, and interpreting performance data to inform decisions, and producing clear weekly client reports.
Analyzing creative performance using soft metrics (CTR, CPM, CPC), benchmarking against account averages, and clearly communicating those reads to strategists and clients.
Helping maintain clean account hygiene, naming conventions, and tracking.
0-2 years of hands-on paid media experience (internships, freelance, or running your own / your brand's ads all count).
Clear written communication in English.
Genuine fluency with Meta Ads Manager — you've built campaigns, not just read about them.
Strong numerical sense; comfortable living in spreadsheets and dashboards.
A direct-response mindset: you care about CPA, ROAS, and contribution margin, not vanity metrics.
Familiarity with DTC e-commerce, Shopify, and the fashion/beauty/lifestyle space.
Working knowledge of tracking and attribution tools (pixels, CAPI, ClickFlare, GA4, etc.).
Comfortable staying responsive when accounts demand it, including the occasional evening or weekend during launches or scaling pushes.
Availability during key EST windows to analyze performance and take action (scale, kill, pull back, etc.).
Experience with AdManage (strongly preferred).
Hands-on time with AppLovin / Axon or other programmatic / native platforms.
Basic understanding of creative.
Competitive base salary.
Fully remote, full-time contractor role.
Learn direct-response media buying directly from senior operators managing significant spend.
Real ownership early—you won't be stuck pulling reports for a year.
Work across a diverse roster of scaling, fast-growing DTC brands in fashion, beauty, and lifestyle.
A fast-paced team environment that rewards results and fosters growth into a senior media buyer role.
description:
About the job

Paired is a global staffing and recruiting agency that specializes in pairing remote work with top-tier talent!

We are dedicated to connecting talented individuals with remarkable companies in need of their skills. Our goal is to create fulfilling job opportunities for professionals around the world.

Our client:



Looking for a Junior Media Buyer who wants to learn the performance marketing craft properly. This is a hands-in-the-account role from day one for someone obsessed with what makes an ad account scale profitably, not just spend. You will work hand-in-hand with senior buyers to plan, launch, and optimize paid campaigns primarily on Meta, with growing responsibility on AppLovin / Axon.

You don't need years of experience, but you do need sharp instincts, comfort with numbers, and the drive to get great at this fast. You'll be building campaigns, reading data, spotting what's working, and learning to make confident scale/kill decisions across a portfolio of fast-growing DTC e-commerce brands.

This is a high-performance environment built for hungry, ambitious people who take real ownership of their accounts and treat client budgets like their own.

You'll be responsible for


Building, launching, and managing Meta campaigns (CBO/ABO structures, audiences, placements, creative testing).
Supporting and growing into independent buying on AppLovin / Axon.
Monitoring daily performance and flagging scaling opportunities, underperformers, and anomalies.
Running structured creative and audience tests, and documenting the results.
Pulling, organizing, and interpreting performance data to inform decisions, and producing clear weekly client reports.
Analyzing creative performance using soft metrics (CTR, CPM, CPC), benchmarking against account averages, and clearly communicating those reads to strategists and clients.
Helping maintain clean account hygiene, naming conventions, and tracking.


Requirements


0-2 years of hands-on paid media experience (internships, freelance, or running your own / your brand's ads all count).
Clear written communication in English.
Genuine fluency with Meta Ads Manager — you've built campaigns, not just read about them.
Strong numerical sense; comfortable living in spreadsheets and dashboards.
A direct-response mindset: you care about CPA, ROAS, and contribution margin, not vanity metrics.
Familiarity with DTC e-commerce, Shopify, and the fashion/beauty/lifestyle space.
Working knowledge of tracking and attribution tools (pixels, CAPI, ClickFlare, GA4, etc.).
Comfortable staying responsive when accounts demand it, including the occasional evening or weekend during launches or scaling pushes.
Availability during key EST windows to analyze performance and take action (scale, kill, pull back, etc.).
Nice to have


Experience with AdManage (strongly preferred).
Hands-on time with AppLovin / Axon or other programmatic / native platforms.
Basic understanding of creative.


Benefits


Competitive base salary.
Fully remote, full-time contractor role.
Learn direct-response media buying directly from senior operators managing significant spend.
Real ownership early—you won't be stuck pulling reports for a year.
Work across a diverse roster of scaling, fast-growing DTC brands in fashion, beauty, and lifestyle.
A fast-paced team environment that rewards results and fosters growth into a senior media buyer role.

title: Remote Marketing Strategist
skills: Designs high-performance static and dynamic motion ads tailored specifically to the algorithms and specs of Meta, TikTok, and Google Display networks.
Produces short-form video content, kinetic typography, and animations (GIFs, motion graphics) designed to maximize engagement and watch time within paid channels.
Masters platform-specific technical requirements, striking a perfect balance between polished brand content and authentic, high-converting "UGC-style" aesthetics.
Architectures high-fidelity wireframes and designs for standalone landing pages, prioritizing Conversion Rate Optimization (CRO) and user experience (UX).
Creates visually engaging, responsive layouts for automated email lifecycle flows and editorial newsletters that maximize click-through rates (CTR).
Collaborates daily with the media buying team to audit performance data arrays, rapidly executing design iterations on "winning" assets to extend campaign longevity.
The "Conversion Eye": A profound understanding of visual hierarchy, direct-response design frameworks, and cognitive patterns that naturally guide a user’s eye toward a clear conversion event.
Multi-Disciplinary Toolbelt: Complete professional proficiency across Figma and the Adobe Creative Suite (specifically Photoshop, Illustrator, Premiere Pro, and After Effects).
Speed & Operational Agility: A proven track record of maintaining exceptional aesthetic standards while operating comfortably at a high-velocity pace inside an agile marketing team.

Data-Informed Portfolio: A comprehensive design portfolio showcasing real-world marketing assets rather than abstract art. The candidate must be equipped to explain the analytical data, testing variables, and conversion "why" behind their design executions.

Workplace Infrastructure: Ownership of a high-performance computer setup capable of handling complex video rendering and multi-frame animation software, a dedicated home office workspace, and a stable, high-speed internet connection.
Practical experience working natively within landing page builders such as Webflow, Unbounce, or Instapage.
Foundational understanding of digital marketing frameworks, including multi-variable A/B testing principles and customer acquisition cost (CAC) goals.
Direct background supporting fast-scaling D2C, E-commerce, or B2G/B2B SaaS performance marketing structures.
Performance-Based Compensation Growth: A wide baseline salary band ($1,200 – $3,000 USD) that rewards top-tier technical capability, high qualifications, and exceptional asset delivery speeds.
Direct Growth Autonomy: Step out of rigid corporate design constraints into a performance-driven sandbox where your creative variations directly scale revenue.
Highly Collaborative Environment: Partner closely with data analysts and media buyers, gaining elite, real-time feedback loops on your design executions.
100% Remote Infrastructure: Long-term, secure full-time contractor engagement managed entirely from the comfort of a dedicated home office.
description:
About the job

HireLatam is a premier recruitment agency that places top Latin American talent in independent contractor roles in US companies. With a proven track record and a commitment to excellence, we're your trusted partner in the pursuit of career success. Our extensive network, personalized approach, and supportive guidance ensure that you're in the best hands to find your next job opportunity.




Job Title: Remote Marketing Strategist (100% Work From Home) 

Location: Remote from Latin America

Position Type: Full-time

Salary: $1,200 – $3,000 USD/month (Fully dependent on creative skill level, qualifications, and design output speed)

Schedule: Monday – Friday, 9:00 AM – 6:00 PM US Eastern Time (EST), featuring a structured 1-hour lunch break from 12:00 PM – 1:00 PM EST.




About our Client




Our Client is a fast-growing, data-driven enterprise that relies on cutting-edge digital acquisition to scale its market presence. Operating with a high-velocity, performance-first marketing team, they prioritize conversion metrics over vanity metrics. They foster an agile, collaborative remote environment where creative assets are constantly measured, iterated upon, and optimized to drive real business growth and pipeline value.




The Role




Our Client is seeking a high-output, conversion-oriented Performance Designer to take complete ownership of visual assets across the entire customer acquisition funnel. This is not a traditional branding role focused solely on aesthetics; it is a high-ownership position where design must directly drive performance. The successful candidate will blend creative mastery with analytical reasoning to engineer thumb-stopping social ads, high-converting landing page layouts, engaging email campaigns, and short-form motion graphics that systematically guide users toward a definitive Call to Action (CTA).




Key Responsibilities




Paid Media Ad Creative & Motion Design




Designs high-performance static and dynamic motion ads tailored specifically to the algorithms and specs of Meta, TikTok, and Google Display networks.
Produces short-form video content, kinetic typography, and animations (GIFs, motion graphics) designed to maximize engagement and watch time within paid channels.
Masters platform-specific technical requirements, striking a perfect balance between polished brand content and authentic, high-converting "UGC-style" aesthetics.




Web Layouts, Lifecycle Marketing & Iterative Testing




Architectures high-fidelity wireframes and designs for standalone landing pages, prioritizing Conversion Rate Optimization (CRO) and user experience (UX).
Creates visually engaging, responsive layouts for automated email lifecycle flows and editorial newsletters that maximize click-through rates (CTR).
Collaborates daily with the media buying team to audit performance data arrays, rapidly executing design iterations on "winning" assets to extend campaign longevity.







Qualifications, Skills, and Key Competencies




Required Qualifications




The "Conversion Eye": A profound understanding of visual hierarchy, direct-response design frameworks, and cognitive patterns that naturally guide a user’s eye toward a clear conversion event.
Multi-Disciplinary Toolbelt: Complete professional proficiency across Figma and the Adobe Creative Suite (specifically Photoshop, Illustrator, Premiere Pro, and After Effects).
Speed & Operational Agility: A proven track record of maintaining exceptional aesthetic standards while operating comfortably at a high-velocity pace inside an agile marketing team.
Data-Informed Portfolio: A comprehensive design portfolio showcasing real-world marketing assets rather than abstract art. The candidate must be equipped to explain the analytical data, testing variables, and conversion "why" behind their design executions.
Workplace Infrastructure: Ownership of a high-performance computer setup capable of handling complex video rendering and multi-frame animation software, a dedicated home office workspace, and a stable, high-speed internet connection.




Preferred Qualifications




Practical experience working natively within landing page builders such as Webflow, Unbounce, or Instapage.
Foundational understanding of digital marketing frameworks, including multi-variable A/B testing principles and customer acquisition cost (CAC) goals.
Direct background supporting fast-scaling D2C, E-commerce, or B2G/B2B SaaS performance marketing structures.




Benefits




Performance-Based Compensation Growth: A wide baseline salary band ($1,200 – $3,000 USD) that rewards top-tier technical capability, high qualifications, and exceptional asset delivery speeds.
Direct Growth Autonomy: Step out of rigid corporate design constraints into a performance-driven sandbox where your creative variations directly scale revenue.
Highly Collaborative Environment: Partner closely with data analysts and media buyers, gaining elite, real-time feedback loops on your design executions.
100% Remote Infrastructure: Long-term, secure full-time contractor engagement managed entirely from the comfort of a dedicated home office.

title: Growth Marketer
skills: Nurture and conversion. Build and optimize nurture, re-engagement, and sales-funnel catalyst campaigns in GoHighLevel that move new leads through the funnel and into sales conversations.
Lead-to-sales handoff. Design the handoff so reps get context, not just a name. The goal is qualified leads that are ready to engage, not raw volume.
Paid search. Improve performance from Google Ads, from targeting and copy to landing pages and conversion tracking. Kill what’s not working, double down on what is.
Social creative. Manage the generation and optimization of creative for social ads, often AI-assisted. Test fast, read the data, cut losers quickly.
Reporting. Work with Operations to build improved reporting across all leads and the full marketing funnel, from first-touch attribution through to closed revenue.
The number. Own a monthly qualified-lead and pipeline target. You’ll have visibility into your performance and the autonomy to act on it.
Creative. You come up with the hook, the angle, and the test, not just the spreadsheet.
AI-native. You use AI well beyond chat. You’ve built loops, agents, or automations to do your own job faster, and you can show us what you’ve made.
A systems thinker. Your instinct is to build a repeatable system, not to grind through the same manual task twice.
Metrics-driven. You’re comfortable owning a number and being measured against it. You speak in CPL, CAC, conversion rate, and ROAS.
Excited by speed. You want to work at a fast-growing company where you ship, learn, and iterate, not wait on a quarterly planning cycle.
Target-based Experimenter: testing > assuming. You build and measure, adjust, repeat, and then scale. And know when to double down based on data + your experience.
Your answer to every lead-gen problem is “spend more on ads.” Paid is one channel, not the strategy.
You avoid committing to a number, or treat activity as the same thing as results.
description:
About the job

Growth Marketer

Seneca Cost Segregation · Remote · Salary: $2000-$3000/month + Target based bounus




About Seneca

We help real estate investors keep more of their money. Cost segregation is a tax strategy that accelerates depreciation and puts cash back in our clients’ hands, often six figures in Year 1. We’ve performed over 10,200 studies and analyzed more than $1B in cost basis, and we’ve never lost an IRS audit.

We’re a lean, fast-growing team scaling toward $5–10M. That means real ownership, real budget, and real impact from day one. No layers, no politics, no waiting for permission.




The Role

We’re hiring a Growth Marketer to own the full marketing funnel, from first touch to qualified pipeline. You’ll build the nurture and conversion systems that turn leads into sales conversations, sharpen our paid channels, and create the reporting that tells us exactly what’s working.




This is a hands-on builder role, not a team-management role (yet). You’ll do the work, with the leverage to grow as we scale. If you think in systems instead of manual effort, use AI to multiply your own output, and want to see your work move the revenue number, this is for you.




What You’ll Own

Nurture and conversion. Build and optimize nurture, re-engagement, and sales-funnel catalyst campaigns in GoHighLevel that move new leads through the funnel and into sales conversations.
Lead-to-sales handoff. Design the handoff so reps get context, not just a name. The goal is qualified leads that are ready to engage, not raw volume.
Paid search. Improve performance from Google Ads, from targeting and copy to landing pages and conversion tracking. Kill what’s not working, double down on what is.
Social creative. Manage the generation and optimization of creative for social ads, often AI-assisted. Test fast, read the data, cut losers quickly.
Reporting. Work with Operations to build improved reporting across all leads and the full marketing funnel, from first-touch attribution through to closed revenue.
The number. Own a monthly qualified-lead and pipeline target. You’ll have visibility into your performance and the autonomy to act on it.




Who You Are

Creative. You come up with the hook, the angle, and the test, not just the spreadsheet.
AI-native. You use AI well beyond chat. You’ve built loops, agents, or automations to do your own job faster, and you can show us what you’ve made.
A systems thinker. Your instinct is to build a repeatable system, not to grind through the same manual task twice.
Metrics-driven. You’re comfortable owning a number and being measured against it. You speak in CPL, CAC, conversion rate, and ROAS.
Excited by speed. You want to work at a fast-growing company where you ship, learn, and iterate, not wait on a quarterly planning cycle.
Target-based Experimenter: testing > assuming. You build and measure, adjust, repeat, and then scale. And know when to double down based on data + your experience. 

 

This Probably Isn’t a Fit If…

Your answer to every lead-gen problem is “spend more on ads.” Paid is one channel, not the strategy.
You avoid committing to a number, or treat activity as the same thing as results.




Our Stack

GoHighLevel (CRM and automation), Google Ads, Meta Ads, GA4 and Google Search Console, GTM, WordPress site, Claude AI and some Codex. You don’t need to be an expert in all of these on day one, but you should be the kind of person who gets fluent in a new tool fast and self-starts to hit your targets.




How to Apply

Send us a short note on a growth system you built and the result it drove. Include one example of a loop, agent, or automation you made to speed up your own work. We read every application that shows us something real.

description:
About the job

Ready to lead, disrupt and reinvent the sleep industry?




We are Emma – The Sleep Company. Founded in 2015, we’ve become the world’s largest D2C sleep brand, available in over 35 countries with more than 25 stores across Europe. Recommended by leading consumer associations in EMEA, APAC, and the Americas, we develop the best sleep comfort products that empower our customers to awaken their best every day. 

 

At Emma, we are a community of smart, driven people, united by a strong culture of collaboration, teamwork, and knowledge sharing. We think big, take ownership, and are empowered to drive meaningful impact. Through hands-on experience, mentorship, and continuous learning, we drive our own growth and learning. With Emmies from over 60 nationalities and offices across multiple regions, our diverse perspectives enrich our workplace culture. Ready to shape the future of sleep with us? Let’s make it happen!




What you will do: 



Develop and implement marketing strategies: Lead the creation and optimization of marketing strategies in marketplaces to enhance brand awareness and drive performance in the marketplace


Performance management: Continuously monitor and manage the performance of various marketing methods and KPIs, adapting strategies as necessary to meet business goals


Ad optimization: Oversee the performance and optimization of m


arketplace ads to ensure they effectively reach targeted audiences and yield high return on investment


Content optimization: Lead efforts in optimizing listings with updated titles, engaging descriptions, A+ content, and visually appealing images to attract and retain customers


Collaborative approach: Work cross-functionally with other teams to develop cohesive and impactful marketing campaigns, both internally in Emma and with the marketplaces POCs


Self-starter mentality: Thrive in a high-paced environment with a willingness to learn and adapt, taking complete ownership of your work stream without expecting micromanagement. 


Analytics: Utilize data and analytics to inform decision-making and strategic optimization


Flat organizational structure: Comfortable working in a non-hierarchical organization where roles are high-impact and entail a substantial degree of ownership


Budgment Management and Control: Manage, and optimize the internal budget and spend for the marketplaces channels, aligning with the counterparts for each marketplace. 








Who we are looking for:


Nice to have experience: Working with Amazon or Mercado Libre Ads or a background in selling in marketplaces


Detail-oriented: A meticulous attention to detail in developing and implementing marketing strategies and optimizing content


Innovative thinker: A proactive approach to problem-solving, with the ability to think outside the box and develop innovative solutions to complex problems


Growth-oriented: A desire to learn exponentially, constantly seeking opportunities for personal and professional development








What we offer:


A combination of personal and company growth to accelerate your career and help you reach your goals


The chance to work on exciting and challenging projects either independently or as part of a dedicated, international team


Wellbeing activities


Hybrid home office policy


Flexible schedules


Pet-friendly office


Monthly salary + pantry & restaurant vouchers


Benefits higher than the law











Become an Emmie




Emma is transforming the world of sleep - and we want the highest-performing people to help us pull it off. We want you. But only if you're willing to go all in. Only if you're willing to question, disrupt, innovate, and create from the ground up. 




We proudly celebrate diversity. We are an equal-opportunity employer committed to promoting inclusion in our workplace. We consider all qualified applicants for employment without regard to race, ethnic origin, religion or belief, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, disability, or age.




Our aim is to get back to you in a couple of days, however, we are currently receiving a large number of applications and this might lead to a delay in the process. We will get back to you as soon as possible! 




Upon your consent, we may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as get the interviews transcript and summarised by AI. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgement. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please reach out to privacy@emma-sleep.com

description:
About the job

About WPP Media

WPP is the trusted growth partner for the world’s leading brands. With exceptional talent, trusted data and intelligence, and world-class partnerships – all united by our pioneering agentic marketing platform, WPP Open – we help clients navigate change, capture opportunity, and deliver transformational growth. 

WPP Media is WPP's AI-driven media operating unit, bringing together media, data, and partnerships to deliver creative personalisation at scale. Connected through WPP Open and powered by Open Intelligence, clients see exactly where, how, and why their media investment is working.

For more information, visit wppmedia.com.

Senior Executive Social, GOC

About WPP Media

WPP is the creative transformation company. We use the power of creativity to build better futures for our people, planet, clients and communities. For more information, visit wpp.com.

WPP Media is WPP’s global media collective. In a world where media is everywhere and in everything, we bring the best platform, people, and partners together to create limitless opportunities for growth. For more information, visit wppmedia.com

At WPP Media, We Believe In The Power Of Our Culture And Our People. It’s What Elevates Us To Deliver Exceptional Experiences For Both Our Clients And Each Other. In This Role It Will Be Critical To Embrace WPP & WPP Media’s Shared Core Values:


Be Extraordinary by Leading Collectively to Inspire transformational Creativity. 
Create an Open environment by Balancing People and Client Experiences by Cultivating Trust. 
Lead Optimistically by Championing Growth and Development to Mobilize the Enterprise. 


Role Summary And Impact

As part of the Global Operations Centre (GOC), you will work closely with teams across the United States, Europe, and Asia to deliver high-impact solutions in a collaborative, multicultural environment. We are looking for a Senior Executive Social who will:


Develop strong relationships with counterparts in WPP Media’s regional teams (Europe, United States and Asia). 
Support campaigns on Facebook Ads, Twitter Ads, LinkedIn Ads, Instagram Ads, etc., for clients, according to WPP Media guidelines. 
Ensure campaigns comply with WPP Media’s global policies and guidelines. 
Monitor campaigns daily on each of the platforms and ensure accuracy of data across platforms for campaign reporting. 
Generate daily campaign reports for reporting to regional teams. 


Skills And Experience

If this opportunity looks right for you, we are looking for candidates who have:


Bachelor’s degree. 
Advanced English level (B2–C2). 
Have 1.5-2 years of implementing and optimizing paid social campaigns in platforms such as: Facebook Ads, LinkedIn Ads, Instagram Ads, Twitter Ads, etc. 
Advanced Excel skills. 


Life At WPP Media & Benefits

Our passion for shaping the next era of media includes investing in our employees to help them do their best work, and we’re just as committed to employee growth as we are to responsible media investment. WPP Media employees can tap into the global WPP Media & WPP networks to pursue their passions, grow their networks, and learn at the cutting edge of marketing and advertising. We have a variety of employee resource groups and host frequent in-office events showcasing team wins, sharing thought leadership, and celebrating holidays and milestone events. Our benefits include access to internal learning platforms, certifications, and training sessions, life insurance, wellness programs, significant paid time off, preferential partner discounts, employee mental health awareness day, and more. 

WPP Media is an equal opportunity employer and considers applicants for all positions without discrimination or regard to particular characteristics. We are committed to fostering a culture of respect in which everyone feels they belong and has the same opportunities to progress in their careers.

We believe the best work happens when we're together, fostering creativity, collaboration, and connection. If you require accommodations or flexibility, please discuss this with the hiring team during the interview process.

For Mexico:

This position is available in a Remote or Hybrid Work Modality. The candidate must perform their work from any location within Mexico. Only Mexican nationals residing in the country or foreign nationals with a valid work permit may apply. Remote work from abroad is not permitted under any circumstances.

For Colombia:

This position is in a Remote or Hybrid Work Modality and must be performed within Colombian territory. Only candidates residing in Colombia with a valid work permit may apply. Remote work contracts do not allow employment or relocation abroad.

Please read our Privacy Notice (https://www.wppmedia.com/pages/privacy-policy ) for more information on how we process the information you provide.

While we appreciate all applications received, only those candidates selected for an interview will be contacted. 

Please read our Privacy Notice for more information on how we process the information you provide.

Minh Vo - Founder
description:
About the job

I’m helping Email Clout find a top candidate to join their team flexible for the role of Email Marketing Account Manager.




You'll drive revenue and client success by independently managing end-to-end email marketing campaigns.




Compensation:

USD 300 - 1.5K/month




Location:

Remote: Mexico




Mission of Email Clout:

"Empower online brands with high-impact, revenue-driving email campaigns. Fast, accountable, and world-class results."




What makes you a strong candidate:

You are proficient in Project management, Marketing strategy, Email marketing, Data analytics, Copywriting.
You have the potential to develop in Slack, Shopify, Notion, Figma.
English - Fully fluent




Responsibilities and more:

Email Clout is an email marketing agency that partners with online brands to create unique, engaging, and revenue-generating email campaigns and flows. We’re fully remote, small, and love to move fast. We hold ourselves accountable for delivering a world-class client experience and industry-shattering email performance. 




We are looking for the ideal candidate for the role of Email Marketing Account Manager, who will be responsible for the experience and performance of client accounts. In this role, you will manage and execute email marketing campaigns and flows from start to finish. This includes coming up with ideas, planning, writing copy, communicating with clients and internal teams, as well as implementation in Klaviyo. You will use Klaviyo and Figma while managing your tasks independently through Notion. Below are the key responsibilities for this role. 




- Create and manage multiple email content calendars. 

- Plan, write, and schedule email marketing campaigns and flows. 

- Collect, report, present, and analyze past data to make the best decisions for clients. 

- Conduct and lead client calls. 

- Handle difficult questions related to email performance. 

- Make design and copy changes in Figma. 

- Collaborate and manage designers and copywriters. 

- Stay up-to-date with industry trends and best practices in email marketing. 




Software stack you’ll be using: 

- Slack 

- Klaviyo 

- Figma 

- Shopify 

- Notion 




Requirements: 

- 1-2 years of experience in email marketing, preferably with experience using Klaviyo. 

- Proficiency in Figma or similar design software. 

- Excellent written and verbal communication skills. 

- Strong attention to detail and multitasking ability. 

- Familiarity with email marketing best practices and metrics. 

- Confident in communicating with clients. 

- Perfect English (spoken and written). 




Do Not Apply If: 

- You’re okay with delays or errors in client work. 

- You’re willing to work only during regular hours. 

- You can only write when assisted by ChatGPT. 

- You don’t take deadlines seriously. 

- You lack urgency in completing work. 

- You don’t have the resourcefulness to solve problems on your own first. 

- You don’t feel confident in your email marketing abilities.




Your potential leader(s):

Minh Vo - Founder



Desired Skills and Experience

Project management, Marketing strategy, Email marketing, Data analytics, Copywriting, Client communication, Slack, Shopify, Notion, Figma

title: Marketing Specialist
skills: Digital Marketing Strategy: Develop and execute comprehensive marketing strategies aimed at first responder agencies and healthcare professionals.

Mindset: A "shark" mentality—proactive, aggressive in pursuing visibility, and capable of working without constant micromanagement.
Education: Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Communications, or a related field is highly preferred.
Monitoring Tools: Open to using monitoring tools like Hubstaff.

This is not a passive task-taking role; they need a strategic thinker who understands the American healthcare landscape and can proactively drive lead generation. You will be responsible for translating the clinical expertise of the Client into a digital presence that attracts the "right fit" clients—specifically those seeking therapy and testing rather than medication management.




Key Responsibilities

Digital Marketing Strategy: Develop and execute comprehensive marketing strategies aimed at first responder agencies and healthcare professionals.
Content Creation: Craft compelling content including blog posts, newsletters, and social media updates that resonate with our target audience.

Social Media Engagement: Manage social media platforms, creating engaging posts that drive awareness and interaction among followers.
Analytics and Reporting: Analyze marketing performance metrics to assess the effectiveness of campaigns and suggest improvements.
Audience Segmentation: Identify and segment target audiences to tailor messaging specifically for different groups, including agencies and individual first responders.
Networking and Outreach: Develop relationships with potential referral sources, including healthcare providers and first responder agencies, to expand our reach.
Collaboration: Work closely with our team to align marketing efforts with overall practice goals and client needs.




Qualifications & Skills

Experience: Minimum 2+ years of experience marketing for American-based companies (Healthcare or Service Industry preferred).
Technical Savvy: Current with modern marketing technologies and social media algorithms; ability to use AI tools to optimize workflow (but not rely on them for core creativity).
Communication: Exceptional written and verbal English skills. While primarily a creative role, you must be comfortable making occasional outbound "warm" contacts to build referral relationships.

description:
About the job

We’re not looking for a marketing guru.

We’re looking for someone who can keep things moving.




A growing property management company needs a Marketing Assistant who can turn ideas into content, keep listings updated, and make sure properties are presented professionally across every platform.




Because in property management, empty listings cost money.

And inconsistent marketing creates confusion.




That’s where you come in.

If you’re organized, creative, and know how to manage content without letting details slip through the cracks, this could be the role for you.




What You’ll Do

Create marketing content and graphics using Canva
Repurpose existing materials into fresh, engaging content
Draft flyers, newsletters, and property descriptions
Update and maintain property listings on Apartments.com and other rental platforms
Schedule and manage social media posts on Facebook and Instagram
Keep marketing materials accurate, on-brand, and posted on time
Maintain a content calendar for campaigns and updates
Support basic video editing for social and marketing content
Monitor engagement and respond to simple social media inquiries when needed




What We’re Looking For

Experience with Canva and basic marketing content creation
Familiarity with social media scheduling and posting
Basic video editing skills
Experience updating websites or property listings
Strong attention to detail and organization
Ability to manage multiple tasks independently
Clear written and verbal communication skills
Property management marketing experience is a plus




Why This Role Matters

Good marketing helps great properties get noticed.

Your work will help keep listings active, content consistent, and marketing operations running smoothly, so the team can focus on serving residents and growing the business.

If you enjoy creating content, staying organized, and making sure nothing falls through the cracks, we’d love to hear from you.

description:
About the job

Descripción del puesto En el puesto de Especialista en CRM y automatización en Centro SanaMente te encargarás de diseñar, configurar y optimizar flujos de trabajo automatizados para la gestión de personas usuarias y pacientes potenciales. En tu día a día segmentarás bases de datos, crearás campañas automatizadas (emails, recordatorios, seguimientos) y monitorizarás su rendimiento para proponer mejoras continuas. Colaborarás con los equipos de marketing y atención al paciente para definir procesos, mejorar la experiencia de la persona usuaria y aumentar la conversión y fidelización. También documentarás los procesos, elaborarás informes periódicos y ayudarás a implementar nuevas herramientas o integraciones relacionadas con el CRM. Este es un puesto de media jornada (part time) y se desarrolla en modalidad remota.




Lo que necesitamos:

Configuración y optimización de flujos en WATI (WhatsApp Business API)
Diseño e implementación de secuencias de email marketing (Mailchimp, ActiveCampaign o similar)
Criterio para adaptar los mensajes a una audiencia que viene a buscar apoyo psicológico — no cualquier tono sirve aquí
Aptitudes analíticas: capacidad para interpretar datos, extraer conclusiones accionables y proponer optimizaciones basadas en métricas y KPIs.
Gestión de relaciones con personas usuarias y ventas: experiencia en Customer Relationship Management (CRM) y en procesos de ventas orientados a la fidelización y al acompañamiento personalizado.
Comunicación y trabajo colaborativo: excelentes habilidades de comunicación escrita y verbal, así como capacidad para coordinarte con equipos multidisciplinares de forma remota.
Gestión de proyectos: aptitudes en Project Management para planificar, priorizar y hacer seguimiento de iniciativas de automatización y mejora de procesos.




Requisitos no negociables:

Basado/a en México
Facturación activa (RFC con actividad económica vigente) — indispensable para formalizar la colaboración

Lo que ofrecemos:

Colaboración recurrente part-time con pago por honorarios
Autonomía real en las decisiones técnicas dentro de tu área




Si cumples los requisitos y te interesa, escríbeme por mensaje directo incluyendo: tu tarifa para la configuración inicial de WATI y tu esquema de cobro para secuencias de email (por proyecto, por hora o como lo manejes). Eso nos permite avanzar rápido.

description:
About the job

About Puig




At Puig, we are a family-owned company with a long-term commitment to creativity, innovation, and people development. Through our Graduate Program, we are looking for exceptional young talent ready to begin their professional journey in a dynamic, international, and purpose-driven environment.




This is a unique opportunity to accelerate your career, gain exposure to a leading global organization, and contribute to the growth of prestigious dermatological brands that improve people's quality of life through science-backed skincare solutions.




Your Mission




As a Marketing Graduate (Entry Level) within our Derma Division, you will support the Marketing team in the development and execution of brand strategies, product launches, and commercial initiatives while gaining hands-on experience across multiple areas of the business.




You will work closely with cross-functional teams including Medical Affairs, Sales, Trade Marketing, Digital, and Finance, contributing to projects that have a direct impact on business performance and patient outcomes.




What You'll Do




• Support the planning, execution, and optimization of digital marketing campaigns across social media platforms, with a strong focus on TikTok and emerging digital channels.

• Assist in the creation, coordination, and publishing of engaging content aligned with brand strategies, consumer trends, and business objectives.

• Collaborate closely with the Digital & Marketing team to manage community engagement, monitor social conversations, and identify opportunities to increase brand relevance and reach.

• Support influencer and content creator initiatives, including campaign coordination, performance tracking, and reporting.

• Monitor digital KPIs and campaign performance, providing insights and recommendations to improve engagement, visibility, and business impact.

• Contribute to the development and management of a dedicated social media project aimed at healthcare professionals, helping create educational and scientifically relevant content for derma brands.

• Coordinate with Medical Visit and cross-functional teams to ensure content accuracy, compliance, and alignment with brand objectives.

• Assist in the preparation of presentations, reports, and business reviews related to digital performance, marketing initiatives, and key projects.

• Participate in local and global marketing projects, gaining exposure to brand management, consumer engagement, and omnichannel strategies.

• Provide support on ad hoc marketing initiatives, product launches, events, and special projects across the Derma division.




Who You Are

Recent graduate (Bachelor's degree completed within the last 1–2 years) in Marketing, Business Administration, Communication, Pharmaceutical Business, or related fields.
Previous internship, trainee, student leadership, or extracurricular experience is highly valued.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
Excellent communication and presentation abilities.
Curious, proactive, and eager to learn.
Collaborative mindset and ability to work in diverse teams.
Passion for marketing, healthcare, skincare, and consumer behavior.
Comfortable working in a fast-paced and dynamic environment.




Requirements

Bachelor's degree completed by the start date.
Intermediate English proficiency (written and spoken).
Advanced knowledge of Microsoft Office (Excel and PowerPoint preferred).
Availability to work full-time.
Authorization to work in Mexico.




What We Offer

Participation in a global Graduate Program designed to accelerate your development.
Exposure to senior leaders and international projects.
Structured learning and development opportunities.
Mentorship and career guidance.
A collaborative, inclusive, and innovative culture.
Opportunity to build a long-term career within Puig.




Our Commitment

At Puig, we believe that diversity, equity, and inclusion drive innovation and creativity. We are committed to creating an environment where everyone can thrive, contribute, and grow.

Join us and help shape the future of dermatological skincare through science, innovation, and purpose. ✨

title: Manager, Partner Product and Delivery
