title: Remote Marketing Strategist
skills: Designs high-performance static and dynamic motion ads tailored specifically to the algorithms and specs of Meta, TikTok, and Google Display networks.
Produces short-form video content, kinetic typography, and animations (GIFs, motion graphics) designed to maximize engagement and watch time within paid channels.
Masters platform-specific technical requirements, striking a perfect balance between polished brand content and authentic, high-converting "UGC-style" aesthetics.
Architectures high-fidelity wireframes and designs for standalone landing pages, prioritizing Conversion Rate Optimization (CRO) and user experience (UX).
Creates visually engaging, responsive layouts for automated email lifecycle flows and editorial newsletters that maximize click-through rates (CTR).
Collaborates daily with the media buying team to audit performance data arrays, rapidly executing design iterations on "winning" assets to extend campaign longevity.
The "Conversion Eye": A profound understanding of visual hierarchy, direct-response design frameworks, and cognitive patterns that naturally guide a user’s eye toward a clear conversion event.
Multi-Disciplinary Toolbelt: Complete professional proficiency across Figma and the Adobe Creative Suite (specifically Photoshop, Illustrator, Premiere Pro, and After Effects).
Speed & Operational Agility: A proven track record of maintaining exceptional aesthetic standards while operating comfortably at a high-velocity pace inside an agile marketing team.

Data-Informed Portfolio: A comprehensive design portfolio showcasing real-world marketing assets rather than abstract art. The candidate must be equipped to explain the analytical data, testing variables, and conversion "why" behind their design executions.

Workplace Infrastructure: Ownership of a high-performance computer setup capable of handling complex video rendering and multi-frame animation software, a dedicated home office workspace, and a stable, high-speed internet connection.
Practical experience working natively within landing page builders such as Webflow, Unbounce, or Instapage.
Foundational understanding of digital marketing frameworks, including multi-variable A/B testing principles and customer acquisition cost (CAC) goals.
Direct background supporting fast-scaling D2C, E-commerce, or B2G/B2B SaaS performance marketing structures.
Performance-Based Compensation Growth: A wide baseline salary band ($1,200 – $3,000 USD) that rewards top-tier technical capability, high qualifications, and exceptional asset delivery speeds.
Direct Growth Autonomy: Step out of rigid corporate design constraints into a performance-driven sandbox where your creative variations directly scale revenue.
Highly Collaborative Environment: Partner closely with data analysts and media buyers, gaining elite, real-time feedback loops on your design executions.
100% Remote Infrastructure: Long-term, secure full-time contractor engagement managed entirely from the comfort of a dedicated home office.
description:
About the job

HireLatam is a premier recruitment agency that places top Latin American talent in independent contractor roles in US companies. With a proven track record and a commitment to excellence, we're your trusted partner in the pursuit of career success. Our extensive network, personalized approach, and supportive guidance ensure that you're in the best hands to find your next job opportunity.




Job Title: Remote Marketing Strategist (100% Work From Home) 

Location: Remote from Latin America

Position Type: Full-time

Salary: $1,200 – $3,000 USD/month (Fully dependent on creative skill level, qualifications, and design output speed)

Schedule: Monday – Friday, 9:00 AM – 6:00 PM US Eastern Time (EST), featuring a structured 1-hour lunch break from 12:00 PM – 1:00 PM EST.




About our Client




Our Client is a fast-growing, data-driven enterprise that relies on cutting-edge digital acquisition to scale its market presence. Operating with a high-velocity, performance-first marketing team, they prioritize conversion metrics over vanity metrics. They foster an agile, collaborative remote environment where creative assets are constantly measured, iterated upon, and optimized to drive real business growth and pipeline value.




The Role




Our Client is seeking a high-output, conversion-oriented Performance Designer to take complete ownership of visual assets across the entire customer acquisition funnel. This is not a traditional branding role focused solely on aesthetics; it is a high-ownership position where design must directly drive performance. The successful candidate will blend creative mastery with analytical reasoning to engineer thumb-stopping social ads, high-converting landing page layouts, engaging email campaigns, and short-form motion graphics that systematically guide users toward a definitive Call to Action (CTA).




Key Responsibilities




Paid Media Ad Creative & Motion Design




Designs high-performance static and dynamic motion ads tailored specifically to the algorithms and specs of Meta, TikTok, and Google Display networks.
Produces short-form video content, kinetic typography, and animations (GIFs, motion graphics) designed to maximize engagement and watch time within paid channels.
Masters platform-specific technical requirements, striking a perfect balance between polished brand content and authentic, high-converting "UGC-style" aesthetics.




Web Layouts, Lifecycle Marketing & Iterative Testing




Architectures high-fidelity wireframes and designs for standalone landing pages, prioritizing Conversion Rate Optimization (CRO) and user experience (UX).
Creates visually engaging, responsive layouts for automated email lifecycle flows and editorial newsletters that maximize click-through rates (CTR).
Collaborates daily with the media buying team to audit performance data arrays, rapidly executing design iterations on "winning" assets to extend campaign longevity.







Qualifications, Skills, and Key Competencies




Required Qualifications




The "Conversion Eye": A profound understanding of visual hierarchy, direct-response design frameworks, and cognitive patterns that naturally guide a user’s eye toward a clear conversion event.
Multi-Disciplinary Toolbelt: Complete professional proficiency across Figma and the Adobe Creative Suite (specifically Photoshop, Illustrator, Premiere Pro, and After Effects).
Speed & Operational Agility: A proven track record of maintaining exceptional aesthetic standards while operating comfortably at a high-velocity pace inside an agile marketing team.
Data-Informed Portfolio: A comprehensive design portfolio showcasing real-world marketing assets rather than abstract art. The candidate must be equipped to explain the analytical data, testing variables, and conversion "why" behind their design executions.
Workplace Infrastructure: Ownership of a high-performance computer setup capable of handling complex video rendering and multi-frame animation software, a dedicated home office workspace, and a stable, high-speed internet connection.




Preferred Qualifications




Practical experience working natively within landing page builders such as Webflow, Unbounce, or Instapage.
Foundational understanding of digital marketing frameworks, including multi-variable A/B testing principles and customer acquisition cost (CAC) goals.
Direct background supporting fast-scaling D2C, E-commerce, or B2G/B2B SaaS performance marketing structures.




Benefits




Performance-Based Compensation Growth: A wide baseline salary band ($1,200 – $3,000 USD) that rewards top-tier technical capability, high qualifications, and exceptional asset delivery speeds.
Direct Growth Autonomy: Step out of rigid corporate design constraints into a performance-driven sandbox where your creative variations directly scale revenue.
Highly Collaborative Environment: Partner closely with data analysts and media buyers, gaining elite, real-time feedback loops on your design executions.
100% Remote Infrastructure: Long-term, secure full-time contractor engagement managed entirely from the comfort of a dedicated home office.

title: Marketing Specialist
skills: Digital Marketing Strategy: Develop and execute comprehensive marketing strategies aimed at first responder agencies and healthcare professionals.
Content Creation: Craft compelling content including blog posts, newsletters, and social media updates that resonate with our target audience.
SEO Management: Optimize website content for search engines to enhance visibility for relevant keywords related to mental health services for first responders.
Social Media Engagement: Manage social media platforms, creating engaging posts that drive awareness and interaction among followers.
Analytics and Reporting: Analyze marketing performance metrics to assess the effectiveness of campaigns and suggest improvements.
Audience Segmentation: Identify and segment target audiences to tailor messaging specifically for different groups, including agencies and individual first responders.
Networking and Outreach: Develop relationships with potential referral sources, including healthcare providers and first responder agencies, to expand our reach.
Collaboration: Work closely with our team to align marketing efforts with overall practice goals and client needs.
Experience: Minimum 2+ years of experience marketing for American-based companies (Healthcare or Service Industry preferred).
Technical Savvy: Current with modern marketing technologies and social media algorithms; ability to use AI tools to optimize workflow (but not rely on them for core creativity).
Communication: Exceptional written and verbal English skills. While primarily a creative role, you must be comfortable making occasional outbound "warm" contacts to build referral relationships.
Mindset: A "shark" mentality—proactive, aggressive in pursuing visibility, and capable of working without constant micromanagement.
Education: Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Communications, or a related field is highly preferred.
Monitoring Tools: Open to using monitoring tools like Hubstaff.
description:
About the job

✨ IMPORTANT – WATCH THIS QUICK LOOM VIDEO ON HOW TO GET HIRED:

📽️ https://www.loom.com/share/345f776084e642ba99c24aabe40a67a0

🇬🇧 C1 ENGLISH LEVEL IS REQUIRED.

The selected candidate will be speaking strictly in English during their entire shift.

🎤 PLEASE SUBMIT YOUR RESUME AND AUDIO RECORDING IN ENGLISH




Job Details




JOB TITLE

Marketing Specialist

JOB ID

BYRTIA1

INDUSTRY

Medical

LOCATION

LATAM / South Africa / PH if needed

JOB STATUS

Part Time (to full time)

WORK SCHEDULE

Monday to Friday, availability during EST business hours

SALARY

$8 - $11 per hour

TARGET START DATE

ASAP




ROLE OVERVIEW




About the Client:

The Client is a growing psychological therapy and testing practice. We specialize in providing high-quality therapy and diagnostic testing (e.g., for first responders and police candidates). We are currently at a pivotal growth phase and are looking for a proactive, tech-savvy marketing professional to help us overcome our current financial bottleneck and scale our team of clinicians.




About the Role:

Our Client is seeking a high-energy Marketing Specialist (Virtual Assistant). This is not a passive task-taking role; they need a strategic thinker who understands the American healthcare landscape and can proactively drive lead generation. You will be responsible for translating the clinical expertise of the Client into a digital presence that attracts the "right fit" clients—specifically those seeking therapy and testing rather than medication management.




Key Responsibilities

Digital Marketing Strategy: Develop and execute comprehensive marketing strategies aimed at first responder agencies and healthcare professionals.
Content Creation: Craft compelling content including blog posts, newsletters, and social media updates that resonate with our target audience.
SEO Management: Optimize website content for search engines to enhance visibility for relevant keywords related to mental health services for first responders.
Social Media Engagement: Manage social media platforms, creating engaging posts that drive awareness and interaction among followers.
Analytics and Reporting: Analyze marketing performance metrics to assess the effectiveness of campaigns and suggest improvements.
Audience Segmentation: Identify and segment target audiences to tailor messaging specifically for different groups, including agencies and individual first responders.
Networking and Outreach: Develop relationships with potential referral sources, including healthcare providers and first responder agencies, to expand our reach.
Collaboration: Work closely with our team to align marketing efforts with overall practice goals and client needs.




Qualifications & Skills

Experience: Minimum 2+ years of experience marketing for American-based companies (Healthcare or Service Industry preferred).
Technical Savvy: Current with modern marketing technologies and social media algorithms; ability to use AI tools to optimize workflow (but not rely on them for core creativity).
Communication: Exceptional written and verbal English skills. While primarily a creative role, you must be comfortable making occasional outbound "warm" contacts to build referral relationships.
Mindset: A "shark" mentality—proactive, aggressive in pursuing visibility, and capable of working without constant micromanagement.
Education: Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Communications, or a related field is highly preferred.
Monitoring Tools: Open to using monitoring tools like Hubstaff.

title: Creative Technology Senior Associate
skills: Support ad technology, tech integration tests, dynamic [DCO], and omni-channel campaign production efforts across multiple brand teams, geographical locations and products (including new business and new agency tech initiatives)
Contribute to large scale programmatic, social and video campaigns across multiple formats and channels employing underlying code bases such as HTML/JS/CSS, and industry leading ad technology.

Support in the auditing and defining criteria for all functional and non-functional deliverable requirements including dynamic ad serving, data pass work streams, API/product feed integrations, asset tracking and tagging, performance outputs, creative authoring tools, scalability and quality assurance

Facilitate and cultivate relationships with our preferred platform partners, to establish development best practices and requirements, ensure continued innovation through product advancements/ways of working, and continuity of service through reporting and troubleshooting.
Encourage innovation, adoption of cutting-edge technologies, inclusion, out-of-the-box thinking, cross-team collaboration, self-organization, and diversity

Support PMG on devising strategic roadmaps and onboarding materials for the continued advancements in product development and campaign delivery, QA automation tools and process adoption through our international developer hubs, with POVs on Build, Buy, Borrow.
Become a super-user on new tools developed by PMG Product Teams.
3+ years of experience, including at least 2 years working at a Creative, AdTech and or Digital Agency
Knowledge of digital creative best practices and deliverables
Ability to write scalable and modular code to employ multi-variant testing and dynamic creative industry standards.
Digital Creative Development (front-end) or relevant ad technology background with 6+ years experience
Demonstrated ability to balance campaign objectives and time-to-market requirements without compromising product quality
Strong understanding of IAB Standards and Digital Ad Formats & specifications

Training and Certifications on PMG required DCO ad serving platforms - primary Google GMP and similar knowledge of Clinch, Celtra, Google GMP (DCM, GWD, DCS), Innovid, Smartly, Jivox, Bannerflow, Google Web Designer
Working knowledge of Project Management & DevOps tools like Airtable, JIRA, Smartsheets, Asana, Github
Self-starter with a high degree of self-motivation and initiative with the ability to build processes from the ground up and collaborate in a multi-stakeholder environment
Strong written and verbal communication skills
Detailed oriented with the ability support development team through troubleshooting and Quality assurance
Proficiency in Microsoft Office Tools (Excel, Word, PPT, Outlook, Teams)
Familiarity working with Creative Assets and Design apps such as the Adobe Creative Suite (primarily Photoshop, Illustrator, Adobe XD, Adobe After Effects)
2+ years working with internal and third-party APIs [REST, SOAP, JSON] like Open Weather Map or CRM data
1+ years Python experience or comparable programming language
Understanding of audience analytics capabilities and best practices (measurement, matching, onboarding, workflow, limitations, and workarounds, etc.) in the current environment
Experience working with excel formulas for data matching, aggregation and math
Experience with database systems, Website CMS tools queries and normalization
Basic understanding and ability to work in After Effects as needed.
Working knowledge of data factors driving project success (e.g. PII vs. anonymized data, cookies vs. people, panels vs. census, passive vs. active/survey data, fused vs. matched data, etc.)
Professional Development: Take advantage of our learning and development programs, mentorship opportunities, and career advancement support.
Generous Time Off: Enjoy generous paid time off and holiday allowances to recharge and spend time with loved ones.
Parental Leave: We provide paid parental leave to support your family during important life events.
Retirement & Pension Plans: Plan for your future with competitive retirement or pension programs, including contribution matching.
Fertility and Family Support: Access fertility benefits for all team members and their spouses.
Healthcare: Coverage and support for everyday medical expenses and routine care, tailored by geography.
Pet Insurance: Protect your pet's health and your finances.
Lifestyle Spending Accounts: Enjoy 100% company-funded accounts to promote healthy habits and well-being.
Commuter Benefits: Access support for travel and commuting needs, where available.
Annual Bonus: All employees are eligible for an annual bonus.
Volunteering Opportunities: Receive 8 give-back hours to volunteer in your local communities.
AI Enterprise License: Access AI Enterprise accounts and participate in weekly AI training sessions to empower and ensure AI safety.
description:
About the job

We are PMG

Named Ad Age Best Places to Work for 11 years in a row and Fast Company Best Workplaces for Innovators since 2022, PMG is a global independent marketing services and technology company that seeks to inspire people and brands that anything is possible. Driven by shared success, PMG leverages business strategy and transformation, creative, media, and insights—as well as its proprietary operating system, Alli—to deliver cutting-edge digital solutions and innovative media strategies.

Our team comprises over 900 employees globally, and our work for ambitious brands like Apple, Nike, Sephora, Experian, Therabody, Best Western Hotels & Resorts, Creed Fragrances, BNY, Intuit, CKE Restaurants, Travelex, and Whole Foods spans 85+ countries.

Professional proficiency in written and spoken English is required, including for resume submission and interviews.

Our Creative team is looking for Creative Technology Senior Associate to join a team of multidisciplinary developers, executing best-in-class omni-channel projects leveraging industry leading Advertising Technologies, Ad Servers and Ad builder/DCO [dynamic content optimization] tools.

Working directly with a wide range of tech partners, inter-company and external integrated teams, the Creative Technology Senior Associate will support all dynamic creative and asset automation project buildouts, delivery and integration into ad servers. You should be comfortable working directly with multi-disciplinary team leads across the PMG both in your primary office location and supported global markets.

What You'll Be Doing


Support ad technology, tech integration tests, dynamic [DCO], and omni-channel campaign production efforts across multiple brand teams, geographical locations and products (including new business and new agency tech initiatives)
Contribute to large scale programmatic, social and video campaigns across multiple formats and channels employing underlying code bases such as HTML/JS/CSS, and industry leading ad technology. 
Support in the auditing and defining criteria for all functional and non-functional deliverable requirements including dynamic ad serving, data pass work streams, API/product feed integrations, asset tracking and tagging, performance outputs, creative authoring tools, scalability and quality assurance
Facilitate and cultivate relationships with our preferred platform partners, to establish development best practices and requirements, ensure continued innovation through product advancements/ways of working, and continuity of service through reporting and troubleshooting. 
Encourage innovation, adoption of cutting-edge technologies, inclusion, out-of-the-box thinking, cross-team collaboration, self-organization, and diversity
Support PMG on devising strategic roadmaps and onboarding materials for the continued advancements in product development and campaign delivery, QA automation tools and process adoption through our international developer hubs, with POVs on Build, Buy, Borrow. 
Become a super-user on new tools developed by PMG Product Teams. 


What You'll Bring


3+ years of experience, including at least 2 years working at a Creative, AdTech and or Digital Agency 
Knowledge of digital creative best practices and deliverables
Ability to write scalable and modular code to employ multi-variant testing and dynamic creative industry standards. 
Digital Creative Development (front-end) or relevant ad technology background with 6+ years experience 
Demonstrated ability to balance campaign objectives and time-to-market requirements without compromising product quality
Strong understanding of IAB Standards and Digital Ad Formats & specifications
Training and Certifications on PMG required DCO ad serving platforms - primary Google GMP and similar knowledge of Clinch, Celtra, Google GMP (DCM, GWD, DCS), Innovid, Smartly, Jivox, Bannerflow, Google Web Designer
Working knowledge of Project Management & DevOps tools like Airtable, JIRA, Smartsheets, Asana, Github
Self-starter with a high degree of self-motivation and initiative with the ability to build processes from the ground up and collaborate in a multi-stakeholder environment
Strong written and verbal communication skills
Detailed oriented with the ability support development team through troubleshooting and Quality assurance
Proficiency in Microsoft Office Tools (Excel, Word, PPT, Outlook, Teams) 
Familiarity working with Creative Assets and Design apps such as the Adobe Creative Suite (primarily Photoshop, Illustrator, Adobe XD, Adobe After Effects)


Preferred Skills


2+ years working with internal and third-party APIs [REST, SOAP, JSON] like Open Weather Map or CRM data
1+ years Python experience or comparable programming language 
Understanding of audience analytics capabilities and best practices (measurement, matching, onboarding, workflow, limitations, and workarounds, etc.) in the current environment
Experience working with excel formulas for data matching, aggregation and math
Experience with database systems, Website CMS tools queries and normalization
Basic understanding and ability to work in After Effects as needed. 
Working knowledge of data factors driving project success (e.g. PII vs. anonymized data, cookies vs. people, panels vs. census, passive vs. active/survey data, fused vs. matched data, etc.)


What We Offer


Professional Development: Take advantage of our learning and development programs, mentorship opportunities, and career advancement support. 
Generous Time Off: Enjoy generous paid time off and holiday allowances to recharge and spend time with loved ones. 
Parental Leave: We provide paid parental leave to support your family during important life events. 
Retirement & Pension Plans: Plan for your future with competitive retirement or pension programs, including contribution matching. 
Fertility and Family Support: Access fertility benefits for all team members and their spouses. 
Healthcare: Coverage and support for everyday medical expenses and routine care, tailored by geography. 
Pet Insurance: Protect your pet's health and your finances. 
Lifestyle Spending Accounts: Enjoy 100% company-funded accounts to promote healthy habits and well-being. 
Commuter Benefits: Access support for travel and commuting needs, where available. 
Annual Bonus: All employees are eligible for an annual bonus. 
Volunteering Opportunities: Receive 8 give-back hours to volunteer in your local communities. 
AI Enterprise License: Access AI Enterprise accounts and participate in weekly AI training sessions to empower and ensure AI safety. 


Benefits vary by country, location, and geography to reflect local laws, norms, and expectations.

What Sets Us Apart

Being part of PMG means joining a company culture that’s unmatched in digital. We're dedicated to working hard to serve our employees and clients, delivering value, results, and innovation—which often requires true grit and agility. We believe in taking care of ourselves and each other to continuously improve in every way.

In alignment with our core values to be inclusive and always change for the better, PMG is committed to creating a more diverse and inclusive culture, and we are proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We believe that we only change for the better by bringing diverse perspectives to our company. PMG recruits, employs, trains, compensates, and promotes regardless of race, color, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, genetics, disability, age, or veteran status.

title: Senior SEO Content Strategist
skills: Own and execute the organic growth roadmap — keyword strategy, competitive benchmarking, prioritization, and quarterly KPI targets.
Lead technical SEO: site architecture, crawlability, page speed, schema markup, internal linking, and Local SEO across CoinFlip’s kiosk footprint (Google Business Profiles).
Partner with developers and designers to ship technical improvements and prevent regressions on launches.
Maintain SEO tooling stack (GSC, GA, SEMrush/Ahrefs, Screaming Frog) and report on performance with actionable insights.
Develop and execute the content strategy across blog, landing pages, and educational guides.
Write and edit high-quality, SEO-optimized content that translates complex crypto and fintech concepts into clear, accurate, user-friendly material.
Run regular content audits; refresh, consolidate, or sunset underperforming pages.
Maintain CoinFlip’s tone of voice and ensure content meets compliance review standards.

Partner with the Communications team on data-led digital PR campaigns — supplying original research, industry data studies, and newsworthy assets designed to earn backlinks from crypto, fintech, and mainstream publications.
Source and respond to reporter requests via HARO, Qwoted, and similar platforms in coordination with Communications, producing expert commentary on a tight turnaround.
Identify backlink targets and feed a shared target publication list with Communications.
Track domain authority, referring domains, and link quality; report on link velocity and the SEO impact of earned coverage.
Partner cross-functionally with product, compliance, design, growth, and communications on launches, campaigns, and integrated initiatives.
Other duties as assigned.
5–7 years of combined SEO and content experience, ideally in fintech, crypto, or another regulated industry.
Demonstrated track record of driving organic traffic and ranking growth as both strategist and individual contributor — portfolio of published content and link-earning campaigns required.
Deep technical SEO knowledge: search algorithms, site architecture, page speed, schema markup, Core Web Vitals.
Strong writer and editor — able to produce publication-quality content on complex financial topics without sacrificing accuracy.
Hands-on experience supporting digital PR / link-building via HARO, Qwoted, or similar.
Proficiency with GSC, GA, SEMrush, Ahrefs, Screaming Frog, and a CMS (WordPress, Drupal, or similar).
Working knowledge of HTML/CSS.
Strong analytical skills; able to translate data into strategy and into clear written narratives.
Excellent communication and cross-functional collaboration skills.
Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Communications, Journalism, Business, or a related field.
Working knowledge of cryptocurrency, blockchain, and fintech.
Experience optimizing Local SEO via Google Business Profiles at scale.
Familiarity with how SEO integrates with paid media, email, and social.
Basic data visualization (Tableau, Looker, or similar) for building data-led link-earning studies.
description:
About the job

Description

CoinFlip is a global digital currency platform company, focused on providing consumers simple and secure access to buy and sell cryptocurrency. The company operates the world's largest network of cryptocurrency kiosks by transaction volume with more than 5,500 kiosks across 49 U.S. states, Puerto Rico, Canada, Australia, New Zealand, South Africa, Italy, Panama, Brazil Mexico, and Spain. CoinFlip’s digital currency kiosks make buying and selling major cryptocurrencies accessible and safe for consumers who wish to purchase their digital currency using cash. CoinFlip also operates CoinFlip Preferred, a personalized over-the-counter service that provides investors with custom, white glove support for their cryptocurrency transactions. In 2022, CoinFlip launched CoinFlip Ventures, an investment group offering coaching, funding, and networking support to early-stage crypto and web3 projects.

CoinFlip was founded in 2015 by Daniel Polotsky, Kris Dayrit, Alan Gurevich, and Ben Weiss. Headquartered in Chicago, CoinFlip placed in the top 500 on the 2021, 2022, and 2023 Inc. 5000 list, and on the 2022 and 2023 Deloitte Technology Fast 500, was named the 2021 and 2022 #1 fastest-growing company in Chicago by Crain's, ranked in Chicago Tribune’s Top Workplaces in 2021 and 2022, and was awarded the 2021 and 2022 Stevie ® Awards for Customer Service. To learn more about CoinFlip and how to get started on your digital currency journey, visit www.CoinFlip.tech.

CoinFlip is looking for a Senior SEO Content Strategist to own organic growth end-to-end — from strategy and technical SEO through content production and digital PR. This is a hands-on hybrid role for someone who can think strategically about keyword opportunities and competitive positioning, execute the technical work to capture them, write the content that ranks and converts, and partner with communications to earn the high-authority backlinks that compound it all. You’ll be the single point of accountability for organic traffic and organic-sourced conversions across CoinFlip’s web properties, partnering with product, compliance, design, growth, and communications to translate complex crypto and fintech topics into content people search for and publications cite.

Responsibilities


Own and execute the organic growth roadmap — keyword strategy, competitive benchmarking, prioritization, and quarterly KPI targets. 
Lead technical SEO: site architecture, crawlability, page speed, schema markup, internal linking, and Local SEO across CoinFlip’s kiosk footprint (Google Business Profiles). 
Partner with developers and designers to ship technical improvements and prevent regressions on launches. 
Maintain SEO tooling stack (GSC, GA, SEMrush/Ahrefs, Screaming Frog) and report on performance with actionable insights. 
Develop and execute the content strategy across blog, landing pages, and educational guides. 
Write and edit high-quality, SEO-optimized content that translates complex crypto and fintech concepts into clear, accurate, user-friendly material. 
Run regular content audits; refresh, consolidate, or sunset underperforming pages. 
Maintain CoinFlip’s tone of voice and ensure content meets compliance review standards. 
Partner with the Communications team on data-led digital PR campaigns — supplying original research, industry data studies, and newsworthy assets designed to earn backlinks from crypto, fintech, and mainstream publications. 
Source and respond to reporter requests via HARO, Qwoted, and similar platforms in coordination with Communications, producing expert commentary on a tight turnaround. 
Identify backlink targets and feed a shared target publication list with Communications. 
Track domain authority, referring domains, and link quality; report on link velocity and the SEO impact of earned coverage. 
Partner cross-functionally with product, compliance, design, growth, and communications on launches, campaigns, and integrated initiatives. 
Other duties as assigned. 


Qualifications


5–7 years of combined SEO and content experience, ideally in fintech, crypto, or another regulated industry. 
Demonstrated track record of driving organic traffic and ranking growth as both strategist and individual contributor — portfolio of published content and link-earning campaigns required. 
Deep technical SEO knowledge: search algorithms, site architecture, page speed, schema markup, Core Web Vitals. 
Strong writer and editor — able to produce publication-quality content on complex financial topics without sacrificing accuracy. 
Hands-on experience supporting digital PR / link-building via HARO, Qwoted, or similar. 
Proficiency with GSC, GA, SEMrush, Ahrefs, Screaming Frog, and a CMS (WordPress, Drupal, or similar). 
Working knowledge of HTML/CSS. 
Strong analytical skills; able to translate data into strategy and into clear written narratives. 
Excellent communication and cross-functional collaboration skills. 


Nice To Have


Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Communications, Journalism, Business, or a related field. 
Working knowledge of cryptocurrency, blockchain, and fintech. 
Experience optimizing Local SEO via Google Business Profiles at scale. 
Familiarity with how SEO integrates with paid media, email, and social. 
Basic data visualization (Tableau, Looker, or similar) for building data-led link-earning studies. 


Compensation

Expected Annual Salary: $30,000 - $50,000 USD

Working at CoinFlip means collaborating with experienced and innovative leaders who share a clear vision and a track record of success. We offer a collaborative and positive working environment where we encourage employees to balance productivity with time to recharge. Compensation is above and beyond a typical “startup” — we offer competitive salaries, performance-based incentives, and competitive benefits for full-time employees.

CoinFlip values diversity in the workplace and is an equal opportunity employer committed to providing an inclusive and accessible work environment. We thank all candidates who apply, but only those selected for an interview will be contacted.

By applying to this role, you give express consent to CoinFlip to send you informational text (SMS) messages regarding this role and the application process. You can cancel the SMS service at any time by replying "STOP" to the text message you received. If at any time you forget what keywords are supported, just reply "HELP." Message and data rates apply. If you require a special accommodation, please let us know and we’ll work with you to meet your needs.

title: Manager, Partner Product and Delivery
skills: Participate in initial discussions with potential partners, explaining how partners can distribute premium offerings in different ways.
Work with the business team on the technical setup of the partnership.
Ensure that Spotify's commitments in a partner relationship are delivered, as well as follow-up on the partner's commitments.
Document all actions with partners for keeping track of changes.
Develop our tooling together with R&D to make sure we can scale the business.
Documented working experience within telecom, Internet start-up, media or the digital music industry.
Proficient in written and spoken English and Spanish.
Bachelor's degree in Computer Science or equivalent experience.

You are proficient with REST APIs, SQL and big data analytics such as BigQuery, product and project tools such as JIRA and Github, productivity tools such as Gemini, ChatGPT, and common understanding of AI functionality, Unix and Shell scripting.
Experience in writing software in one or more languages, such as Java or Python.
A self-motivated can-do demeanor, you won’t be afraid to step in when needed.
You understand that collaboration and team play is key to success.
Strong presentation skills and shown success in working directly with partners.
A consistent track record in technical leadership, explaining complex relations both to technical and business people.
Experience working under deadlines and prioritizing multiple projects simultaneously.
Willing to travel, mainly Latin America.

For this role, the preferred locations are Mexico City, or Sao Paolo. We offer you the flexibility to work where you work best! There will be some in-person meetings, but still allows for flexibility to work from home.
This team operates within the Eastern Standard time zone for collaboration.
description:
About the job

We are looking for a Manager, Partner Product and Delivery, who will join our team spread across the globe, and help manage Spotify's important Premium Partnerships projects in Latin America. You will have the operational and day-to-day responsibility to manage the technical implementation and performance of the partnerships. You will report directly to the Associate Director, Partner Product and Delivery.

What You'll Do


Participate in initial discussions with potential partners, explaining how partners can distribute premium offerings in different ways.
Work with the business team on the technical setup of the partnership.
Ensure that Spotify's commitments in a partner relationship are delivered, as well as follow-up on the partner's commitments.
Document all actions with partners for keeping track of changes.
Develop our tooling together with R&D to make sure we can scale the business.


Who You Are


Documented working experience within telecom, Internet start-up, media or the digital music industry.
Proficient in written and spoken English and Spanish.
Bachelor's degree in Computer Science or equivalent experience.
You are proficient with REST APIs, SQL and big data analytics such as BigQuery, product and project tools such as JIRA and Github, productivity tools such as Gemini, ChatGPT, and common understanding of AI functionality, Unix and Shell scripting.
Experience in writing software in one or more languages, such as Java or Python.
A self-motivated can-do demeanor, you won’t be afraid to step in when needed.
You understand that collaboration and team play is key to success.
Strong presentation skills and shown success in working directly with partners.
A consistent track record in technical leadership, explaining complex relations both to technical and business people.
Experience working under deadlines and prioritizing multiple projects simultaneously.
Willing to travel, mainly Latin America.


Where You'll Be


For this role, the preferred locations are Mexico City, or Sao Paolo. We offer you the flexibility to work where you work best! There will be some in-person meetings, but still allows for flexibility to work from home.
This team operates within the Eastern Standard time zone for collaboration.


Spotify is an equal opportunity employer. You are welcome at Spotify for who you are, no matter where you come from, what you look like, or what’s playing in your headphones. Our platform is for everyone, and so is our workplace. The more voices we have represented and amplified in our business, the more we will all thrive, contribute, and be forward-thinking! So bring us your personal experience, your perspectives, and your background. It’s in our differences that we will find the power to keep revolutionizing the way the world listens.

At Spotify, we are passionate about inclusivity and making sure our entire recruitment process is accessible to everyone. We have ways to request reasonable accommodations during the interview process and help assist in what you need. If you need accommodations at any stage of the application or interview process, please let us know - we’re here to support you in any way we can.

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us. Find our AI notice here: https://lifeatspotify.com/ai-notice

Today, we are the world’s most popular audio streaming subscription service.

description:
About the job

We're looking for a Travel Concierge who is passionate about crafting seamless travel experiences and delivering high-quality client service. You will be the trusted expert behind every journey, using AI-powered tools to research and recommend hotels, coordinating logistics across platforms, and communicating directly with travelers via WhatsApp to make every trip feel effortless and personal.




This role is for someone who loves travel, writes with warmth and clarity, thinks fast when plans change, and takes pride in the kind of attention to detail that makes clients feel like VIPs from the first message through check-in.




Why You'll Want to Join

You will be paid in USD (bi-monthly: every 15th and 30th)
Paid Time Off in accordance with company policy
Observance of Holidays per company guidelines
100% remote setup so you can work wherever you're most productive
Work in the luxury travel and hospitality space with access to premium brands and destinations
Use cutting-edge AI tools to enhance research and elevate the client experience




What You'll Work On

Client Engagement and Support

Chat with travelers via WhatsApp to understand their preferences and deliver personalized recommendations
Respond quickly and thoughtfully to incoming inquiries with initial responses landing within 10 to 20 minutes during your shift
Handle special requests and ensure smooth communication between clients and hotels or vendors
Maintain a calm, professional, and reassuring tone even when plans change at the last minute




Research and Booking

Research and recommend hotels, restaurants, and attractions using AI tools and reliable online sources
Verify every detail including location, timing, currency, and amenities before confirming bookings
Coordinate hotel reservations, car rentals, and travel logistics across multiple booking platforms




Content and Destination Curation

Write and curate destination guides and travel recommendations that blend editorial polish with practical, on-the-ground insight
Collaborate with the marketing team to highlight exceptional hotels and destinations for social content
Stay current on travel trends, new openings, and hidden gems worth knowing about




Problem-Solving and Adaptability

Think fast and find creative solutions when plans shift due to flight delays, cancellations, or unexpected changes
Communicate clearly and calmly under pressure to maintain client confidence and satisfaction




What You Bring

Empathetic and client-obsessed: you treat every traveler like a VIP
Detail-oriented and reliable: you catch mistakes others miss before they become problems
Exceptional written English: you write with clarity, warmth, and professionalism
A self-starter: you love learning, exploring, and improving processes without being asked
Tech-savvy: comfortable using Slack, WhatsApp, Google Sheets and Docs, and booking platforms
Fast but thoughtful: you balance responsiveness with high-quality work
Curious and well-informed: you research effectively, verify information, and stay ahead of travel trends




Nice to Have

Background in hospitality, travel, or concierge services
Experience with AI tools such as ChatGPT or travel research assistants
Familiarity with airlines, loyalty programs, and travel booking systems
International travel experience or strong knowledge of major global destinations
Negotiation skills to advocate for clients with vendors and hotels




How to Apply

Please include:

Your updated resume highlighting any customer service or travel-related experience
A short Loom video (1 to 2 minutes) introducing yourself and sharing why you are passionate about travel and client service
A short writing sample such as an email, WhatsApp message, or short paragraph showing your conversational tone




Only candidates who submit a Loom video will be moved to the next step of the hiring process.

If you love connecting with people, solving travel challenges, and creating experiences that feel effortless for the traveler, this role gives you the platform and the tools to do that every single day.

title: Jefe de Producto - APIVITA
skills: Liderar la estrategia anual de marca, identificando oportunidades de crecimiento y asegurando una ejecución alineada con los objetivos de negocio y la visión global de APIVITA.

Desarrollar e implementar campañas de marketing 360° que integren canales digitales, e-commerce, retail, trade marketing, relaciones públicas, influencers y actividades dirigidas a profesionales de la salud.
Liderar el lanzamiento de innovaciones y novedades, construyendo planes de activación que maximicen el impacto comercial y la relevancia de la marca.

Capacidad para liderar proyectos de manera transversal e influir en equipos multidisciplinarios.
Mentalidad emprendedora, curiosidad, creatividad y pasión por la innovación.
Inglés Intermedio - avanzado
description:
About the job

Acerca de la Compañía.




En Puig creemos que la belleza tiene el poder de transformar la vida de las personas. Hoy buscamos a un(a) apasionado(a) del marketing, con mentalidad estratégica, visión de negocio y una profunda orientación al consumidor, para liderar el crecimiento de APIVITA en México.




Inspirada por la naturaleza y respaldada por la ciencia, es una marca que promueve una visión holística de la belleza y el bienestar. Esta posición representa una oportunidad única para desarrollar una marca con propósito, construir experiencias memorables y generar un impacto real en el mercado de la dermo.




Tu misión




Serás responsable de diseñar e implementar la estrategia integral 360 de APIVITA en México, impulsando el crecimiento de la marca, fortaleciendo su posicionamiento y desarrollando experiencias omnicanal que conecten con nuestros consumidores en cada punto de contacto.




¿Qué harás?




Liderar la estrategia anual de marca, identificando oportunidades de crecimiento y asegurando una ejecución alineada con los objetivos de negocio y la visión global de APIVITA.
Desarrollar e implementar campañas de marketing 360° que integren canales digitales, e-commerce, retail, trade marketing, relaciones públicas, influencers y actividades dirigidas a profesionales de la salud.
Liderar el lanzamiento de innovaciones y novedades, construyendo planes de activación que maximicen el impacto comercial y la relevancia de la marca.
Trabajar de manera cercana con equipos comerciales, trade marketing, medical marketing, e-commerce, retail excellence y stakeholders globales para garantizar una ejecución impecable.
Analizar tendencias de mercado, comportamiento del consumidor y desempeño del negocio para identificar oportunidades, generar insights y acelerar el crecimiento de la marca.
Construir experiencias de marca relevantes, aspiracionales y diferenciadoras, asegurando que cada iniciativa refleje los valores de APIVITA: naturaleza, bienestar, sostenibilidad y eficacia.
Gestionar inversiones y recursos de marketing con un enfoque orientado a resultados, rentabilidad y generación de valor para el negocio.




¿Qué buscamos?




Profesionales con experiencia en Brand Management, Marketing o Product Management con almenos 5 años de experiencia en el mundo de Derma.
Experiencia previa en dermocosmética, skincare, belleza, healthcare o categorías de consumo premium.
Perfil estratégico con capacidad para convertir insights en acciones y resultados.
Alta orientación al consumidor y sensibilidad para construir marcas relevantes.
Capacidad para liderar proyectos de manera transversal e influir en equipos multidisciplinarios.
Mentalidad emprendedora, curiosidad, creatividad y pasión por la innovación.
Inglés Intermedio - avanzado




¿Por qué APIVITA y Puig?




Porque tendrás la oportunidad de desarrollar una marca única, que combina naturaleza, ciencia y bienestar para crear experiencias auténticas y significativas. Formarás parte de una compañía global, emprendedora y en constante evolución, donde las ideas se convierten en proyectos, los proyectos en experiencias y las experiencias en crecimiento.




Si te apasiona construir marcas con propósito, desarrollar estrategias innovadoras y conectar con consumidores de una manera auténtica, queremos conocerte. 🌿✨💛




#WeArePuig #APIVITA #Marketing #BrandManagement #Dermocosmetics #BeautyThatMatters

title: Analyst, Strategy & Planning
skills: At TransUnion de México - Buró de Crédito, we foster a collaborative, dynamic, and innovative environment where we continuously explore new technologies and tools to remain agile. This enables our team to strengthen their skills, develop new capabilities, and reach their full potential.

Supports the LATAM regional business planning process, ongoing planning activities and LATAM strategy execution. Collaborate with the leadership team to facilitate and support planning sessions and business strategy execution. Plans and develop long-term goals and short-term objectives. Supports execution of regional business plans, key initiatives, M&A and partnership processes. Track competition and identifies threats or opportunities. Report progress and analyze issues that affect productivity, profitability, and growth for the region.
Come be a part of our team! You’ll work with great people, pioneering products and cutting-edge technology.
Bachelor’s degree in Industrial Engineering, Business or other related discipline.
Ideal candidate should have 1-2yrs of experience in a top-tier Consulting organization, Investment Banking or Private Equity.
Must have business planning experience along with strong facilitation skills.
Must be proficient with Excel, PowerPoint, and the Office Suite of applications. Think-Cell knowledge is a plus.
Must be 100% fluent in English and Spanish.
Ability to work independently or as part of a dynamic team in a rapidly changing environment.
Good project management capabilities and high attention to detail; comfortable working under deadlines and producing key deliverables.
Sound business judgment and excellent problem solving and influencing skills.
Proficient financial, analytical, organizational, and people skills.
Proven ability to quickly grasp industry and business-wide concepts to assess implications to drive actions and to capitalize on opportunities.
Team worker; excellent communication and presentation skills with experience in providing crisp, insightful information to executive teams. Ability to travel at least 10% of time.
Collaborate with LATAM region to create and maintain business plans that have clear and measurable targets. Develop senior-level presentations for short-term and long-range plans.
Support strategic planning process considering regional objectives.
Support the execution of the regional business plan and track and report current year progress.
Support all regional activity associated with new markets and new LOB development.
Develop financial projections to estimate market size, revenue projections, P&L projections, and investment requirements for new projects
Support M&A and partnership processes across the region.
Conduct industry research to understand market trends and competitive landscape and translate findings into presentations and memos for the leadership team
Execute ad hoc strategic analysis for leadership team. Gather competitive intelligence on an ongoing basis, monitor strategy, and predict competitive response for key competitors.
Build content to support a variety of Strategy and M&A process components and other high-visibility, high-stakes materials involving C-suite internal stakeholders.
Support leadership team in key initiatives across the organization: prepare presentations for leadership team, research, financial analysis, among others.
Develop ongoing reports: Macro, Board of Directors, results.
Support project management of different initiatives across the region.
Perform other related duties as assigned.

title: Advanced Agent Marketing Client Success Support Specialist
skills: Answer client questions, provide written support, and help resolve basic client issues.
Help clients with app-related questions, tool access, integrations, and process confusion.
Update client records, track availability, document issues, and maintain clean client data.
Make sure clients receive apps on time and understand how many apps remain in their order.
Track requests, communicate status internally, and gather context needed for review.
Ensure clients follow the appointment process and complete required Google Sheets after calls.
Help diffuse client frustration and escalate issues appropriately when needed.
Assist with client offboarding, documentation, and final communication steps.
description:
About the job

✨ IMPORTANT ✨

WATCH THIS QUICK LOOM VIDEO ON HOW TO GET HIRED:

https://www.loom.com/share/345f776084e642ba99c24aabe40a67a0

C1 ENGLISH LEVEL IS REQUIRED. THE SELECTED CANDIDATE WILL BE SPEAKING STRICTLY IN ENGLISH DURING THEIR ENTIRE SHIFT.

PLEASE SUBMIT YOUR RESUME AND AUDIO RECORDING IN ENGLISH




Advanced Agent Marketing Client Success Support Specialist

🆔 Job ID: TIMDEE2

🏢 Industry: Financial and Advisory Services

🌎 Location: LATAM

🕒 Job Status: Full Time

⏰ Work Schedule: The Client Success Support Specialist must be available during Eastern Standard Time business hours, especially during the hours when clients are actively taking apps, attending appointments, and submitting support requests. (9:00am to 5:00pm EST)

💰 Salary: This role pays approximately $10/hour, depending on experience and fit.

🚀 Target Start Date: asap




ROLE OVERVIEW




We are looking for a Client Success Support Specialist to support our Customer Success Managers with client communication, technical questions, CRM updates, app/order tracking, refund and credit-back requests, and basic conflict resolution.

This role is ideal for someone who is tech-savvy, highly organized, strong in written English, and comfortable helping clients work through process or technology-related questions. The right person will be able to communicate clearly, keep accurate records, follow up consistently, and make good decisions without needing step-by-step direction for every situation.




Key Responsibilities




The Client Success Support Specialist will assist the Customer Success team by answering client tech questions, helping clients understand apps and internal processes, and making sure client information is accurately updated in the CRM. This person will help monitor client availability, check integrations, track app/order status, and make sure clients understand how many apps they have remaining in their order.

This role will also help quality-check disposition calls, confirm that clients are following the correct appointment process, and make sure clients complete required Google Sheets or tracking forms after calls. The specialist will communicate with internal team members about apps that have been requested for credit-back, refund review, or additional follow-up.

In addition, this person will support offboarding, document client concerns, and help de-escalate frustrated clients in a professional manner. Common client situations may include refund requests, app credit-back requests, clients saying they were not looking for an annuity, did not want a specific state, had a poor call experience, did not want to join Zoom, or are already working with another advisor.




Core Responsibilities




Client Support

Answer client questions, provide written support, and help resolve basic client issues.

Technical Assistance

Help clients with app-related questions, tool access, integrations, and process confusion.

CRM Updates

Update client records, track availability, document issues, and maintain clean client data.

App & Order Tracking

Make sure clients receive apps on time and understand how many apps remain in their order.

Credit-Back & Refund Support

Track requests, communicate status internally, and gather context needed for review.

Process Follow-Up

Ensure clients follow the appointment process and complete required Google Sheets after calls.

Conflict Resolution

Help diffuse client frustration and escalate issues appropriately when needed.

Offboarding Support

Assist with client offboarding, documentation, and final communication steps.

Required Skills and Experience

The ideal candidate must be comfortable working across multiple systems and communicating quickly with both clients and internal team members. Experience with GoHighLevel/GHL and Slack is required. Strong written English is also important because this role involves client communication, internal updates, and accurate documentation.

We are not looking for someone who is simply “point and shoot.” This person needs to understand the situation, use good judgment, and decide when to resolve an issue directly versus when to escalate it to a Customer Success Manager or backend closer.

title: Onboarding Customer Success Specialist
skills: Liderar el proceso completo de onboarding para nuevos clientes, asegurando su integración satisfactoria y el dominio inicial de la plataforma.
Capacitar y guiar a los profesionales de la salud en el uso eficiente de nuestras herramientas digitales, adaptando la formación a sus necesidades específicas.
Identificar áreas de mejora a lo largo del proceso de incorporación y proponer soluciones innovadoras para optimizar la experiencia del usuario.
Monitorear el progreso de cada cliente durante la etapa inicial, anticipando posibles barreras y resolviéndolas de manera proactiva.
Colaborar estrechamente con los equipos de Producto y Soporte para canalizar feedback de los usuarios y contribuir a la mejora continua de nuestras soluciones.
Experiencia: De 1 a 2 años en Customer Success o Account Management (KAM), manejando carteras de alto volumen (100-150 clientes).
ADN Tech: Te mueves como pez en el agua en entornos Startup, Marketing o Publicidad.
Data-Driven: Sabes leer datos, analizar campañas de Google Ads y usarlos para contar una historia de éxito.
Tech Stack: Dominas CRM, Zoom, Slack y el ecosistema de Google Workspace.
Soft Skills: Tienes una mentalidad orientada a la resolución de problemas, la empatía y la resiliencia en la retención de clientes.
Inglés intermedio.
🏠 Esquema Híbrido: El equilibrio perfecto entre la energía de la oficina y la comodidad del home office.
🚀 Esquema de Comisiones: Plan de incentivos para recompensar tu capacidad de manejar cuentas.
🌴 Descanso Real: 22 días de vacaciones desde el año 1 + tu cumpleaños libre para celebrar.
🏥 Salud Primero: Seguro de Gastos Médicos Mayores para tu tranquilidad.
💰 Futuro y Beneficios: Fondo de ahorro y vales de despensa.
💪 Bienestar: Membresía en TotalPass & iFeel para tu bienestar físico y emocional.
🌍 Cultura Global: Proyección profesional en una empresa internacional con presencia en más de 13 países.
description:
About the job

Descripción de la empresa

En Doctoralia, nuestra misión es humanizar la experiencia de la salud a través de la tecnología. Como la plataforma líder en el mercado mexicano y referente global en soluciones SaaS para el sector médico, conectamos a millones de pacientes con especialistas, transformando la práctica clínica en una experiencia digital eficiente y centrada en el paciente.

Estamos buscando un Onboarding Customer Success Specialist apasionado, estratégico y con mentalidad de crecimiento para ser el aliado clave de nuestros especialistas.

Descripción del rol

Como Onboarding Customer Success Specialist, serás el aliado estratégico para los nuevos profesionales de la salud que se suman a nuestra plataforma. Tu misión será garantizar una experiencia de incorporación excepcional, promoviendo la confianza, el dominio de nuestras soluciones digitales y el éxito a largo plazo del especialista. Asegurarás que cada usuario inicie su relación con Doctoralia con bases sólidas y un acompañamiento cercano, contribuyendo a la transformación digital de su práctica profesional.

Responsabilidades clave


Liderar el proceso completo de onboarding para nuevos clientes, asegurando su integración satisfactoria y el dominio inicial de la plataforma.
Capacitar y guiar a los profesionales de la salud en el uso eficiente de nuestras herramientas digitales, adaptando la formación a sus necesidades específicas.
Identificar áreas de mejora a lo largo del proceso de incorporación y proponer soluciones innovadoras para optimizar la experiencia del usuario.
Monitorear el progreso de cada cliente durante la etapa inicial, anticipando posibles barreras y resolviéndolas de manera proactiva.
Colaborar estrechamente con los equipos de Producto y Soporte para canalizar feedback de los usuarios y contribuir a la mejora continua de nuestras soluciones.


🎯Perfil Profesional


Experiencia: De 1 a 2 años en Customer Success o Account Management (KAM), manejando carteras de alto volumen (100-150 clientes).
ADN Tech: Te mueves como pez en el agua en entornos Startup, Marketing o Publicidad.
Data-Driven: Sabes leer datos, analizar campañas de Google Ads y usarlos para contar una historia de éxito.
Tech Stack: Dominas CRM, Zoom, Slack y el ecosistema de Google Workspace.
Soft Skills: Tienes una mentalidad orientada a la resolución de problemas, la empatía y la resiliencia en la retención de clientes.
Inglés intermedio.


Propuesta de Valor (Compensación y Beneficios)

Ofrecemos un entorno de alto rendimiento con una estructura de beneficios competitiva:


🏠 Esquema Híbrido: El equilibrio perfecto entre la energía de la oficina y la comodidad del home office.
🚀 Esquema de Comisiones: Plan de incentivos para recompensar tu capacidad de manejar cuentas.
🌴 Descanso Real: 22 días de vacaciones desde el año 1 + tu cumpleaños libre para celebrar.
🏥 Salud Primero: Seguro de Gastos Médicos Mayores para tu tranquilidad.
💰 Futuro y Beneficios: Fondo de ahorro y vales de despensa.
💪 Bienestar: Membresía en TotalPass & iFeel para tu bienestar físico y emocional.
🌍 Cultura Global: Proyección profesional en una empresa internacional con presencia en más de 13 países.


¿Estás listo para transformar el sector salud en México? ¡Queremos conocerte!

description:
About the job

Who We Are

Solera is a global leader in data and software services that strives to transform every touchpoint of the vehicle lifecycle into a connected digital experience. In addition, we provide products and services to protect life’s other most important assets: our homes and digital identities. Today, Solera processes over 300 million digital transactions annually for approximately 235,000 partners and customers in more than 90 countries. Our 6,500 team members foster an uncommon, innovative culture and are dedicated to successfully bringing the future to bear today through cognitive answers, insights, algorithms and automation. For more information, please visit solera.com.

The Role

As an Inside Customer Success Rep you represent the face of the company and will primarily work with Large, Medium sized customers. You will build and maintain strong customer relationships with our customers while resolving customer issues. The Inside Customer Success Rep must be able to plan and execute daily and weekly, required activities. You must also be adept at multi-tasking, working within a team and other departments that drive successful business outcomes for the customer and for Solera-Omnitracs. In this role you must be comfortable using and/or learning new or other communication and automation tools. A great candidate does well with conflict resolution and can thrive in a fast-paced, change – heavy environment. The ideal candidate is goal and detail oriented, dedicated, hard-working, and maintains a positive attitude.

What You’ll Do


 Engage professionally with customers through multiple channels (inbound calls, outbound calls, email)
 Responsible for understanding and managing a subset of customer accounts
 Responsible for Customer Retention by fostering customer relationships and driving customer loyalty.
 Engage in active listening to promptly answer customer queries and concerns
 Work closely with the account manager to compose joint success plans with customers outlining their objectives, timelines and effectively removing any barriers to achieving business value
 Deeply understand the Omnitracs platform’s capabilities and explain them to businesses of all types – long haul transportation, last mite, ready-mix and more
 Manage customer cases to completion as escalated by the customer or internal Omnitracs teams
 Act as an escalation point for the Customer success front line of support
 Take impeccable notes and stay organized through the CRM to manage all accounts.
 Mitigate escalations and resolve customer issues.
 Guide users through navigating the company site or using products and services
 Be an advocate for the customer, be the voice of the customer
 Hold and lead recurrent customer business meetings
 Interact with other departments to resolve customer related matters
 Continued Development- Learn Industry News, ELD Mandates, Issues Related to Transportation and Updates to Omnitracs Products and Services.
 Ensure team goals, team KPIs, and deadlines are met
 Perform other duties and responsibilities as assigned


What You’ll Bring


 Experience working in a Customer Success environment
 Familiar with DOT/FMCSA/CSA Regulations a plus
 Results-oriented with strong problem- solving skills
 Exceptional customer facing customer skills, the ability to quickly understand customer needs
 Team-oriented and able to build and maintain strong inter-team and inter-departmental relationships
 Excellent verbal and written English communication skills
 Excellent presentation skills
 Excellent consultative skills
 Ability to adapt to and learn new applications and platforms
 Ability to work independently with minimal direction
 Ability to address and resolve customer issues independently
 Ability to prioritize projects and escalate issues to the appropriate parties as necessary
 Excellent organizational/multi-tasking and time management skills
 Intermediate to advance understanding of Salesforce, Five9 a plus
 Willingness to learn and use coaching feedback to improve.
 Strong Relationship (trust) Building skills
 French speaker a plus


Qualifications

EDUCATION: Bachelor’s degree or equivalent work experience and technical certifications

EXPERIENCE: 3+ years of experience in customer success, account management, sales, or consulting

It is impossible to list every requirement for, or responsibility of, any position. Similarly, we cannot identify all the skills a position may require since job responsibilities and the Company’s needs may change over time. Therefore, the above job description is not comprehensive or exhaustive. The Company reserves the right to adjust, add to or eliminate any aspect of the above description. The Company also retains the right to require all employees to undertake additional or different job responsibilities when necessary to meet business needs.

description:
About the job

Sobre nosotros

En Koltin, estamos rediseñando la forma en que cuidamos a nuestros papás, mamás, abuelos y abuelas, con el objetivo de ayudarles a mantenerse saludables, activos e independientes durante el mayor tiempo posible. Lo hacemos a través de nuestras membresías de salud, que integran:

Atención clínica enfocada en la prevención
Cobertura médica de gastos mayores
Programas de bienestar y comunidad

Somos la primera empresa en México que protege a personas adultas mayores, desde los 50 hasta los 84 años. Estamos creciendo rápidamente en todo el país, con un propósito claro: transformar la experiencia de salud para las Grandes Personas, haciéndola más humana, accesible y digna.




Sobre el Puesto

Koltin tiene miles de leads que ya conocen el producto, mostraron interés y no compraron. Ese backlog es el activo más eficiente del negocio — el costo de adquisición ya fue pagado. Hoy está subutilizado. Tu trabajo es convertirlo en ventas reales con un sistema data-driven.

Tu meta en septiembre: 860 leads del backlog convertidos en venta — el 56% del total de ventas de Koltin. El rol es 100% data-driven: cada decisión se toma con números. Si construís el sistema y lo hacés escalar, el rol crece con vos.




Responsabilidades

Diseñar la estrategia de reactivación del backlog: segmentación, scoring, mensajes y timing
Construir y mantener el modelo de conversión: analizar variables que predicen quién va a comprar y cuando
Definir las metas de conversión mensuales, medir el funnel end-to-end y reportar a la VP de Growth
Diseñar y optimizar flujos de CRM en HubSpot — automatizaciones, triggers, secuencias — con foco en D+30+

Trabajar con el equipo de ventas para asegurar que los leads que pasan estén listos para cerrar
Construir el playbook de lifecycle — documentado y automatizado — que escale sin depender de vos




Eres un buen fit si eres una persona que tiene

3+ años en CRM, lifecycle o growth con foco en conversión
Experiencia construyendo flujos de nurturing y automatizaciones desde cero
Manejo avanzado de HubSpot u otra herramienta de CRM
Sólido en análisis de datos: sabes leer un funnel, identificar dónde se cae la conversión y actuar sobre eso
SQL o Python para análisis propio — no dependes de un analista para cada pregunta
Experiencia con herramientas de BI (Metabase, Looker, Power BI o similar)
Conocimiento o curiosidad real en AI aplicada a CRM: scoring automático, AI SDR, generación de mensajes
Experiencia liderando equipos pequeños o coordinando colaboradores
Mentalidad de dueño: te importa el resultado, no la actividad

Crecimiento real: este rol tiene proyección clara hacia liderazgo de toda el área de Lifecycle

Sueldo competitivo + variable por meta de conversión




Cómo medimos tu éxito

About the job

Descripción del puesto En el puesto de Especialista en CRM y automatización en Centro SanaMente te encargarás de diseñar, configurar y optimizar flujos de trabajo automatizados para la gestión de personas usuarias y pacientes potenciales. En tu día a día segmentarás bases de datos, crearás campañas automatizadas (emails, recordatorios, seguimientos) y monitorizarás su rendimiento para proponer mejoras continuas. Colaborarás con los equipos de marketing y atención al paciente para definir procesos, mejorar la experiencia de la persona usuaria y aumentar la conversión y fidelización. También documentarás los procesos, elaborarás informes periódicos y ayudarás a implementar nuevas herramientas o integraciones relacionadas con el CRM. Este es un puesto de media jornada (part time) y se desarrolla en modalidad remota.




Lo que necesitamos:

Configuración y optimización de flujos en WATI (WhatsApp Business API)
Diseño e implementación de secuencias de email marketing (Mailchimp, ActiveCampaign o similar)
Criterio para adaptar los mensajes a una audiencia que viene a buscar apoyo psicológico — no cualquier tono sirve aquí
Aptitudes analíticas: capacidad para interpretar datos, extraer conclusiones accionables y proponer optimizaciones basadas en métricas y KPIs.
Gestión de relaciones con personas usuarias y ventas: experiencia en Customer Relationship Management (CRM) y en procesos de ventas orientados a la fidelización y al acompañamiento personalizado.
Comunicación y trabajo colaborativo: excelentes habilidades de comunicación escrita y verbal, así como capacidad para coordinarte con equipos multidisciplinares de forma remota.
Gestión de proyectos: aptitudes en Project Management para planificar, priorizar y hacer seguimiento de iniciativas de automatización y mejora de procesos.




Requisitos no negociables:

Basado/a en México
Facturación activa (RFC con actividad económica vigente) — indispensable para formalizar la colaboración

Lo que ofrecemos:

Colaboración recurrente part-time con pago por honorarios
Autonomía real en las decisiones técnicas dentro de tu área




Si cumples los requisitos y te interesa, escríbeme por mensaje directo incluyendo: tu tarifa para la configuración inicial de WATI y tu esquema de cobro para secuencias de email (por proyecto, por hora o como lo manejes). Eso nos permite avanzar rápido.

description:
About the job

Job Title: Sales Operations Specialist (CRM & Pipeline Operations)

Location: Remote (LATAM, Philippines, or South Africa Preferred)

Salary: Up to $1,500/month (Depending on experience)

Schedule: US Business Hours (EST/CST)




Independent Contractor Position




About the Role

We are hiring a Sales Operations Specialist to own CRM management, pipeline coordination, lead follow-up, and sales process execution for a U.S.-based sales team.




This is not a basic admin role. This position is built for someone who understands how sales pipelines move, knows how to keep deals from stalling, and can maintain operational visibility across the entire sales process.




You will manage CRM hygiene, coordinate follow-ups, support estimate-to-close workflows, and improve sales operations through organization, process discipline, and automation.

The ideal candidate is highly organized, proactive, comfortable communicating with U.S. clients, and experienced supporting fast-moving sales teams remotely.




What You’ll Own

Manage and maintain CRM pipelines across inbound, outbound, and quoted opportunities
Follow up on open estimates and inactive opportunities to improve conversion rates
Keep pipeline data accurate, updated, and fully visible for leadership
Coordinate directly with U.S.-based clients via email and phone regarding estimates and next steps
Prioritize daily sales activities based on urgency, deal stage, and lead quality
Build structure and consistency around sales follow-up workflows
Identify stale opportunities, missing information, and pipeline gaps proactively
Generate daily and weekly pipeline activity reports for leadership
Support workflow improvements and CRM process optimization




What We’re Looking For

2+ years of experience in Sales Operations, Sales Support, or CRM Coordination
Experience working with U.S.-based companies in client-facing or sales support roles
Strong CRM management experience and pipeline organization skills
Excellent written and verbal English communication
Strong follow-through and ability to manage multiple active opportunities simultaneously
Comfortable communicating directly with U.S. clients by phone and email
Highly organized, detail-oriented, and self-directed
Comfortable working independently in a remote environment
Reliable computer setup capable of supporting daily remote operations




Nice-to-Haves

Experience with CoreBridge CRM
Experience building CRM automations or workflow improvements
Background in signage, printing, or visual communications industries
Familiarity with Google Workspace, Slack, and project management tools

title: Lead, Advanced Analytics, Hosting Services (Brazil)
skills: Conduct advanced data analysis and statistical modeling to extract actionable insights that inform strategic decisions across key geographical markets and host segments.
Manage stakeholder relationships across the homes supply team, ensuring clarity of communication and strategic alignment on analytics initiatives.
Analyze broad data to understand the drivers of new listing supply, availability, revenue, churn and other key metrics related to host needs and engagement.
Define new metrics and identify leading indicators of key business outcomes.
Build dashboards and data pipelines to track key performance metrics across markets and host segments to establish benchmarks.

Develop and support agentic workflows and AI-assisted tools to automate recurring data analysis and reporting tasks, improving the speed and scale of insights delivery and enabling self-serve access to data across the homes supply team.
Conduct A/B tests to improve business performance and maximize incrementality.
