title: Client Service Specialist.
skills: Ownership of card issues – Completely own card related issues. The service specialists will be the face of HSBC/GPS for card issues and liaise with the different departments where required. Card specialists will ensure constant updates and holding responses as appropriate.

First Contact Resolution – Whilst there will be instances where card service specialists will have to coordinate with different teams, the endeavour will always be to drive first contact resolution, either through email and or where required via call/zoom.
Creation of synergies between card operations, card service managers, client account manager, and contact centre.
Compliment / support card service manager and client account manager – The card service specialist will be complimenting the CSS and CAMs for card related issues as appropriate.
Connectivity – The jobholder will where possible engage the client through zoom video calls and not just through audio / emails.
Basic Portuguese and English language is necesary - speaking and writing
Fairly good understanding of payments, and commercial cards.
A passion for serving clients and managing clients, front line client engagement experience
Ability to own issues, to be able to be in front of a client (via Video) and take ownership
High commitment to service excellence and client relationship management
Strong knowledge of back end operational process
Strong interpersonal, influencing and communication skills
Experience in customer service in a front-line role, including complex query handling.
description:
About the job

If you’re looking for a career where you can make a real impression, join Global Service Center (GSC) HSBC and discover how valued you’ll be.

HSBC is one of the largest banking and financial services organizations in the world, with operations in 64 countries and territories. We aim to be where the growth is, enabling businesses to thrive and economies to prosper, and, ultimately, helping people to fulfil their hopes and realize their ambitions.

Client Service Specialist.

The commercial card service specialist team is a ring-fenced team aimed at replicating the client experience provided by card service managers. Whilst these service managers will not be geographically located in the same country as the client, they will provide the client expected service by leveraging technology. These will be service oriented individuals with the right amount of card knowledge to be able to address the different complex card queries from clients. They will not have a client portfolio, however, will own the query that they pick up from the generic pooled mailbox.

The Jobholder Is Primarily Responsible For


Ownership of card issues – Completely own card related issues. The service specialists will be the face of HSBC/GPS for card issues and liaise with the different departments where required. Card specialists will ensure constant updates and holding responses as appropriate.
First Contact Resolution – Whilst there will be instances where card service specialists will have to coordinate with different teams, the endeavour will always be to drive first contact resolution, either through email and or where required via call/zoom.
Creation of synergies between card operations, card service managers, client account manager, and contact centre.
Compliment / support card service manager and client account manager – The card service specialist will be complimenting the CSS and CAMs for card related issues as appropriate.
Connectivity – The jobholder will where possible engage the client through zoom video calls and not just through audio / emails.


Experience


Basic Portuguese and English language is necesary - speaking and writing
Fairly good understanding of payments, and commercial cards.
A passion for serving clients and managing clients, front line client engagement experience
Ability to own issues, to be able to be in front of a client (via Video) and take ownership
High commitment to service excellence and client relationship management
Strong knowledge of back end operational process
Strong interpersonal, influencing and communication skills
Experience in customer service in a front-line role, including complex query handling.


Due to the urgent hiring need, candidates with immediate right to work locally and no relocation need will be prioritized.

At HSBC we offer our colleagues a greater number of days, so that they can fully enjoy their wedding, take care of the new member of the family, or grieve the loss of a family member. Our paid leave package is at the forefront in Mexico, now you have one more reason to be HSBC and proudly live a culture of well-being, balance and care.

HSBC is an equal opportunity employer committed to building a culture where all employees are valued, respected and opinions count. We take pride in providing a workplace that fosters continuous professional development, flexible working and, opportunities to grow within an inclusive and diverse environment. We encourage applications from all suitably qualified persons irrespective of, but not limited to, their gender or genetic information, sexual orientation, ethnicity, religion, social status, medical care leave requirements, political affiliation, people with disabilities, color, national origin, veteran status, etc., We consider all applications based on merit and suitability to the role.

Personal data held by the Bank relating to employment applications will be used in accordance with our Privacy Statement, which is available on our website.

***Issued By HSBC Electronic Data Process Mexico Private LTD***

title: Customer Service Contact
location: San Luis Potosí, San Luis Potosí, Mexico (On-site)
skills: Upon receiving of the customer schedules (via EDI, webEDI or paper), ensure that the Customer requirements are integrated into the system and any integration errors are dealt with and resolved. Loading closed orders onto the system.

Identify & highlight large order variations between the schedules that were forecasted by the Customer & those that are firmed up by the Customer, ensuring that the data has been accurately supplied from the Customer.
If necessary, release picking lists for the Customers’ deliveries.

Follow the complete Supply Chain (procurement, production, and shipping) and chase if necessary, ensure that targeted service levels to the Customer are reached. Monitor the safety stock levels and alert when the min / max levels are reached.
Manage co-operation and logistics issues with logistics external platforms
Ensure that the correct parameters are applied to each reference (MOQ, lead-time, transportation schedule), and if necessary, initiate corrective action plans.
Be aware of/integrated in P1 and P0 projects (new references, new packaging, transfers, new Customers…). Ensure that the returnable packaging loops are adhered to, preventing any packaging shortages.
Prepare the Customs documentation required for exporting if need be.

On a regular basis, communicate complete, precise & reliable information to the Customer contacts. Adapt the chosen method of communication to the level of the Customer contact. Adapt the frequency to the situation (preventative, curative, crisis).
Meet the Customer contacts on a regular basis (once a year for major Customers).
Ensure that the internal scheduling has been launched, taking into account the Customers’ priorities. Make or Ensure the production sequencing based on real customer demand
Measure the daily service rate for each Customer (using the Customers’ calculation and also the internal Customer Service Rate (CSR) calculation). Propose action plans to improve the service level.

Alert sales in the event of a crisis (Customer or internal), to define priorities with the Customer & internally (management, VS for the aftermarket), follow the evolution of any associated action plans. If necessary, escalate through the Logistics Red Alert application.

Make sure that there is a backup for each Customer & train the backup on the way to manage the Customers’ account; ensure that during holidays the position is covered & that both internal & external Customers are notified in advance of the absence and informed of the person that will be temporarily managing the Customers’ account.
one of the largest global innovative companies, with more than 20,000 engineers working in Research & Development
a multi-cultural environment that values diversity and international collaboration
more than 100,000 colleagues in 31 countries... which make a lot of opportunity for career growth

description:
About the job

Valeo est une entreprise mondiale de haute technologie qui conçoit des solutions révolutionnaires pour réinventer la mobilité. Nous sommes un équipementier partenaire des constructeurs automobiles et des acteurs de la nouvelle mobilité dans le monde entier. Notre vision ? Inventer une mobilité plus verte et plus sûre, grâce à des solutions axées sur la conduite intuitive et la réduction des émissions de CO2. Nous sommes leaders dans nos domaines d'activité et reconnus comme l'une des plus grandes entreprises innovantes au monde.

Recognize and manage all demands for products from customers by driving Sales and operation plan and Master production Schedules~ 


Upon receiving of the customer schedules (via EDI, webEDI or paper), ensure that the Customer requirements are integrated into the system and any integration errors are dealt with and resolved. Loading closed orders onto the system. 
Identify & highlight large order variations between the schedules that were forecasted by the Customer & those that are firmed up by the Customer, ensuring that the data has been accurately supplied from the Customer. 
If necessary, release picking lists for the Customers’ deliveries. 
Follow the complete Supply Chain (procurement, production, and shipping) and chase if necessary, ensure that targeted service levels to the Customer are reached. Monitor the safety stock levels and alert when the min / max levels are reached. 
Manage co-operation and logistics issues with logistics external platforms 
Ensure that the correct parameters are applied to each reference (MOQ, lead-time, transportation schedule), and if necessary, initiate corrective action plans. 
Be aware of/integrated in P1 and P0 projects (new references, new packaging, transfers, new Customers…). Ensure that the returnable packaging loops are adhered to, preventing any packaging shortages. 
Prepare the Customs documentation required for exporting if need be. 
On a regular basis, communicate complete, precise & reliable information to the Customer contacts. Adapt the chosen method of communication to the level of the Customer contact. Adapt the frequency to the situation (preventative, curative, crisis). 
Meet the Customer contacts on a regular basis (once a year for major Customers). 


Make sure from the complete customer demand (date & quantity), that this demand will be met~ Ensure the feasibility of the Customers’ programs internally (MPS Manager (weekly), Procurement and Scheduler (daily)) and if necessary, negotiate the planned delivery dates upstream with the Customer. 


Ensure that the internal scheduling has been launched, taking into account the Customers’ priorities. Make or Ensure the production sequencing based on real customer demand 
Measure the daily service rate for each Customer (using the Customers’ calculation and also the internal Customer Service Rate (CSR) calculation). Propose action plans to improve the service level. 


Identify the blocking points and plan appropriate quick actions to solve them~ 


Alert sales in the event of a crisis (Customer or internal), to define priorities with the Customer & internally (management, VS for the aftermarket), follow the evolution of any associated action plans. If necessary, escalate through the Logistics Red Alert application. 
Make sure that there is a backup for each Customer & train the backup on the way to manage the Customers’ account; ensure that during holidays the position is covered & that both internal & external Customers are notified in advance of the absence and informed of the person that will be temporarily managing the Customers’ account.

description:
About the job

Syntax is a leading Managed Cloud Provider for Mission Critical Enterprise Applications and has been providing comprehensive technology solutions to businesses of all sizes since 1972. Syntax has undisputed strength to implement and manage ERP deployments (Oracle, SAP) in a secure and resilient private, public or hybrid cloud. With strong technical and functional consulting services, and world-class monitoring and automation, Syntax serves some of North America’s largest corporations across a diverse range of industries. Syntax has offices worldwide, and partners with Oracle, SAP, AWS, Microsoft, IBM and other global technology leaders.

Customer Support Specialist

Position Summary

The Customer Support Specialist is responsible for providing exceptional support to customers by addressing inquiries, resolving issues, and ensuring a positive customer experience. This role serves as a primary point of contact for customers and works closely with internal teams to deliver timely and effective solutions.

Key Responsibilities


Respond to customer inquiries via phone, email, chat, and support ticketing systems.
Troubleshoot and resolve customer issues in a timely and professional manner.
Document customer interactions, issues, and resolutions accurately.
Escalate complex issues to appropriate internal teams when necessary.
Monitor and manage support tickets to ensure service level agreements (SLAs) are met.
Educate customers on products, services, features, and best practices.
Identify recurring issues and provide feedback to improve products, services, and processes.
Maintain a high level of customer satisfaction and professionalism in all interactions.
Collaborate with cross-functional teams including Sales, Operations, Product, and Technical Support.
Contribute to the development and maintenance of knowledge base articles and support documentation.


Required Qualifications


Bachelor's degree or equivalent work experience.
1–3 years of experience in Customer Service, Customer Support, Help Desk, or a related role.
Strong verbal and written communication skills.
Excellent problem-solving and troubleshooting abilities.
Ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment.
Strong attention to detail and organizational skills.
Proficiency with Microsoft Office and CRM/ticketing platforms.


Preferred Qualifications


Experience with Salesforce, Zendesk, ServiceNow, Freshdesk, or similar support tools.

description:

We are looking for a proactive and results-driven Customer Project Manager to oversee the successful delivery of customer-focused projects within a dynamic technology environment. This role is responsible for coordinating project activities from initiation through completion, ensuring that customer expectations, business objectives, and delivery commitments are consistently achieved.

The successful candidate will have proven experience managing multiple projects simultaneously, building strong relationships with stakeholders, and driving projects to completion while maintaining quality, timelines, and commercial objectives.

title: Junior Media Buyer for a US Performance Marketing Agency (Remote)
skills: Building, launching, and managing Meta campaigns (CBO/ABO structures, audiences, placements, creative testing).
Supporting and growing into independent buying on AppLovin / Axon.
Monitoring daily performance and flagging scaling opportunities, underperformers, and anomalies.
Running structured creative and audience tests, and documenting the results.
Pulling, organizing, and interpreting performance data to inform decisions, and producing clear weekly client reports.
Analyzing creative performance using soft metrics (CTR, CPM, CPC), benchmarking against account averages, and clearly communicating those reads to strategists and clients.
Helping maintain clean account hygiene, naming conventions, and tracking.
0-2 years of hands-on paid media experience (internships, freelance, or running your own / your brand's ads all count).
Clear written communication in English.
Genuine fluency with Meta Ads Manager — you've built campaigns, not just read about them.
Strong numerical sense; comfortable living in spreadsheets and dashboards.
A direct-response mindset: you care about CPA, ROAS, and contribution margin, not vanity metrics.
Familiarity with DTC e-commerce, Shopify, and the fashion/beauty/lifestyle space.
Working knowledge of tracking and attribution tools (pixels, CAPI, ClickFlare, GA4, etc.).
Comfortable staying responsive when accounts demand it, including the occasional evening or weekend during launches or scaling pushes.
Availability during key EST windows to analyze performance and take action (scale, kill, pull back, etc.).
Experience with AdManage (strongly preferred).
Hands-on time with AppLovin / Axon or other programmatic / native platforms.
Basic understanding of creative.
Competitive base salary.
Fully remote, full-time contractor role.
Learn direct-response media buying directly from senior operators managing significant spend.
Real ownership early—you won't be stuck pulling reports for a year.
Work across a diverse roster of scaling, fast-growing DTC brands in fashion, beauty, and lifestyle.
A fast-paced team environment that rewards results and fosters growth into a senior media buyer role.
description:
About the job

Paired is a global staffing and recruiting agency that specializes in pairing remote work with top-tier talent!

We are dedicated to connecting talented individuals with remarkable companies in need of their skills. Our goal is to create fulfilling job opportunities for professionals around the world.

Our client:



Looking for a Junior Media Buyer who wants to learn the performance marketing craft properly. This is a hands-in-the-account role from day one for someone obsessed with what makes an ad account scale profitably, not just spend. You will work hand-in-hand with senior buyers to plan, launch, and optimize paid campaigns primarily on Meta, with growing responsibility on AppLovin / Axon.

You don't need years of experience, but you do need sharp instincts, comfort with numbers, and the drive to get great at this fast. You'll be building campaigns, reading data, spotting what's working, and learning to make confident scale/kill decisions across a portfolio of fast-growing DTC e-commerce brands.

This is a high-performance environment built for hungry, ambitious people who take real ownership of their accounts and treat client budgets like their own.

You'll be responsible for


Building, launching, and managing Meta campaigns (CBO/ABO structures, audiences, placements, creative testing).
Supporting and growing into independent buying on AppLovin / Axon.
Monitoring daily performance and flagging scaling opportunities, underperformers, and anomalies.
Running structured creative and audience tests, and documenting the results.
Pulling, organizing, and interpreting performance data to inform decisions, and producing clear weekly client reports.
Analyzing creative performance using soft metrics (CTR, CPM, CPC), benchmarking against account averages, and clearly communicating those reads to strategists and clients.
Helping maintain clean account hygiene, naming conventions, and tracking.


Requirements


0-2 years of hands-on paid media experience (internships, freelance, or running your own / your brand's ads all count).
Clear written communication in English.
Genuine fluency with Meta Ads Manager — you've built campaigns, not just read about them.
Strong numerical sense; comfortable living in spreadsheets and dashboards.
A direct-response mindset: you care about CPA, ROAS, and contribution margin, not vanity metrics.
Familiarity with DTC e-commerce, Shopify, and the fashion/beauty/lifestyle space.
Working knowledge of tracking and attribution tools (pixels, CAPI, ClickFlare, GA4, etc.).
Comfortable staying responsive when accounts demand it, including the occasional evening or weekend during launches or scaling pushes.
Availability during key EST windows to analyze performance and take action (scale, kill, pull back, etc.).
Nice to have


Experience with AdManage (strongly preferred).
Hands-on time with AppLovin / Axon or other programmatic / native platforms.
Basic understanding of creative.


Benefits


Competitive base salary.
Fully remote, full-time contractor role.
Learn direct-response media buying directly from senior operators managing significant spend.
Real ownership early—you won't be stuck pulling reports for a year.
Work across a diverse roster of scaling, fast-growing DTC brands in fashion, beauty, and lifestyle.
A fast-paced team environment that rewards results and fosters growth into a senior media buyer role.

title: Senior Commercial Market Specialist (m/f/d)
skills: Monitor and analyze regional performance, competitor dynamics, and demand patterns for the North America market, turning data into strategic recommendations.
Own fare management for the region: define regional pricing settings, fallback logic, and verify implementation across the network.
Support the design and execution of commercial activities — promotions, marketing campaigns, A/B tests, and sales initiatives — aligned with regional strategy.
Build and maintain a country-specific campaign calendar (email, social media, and other channels).
Develop and manage briefings for Marketing & Sales channels, including budget tracking and bi-weekly syncs.
Identify revenue and customer flow opportunities: social discounts, special products, customer segments, and willingness-to-pay insights.
Stay ahead of market trends: track the competitive landscape, macroeconomic shifts, regulatory changes, and network dynamics across the region.
Contribute to topline forecasts (weekly and monthly updates) and propose data-driven improvements.
Collect North America-specific e-commerce needs, build business cases, and present recommendations to leadership.
Lead or contribute to cross-functional projects, acting as subject matter expert or project manager on specific commercial topics.
Leverage AI tools and automation to streamline processes and improve commercial efficiency.
Bachelor's degree in Economics, Finance, Mathematics, Business, or a related field.
2–4 years of experience in commercial analysis, revenue management, or business development — ideally in transportation, airlines, hospitality, beverages, or a similarly dynamic industry.
Strong analytical skills with the ability to go beyond the numbers: you design solutions, not just reports.
A natural connector who actively seeks input from stakeholders, builds relationships across teams, and is not afraid to knock on doors to get things done.
Strategic thinker who can navigate fast-changing macro environments (cost shifts, demand fluctuations, competitive moves) with adaptability and clarity.
Proactive and ownership-driven: you set your own goals, follow through on commitments, and hold yourself accountable to financial targets and project deliverables.
Curious about markets — you want to understand North America's geography, network, and customer dynamics, not just analyze spreadsheets.
Collaborative and a strong team player, with excellent communication and presentation skills for senior stakeholders.
Comfortable with ambiguity and freedom; you do not need constant guidance to deliver results.
Fluent in English (additional languages are a plus).
Interest or experience in AI-powered tools and process automation is a strong advantage.
Travel perks: 12 free Flix vouchers + 12 discount vouchers for friends & family.
Work from (M)Anywhere: Depending on your role, work from another location for up to 60 days per year.
Hybrid work model: We are an office-first company, but we offer flexibility to balance work and life.
Wellbeing support: Access confidential 1:1 counselling, courses, and stress management for yourself and up to four family members.
Learning & Development: Take advantage of language classes, training courses, and expert-led sessions to grow your skills.
Mentoring Program: Connect with experienced colleagues to gain insights and accelerate your career.
description:
About the job

At Flix, we offer a dynamic work environment with competitive pay, strong growth opportunities, and a tech-driven approach to making travel more accessible, sustainable, and affordable

We are looking for a Senior Commercial Market Specialist (m/f/d) to join our North America commercial team. This is not a purely analytical role — we need someone who combines strong analytical thinking with the ability to design and implement solutions, build bridges across stakeholders, and take ownership of results. You will interact directly with senior leadership, including the CEO for North America, and shape the commercial strategy of one of the world's fastest-growing mobility networks.

About The Role


Monitor and analyze regional performance, competitor dynamics, and demand patterns for the North America market, turning data into strategic recommendations.
Own fare management for the region: define regional pricing settings, fallback logic, and verify implementation across the network.
Support the design and execution of commercial activities — promotions, marketing campaigns, A/B tests, and sales initiatives — aligned with regional strategy.
Build and maintain a country-specific campaign calendar (email, social media, and other channels).
Develop and manage briefings for Marketing & Sales channels, including budget tracking and bi-weekly syncs.
Identify revenue and customer flow opportunities: social discounts, special products, customer segments, and willingness-to-pay insights.
Stay ahead of market trends: track the competitive landscape, macroeconomic shifts, regulatory changes, and network dynamics across the region.
Contribute to topline forecasts (weekly and monthly updates) and propose data-driven improvements.
Collect North America-specific e-commerce needs, build business cases, and present recommendations to leadership.
Lead or contribute to cross-functional projects, acting as subject matter expert or project manager on specific commercial topics.
Leverage AI tools and automation to streamline processes and improve commercial efficiency.


About You


Bachelor's degree in Economics, Finance, Mathematics, Business, or a related field.
2–4 years of experience in commercial analysis, revenue management, or business development — ideally in transportation, airlines, hospitality, beverages, or a similarly dynamic industry.
Strong analytical skills with the ability to go beyond the numbers: you design solutions, not just reports.
A natural connector who actively seeks input from stakeholders, builds relationships across teams, and is not afraid to knock on doors to get things done.
Strategic thinker who can navigate fast-changing macro environments (cost shifts, demand fluctuations, competitive moves) with adaptability and clarity.
Proactive and ownership-driven: you set your own goals, follow through on commitments, and hold yourself accountable to financial targets and project deliverables.
Curious about markets — you want to understand North America's geography, network, and customer dynamics, not just analyze spreadsheets.
Collaborative and a strong team player, with excellent communication and presentation skills for senior stakeholders.
Comfortable with ambiguity and freedom; you do not need constant guidance to deliver results.
Fluent in English (additional languages are a plus).
Interest or experience in AI-powered tools and process automation is a strong advantage.


We recognize that everyone carries a unique set of valuable skills and experiences. If you think you could have an impact even though you don't meet 100% of the requirements, we still encourage you to apply. We want to hear from you! 

What We Offer


Travel perks: 12 free Flix vouchers + 12 discount vouchers for friends & family. 
Work from (M)Anywhere: Depending on your role, work from another location for up to 60 days per year. 
Hybrid work model: We are an office-first company, but we offer flexibility to balance work and life. 
Wellbeing support: Access confidential 1:1 counselling, courses, and stress management for yourself and up to four family members. 
Learning & Development: Take advantage of language classes, training courses, and expert-led sessions to grow your skills. 
Mentoring Program: Connect with experienced colleagues to gain insights and accelerate your career. 


To view more local benefits specific to each office location, please check out this link: Locations - Flix Career

Why Join Flix?

At Flix, you’ll find teams that rally together to overcome challenges and spark creativity. We believe in ownership culture - giving you the freedom to take initiative, make an impact, and shape your own career path.

As we continue to expand across the globe, you can make a real difference in how we work.

If you’re ready to grow and lead your journey, Flix is the place for you!

description:
About the job

Launch Your Next Successful Chapter with Veta Virtual! 🚀




💸 Competitive Salary in USD | 🌍 100% Remote | 🎯 Paid Search & Social Role | 🏆 Award-Winning Agency Culture




Veta Virtual is hiring a Paid Search & Social Specialist for a high-growth client in the Digital Marketing industry — an award-winning agency specializing in the automotive vertical, recognized as Best Large SEO Agency and winner of Best Low Budget PPC Campaign at the 2024 US Search Awards. This is a learning-forward role for someone curious, resourceful, and ready to run real campaigns that push what is possible.




🔑 Your Role:




You will manage and optimize paid search and social campaigns for automotive dealership clients — trained and mentored by top specialists while taking real ownership of results. Your day-to-day will involve:




Managing and optimizing Google Ads and Meta Ads campaigns — delivering higher ROI and lower cost per conversion for automotive dealership clients.
Conducting daily account audits, budget pacing, and campaign adjustments to keep performance on track and spend optimized.
Generating performance reports from Google Ads and Meta — and conducting client calls to present strategies and results clearly and confidently.
Managing OEM reimbursement processes — reviewing requirements, gathering materials, and submitting claims accurately and on time.
Using Google Analytics, Data Studio, Tag Manager, Merchant Center, and Meta Ads Manager to analyze data and drive campaign improvements.
Collaborating with SEOs, writers, and designers on new experiments and client work — contributing to a team that tests, learns, and pushes boundaries daily.




✅ What You'll Need:




1+ year of hands-on Google Ads experience — able to build, manage, and optimize paid search campaigns confidently.
1+ year of Meta Ads experience — proficient in Facebook and Instagram campaign setup, targeting, and optimization inside Ads Manager.
Working knowledge of Google Analytics, Google Data Studio, Google Ads Editor, Google Tag Manager, and Google Merchant Center.
Prior agency experience — you understand client management, reporting, and the pace of agency work.
Detail-oriented, research-driven, and a strong communicator in written and spoken English.
Bonus: automotive vertical experience, OEM reimbursement knowledge, or campaigns you can show us with real results.
To be considered for this role, you must be currently based in Latin America (LATAM) or Europe.




💼 What We Offer:




💸 Competitive salary in USD — paid consistently every month.
🌴 10 paid vacation days per year plus all U.S. federal holidays.
💻 100% remote — open to candidates based in LATAM or Europe.
📈 Direct mentorship from top paid media specialists — real room to grow and own your campaigns inside a fast-moving agency.
🏆 A high-performance, collaborative culture where testing, learning, and pushing boundaries are part of daily life.
🤝 A supportive environment through Veta Virtual and our client where curiosity is rewarded and your growth is taken seriously from day one.




Ready to Apply?




If you are ready to run real campaigns, learn from the best, and grow inside one of the most innovative digital marketing agencies in the automotive space — apply now and launch your next chapter with Veta Virtual!




Once you apply, you will complete a short 2-minute video question — come ready to tell us about campaigns you have run and results you have driven. We can't wait to meet you!

title: Content & SEO Specialist
skills: Eligibility: This position is open to candidates residing in Latin America.
Application Language: Please submit your CV in English. Applications submitted in other languages will not be considered.

Professional Presentation: We encourage you to showcase your professional experience by including a Loom video in the application form. While this is optional, candidates who provide a video presentation will be given priority.
Interview Policy: The use of artificial intelligence (AI) tools during interviews are strictly prohibited.

Note: As part of the final stages of our hiring process, candidates may be asked to provide professional references for a reference check. If hired, you may also be asked to update your LinkedIn profile :)
AI-Assisted Blog Content → CMS Publishing
Use AI tools (ChatGPT, Claude, or similar) to generate first drafts from content briefs and keywords
Rewrite and refine AI output so it reads natural, human, and brand-consistent
Fact-check, improve clarity, and ensure editorial quality (no “AI-sounding” content)
Publish 2–4 blog posts per week into a CMS (proper formatting: H1/H2/H3, meta title, meta description, slug)
Source and integrate relevant visuals for every post (hero images, diagrams, screenshots where relevant)
Ensure images are contextually relevant, correctly sized, and include alt text
Add internal links following the hub-and-spoke structure
Implement FAQ schema markup where applicable (training provided)
Maintain tracking in the content calendar (status, publish date, URL, performance notes)
Weekly Newsletter
Draft one newsletter per week in an email platform (e.g., Klaviyo or equivalent)
Curate 2–3 external insights (industry news, posts, tools, studies)
Write concise commentary and framing around each item
Include links to newly published content
Maintain a clear, non-promotional tone focused on value and relevance
Schedule, test, and send each issue
Monitor open and click-through rates weekly and flag performance trends
LinkedIn Content (1–2 Posts Weekly)
Write 1–2 posts per week for a company LinkedIn page
Avoid generic “corporate thought leadership” tone — keep it specific, practical, and grounded
Pull insights from blog content, product use cases, customer conversations, or sales insights
Create or source supporting visuals (screenshots, simple graphics, text-based visuals)
Schedule posts using a social scheduling tool or native platform
Monitor engagement and flag important comments or mentions for response
Template Pages & Gated Content
Write landing page copy for template downloads (based on provided briefs)
Format templates into usable assets (PDF, Slides, or Docs)
Publish gated landing pages in CMS with email capture integration
Ensure proper metadata, internal linking, and conversion flow
Maintain consistency between page copy and downloadable asset
Strong experience using AI writing tools effectively (knows the difference between edited output and raw AI content)
Ability to write clearly, directly, and without corporate or filler language
Experience publishing content in a CMS environment
Strong organizational skills and ability to manage workload independently
Ability to source relevant, context-aware visuals
Comfortable receiving editorial feedback and iterating quickly
Experience with CMS platforms (e.g., Webflow or similar)
Email marketing experience
Basic SEO understanding (metadata, structure, internal linking)
Experience writing for B2B audiences (sales, revenue, or SaaS context)
Familiarity with CRM ecosystems
Experience with LinkedIn content creation and distribution
description:
About the job

Hi there! We are South and our client is looking for a Content & SEO Specialist!

Note To Applicants


Eligibility: This position is open to candidates residing in Latin America. 
Application Language: Please submit your CV in English. Applications submitted in other languages will not be considered. 
Professional Presentation: We encourage you to showcase your professional experience by including a Loom video in the application form. While this is optional, candidates who provide a video presentation will be given priority. 
Interview Policy: The use of artificial intelligence (AI) tools during interviews are strictly prohibited. 
Note: As part of the final stages of our hiring process, candidates may be asked to provide professional references for a reference check. If hired, you may also be asked to update your LinkedIn profile :)


The Core Job (Non-Negotiable)

These Four Areas Define Success In This Role


 AI-Assisted Blog Content → CMS Publishing


You will execute a structured content strategy that includes keyword research, content briefs, and a hub-and-spoke architecture already in place.

Your responsibility is to turn briefs into high-quality, published content.


Use AI tools (ChatGPT, Claude, or similar) to generate first drafts from content briefs and keywords
Rewrite and refine AI output so it reads natural, human, and brand-consistent
Fact-check, improve clarity, and ensure editorial quality (no “AI-sounding” content)
Publish 2–4 blog posts per week into a CMS (proper formatting: H1/H2/H3, meta title, meta description, slug)
Source and integrate relevant visuals for every post (hero images, diagrams, screenshots where relevant)
Ensure images are contextually relevant, correctly sized, and include alt text
Add internal links following the hub-and-spoke structure
Implement FAQ schema markup where applicable (training provided)
Maintain tracking in the content calendar (status, publish date, URL, performance notes)
 Weekly Newsletter


Own a weekly email newsletter sent to a B2B audience (sales and revenue professionals).


Draft one newsletter per week in an email platform (e.g., Klaviyo or equivalent)
Curate 2–3 external insights (industry news, posts, tools, studies)
Write concise commentary and framing around each item
Include links to newly published content
Maintain a clear, non-promotional tone focused on value and relevance
Schedule, test, and send each issue
Monitor open and click-through rates weekly and flag performance trends
 LinkedIn Content (1–2 Posts Weekly)


Manage consistent organic presence on LinkedIn.


Write 1–2 posts per week for a company LinkedIn page
Avoid generic “corporate thought leadership” tone — keep it specific, practical, and grounded
Pull insights from blog content, product use cases, customer conversations, or sales insights
Create or source supporting visuals (screenshots, simple graphics, text-based visuals)
Schedule posts using a social scheduling tool or native platform
Monitor engagement and flag important comments or mentions for response
 Template Pages & Gated Content


Build and publish downloadable, conversion-focused content assets.


Write landing page copy for template downloads (based on provided briefs)
Format templates into usable assets (PDF, Slides, or Docs)
Publish gated landing pages in CMS with email capture integration
Ensure proper metadata, internal linking, and conversion flow
Maintain consistency between page copy and downloadable asset


Who This Role Is For

This role is for someone who is highly proficient in AI-assisted content production and can maintain quality at volume.

Required Skills


Strong experience using AI writing tools effectively (knows the difference between edited output and raw AI content)
Ability to write clearly, directly, and without corporate or filler language
Experience publishing content in a CMS environment
Strong organizational skills and ability to manage workload independently
Ability to source relevant, context-aware visuals
Comfortable receiving editorial feedback and iterating quickly


Strong Plus


Experience with CMS platforms (e.g., Webflow or similar)
Email marketing experience
Basic SEO understanding (metadata, structure, internal linking)
Experience writing for B2B audiences (sales, revenue, or SaaS context)
Familiarity with CRM ecosystems
Experience with LinkedIn content creation and distribution


Schedule: Monday to Friday, 9 AM – 6 PM ET

Compensation: USD salary

Location: 100% remote opportunity

If this opportunity sounds good to you, send us your resume!

description:
About the job

About Puig




At Puig, we are a family-owned company with a long-term commitment to creativity, innovation, and people development. Through our Graduate Program, we are looking for exceptional young talent ready to begin their professional journey in a dynamic, international, and purpose-driven environment.




This is a unique opportunity to accelerate your career, gain exposure to a leading global organization, and contribute to the growth of prestigious dermatological brands that improve people's quality of life through science-backed skincare solutions.




Your Mission




As a Marketing Graduate (Entry Level) within our Derma Division, you will support the Marketing team in the development and execution of brand strategies, product launches, and commercial initiatives while gaining hands-on experience across multiple areas of the business.




You will work closely with cross-functional teams including Medical Affairs, Sales, Trade Marketing, Digital, and Finance, contributing to projects that have a direct impact on business performance and patient outcomes.




What You'll Do




• Support the planning, execution, and optimization of digital marketing campaigns across social media platforms, with a strong focus on TikTok and emerging digital channels.

• Assist in the creation, coordination, and publishing of engaging content aligned with brand strategies, consumer trends, and business objectives.

• Collaborate closely with the Digital & Marketing team to manage community engagement, monitor social conversations, and identify opportunities to increase brand relevance and reach.

• Support influencer and content creator initiatives, including campaign coordination, performance tracking, and reporting.

• Monitor digital KPIs and campaign performance, providing insights and recommendations to improve engagement, visibility, and business impact.

• Contribute to the development and management of a dedicated social media project aimed at healthcare professionals, helping create educational and scientifically relevant content for derma brands.

• Coordinate with Medical Visit and cross-functional teams to ensure content accuracy, compliance, and alignment with brand objectives.

• Assist in the preparation of presentations, reports, and business reviews related to digital performance, marketing initiatives, and key projects.

• Participate in local and global marketing projects, gaining exposure to brand management, consumer engagement, and omnichannel strategies.

• Provide support on ad hoc marketing initiatives, product launches, events, and special projects across the Derma division.




Who You Are

Recent graduate (Bachelor's degree completed within the last 1–2 years) in Marketing, Business Administration, Communication, Pharmaceutical Business, or related fields.
Previous internship, trainee, student leadership, or extracurricular experience is highly valued.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
Excellent communication and presentation abilities.
Curious, proactive, and eager to learn.
Collaborative mindset and ability to work in diverse teams.
Passion for marketing, healthcare, skincare, and consumer behavior.
Comfortable working in a fast-paced and dynamic environment.




Requirements

Bachelor's degree completed by the start date.
Intermediate English proficiency (written and spoken).
Advanced knowledge of Microsoft Office (Excel and PowerPoint preferred).
Availability to work full-time.
Authorization to work in Mexico.




What We Offer

Participation in a global Graduate Program designed to accelerate your development.
Exposure to senior leaders and international projects.
Structured learning and development opportunities.
Mentorship and career guidance.
A collaborative, inclusive, and innovative culture.
Opportunity to build a long-term career within Puig.




Our Commitment

At Puig, we believe that diversity, equity, and inclusion drive innovation and creativity. We are committed to creating an environment where everyone can thrive, contribute, and grow.

Join us and help shape the future of dermatological skincare through science, innovation, and purpose. ✨

We create world-class content, which we distribute across our portfolio of film, television, and streaming, and bring to life through our global theme park destinations, consumer products, and experiences. Minh Vo - Founder
description:
About the job

I’m helping Email Clout find a top candidate to join their team flexible for the role of Email Marketing Account Manager.




You'll drive revenue and client success by independently managing end-to-end email marketing campaigns.




Compensation:

USD 300 - 1.5K/month




Location:

Remote: Mexico




Mission of Email Clout:

"Empower online brands with high-impact, revenue-driving email campaigns. Fast, accountable, and world-class results."




What makes you a strong candidate:

You are proficient in Project management, Marketing strategy, Email marketing, Data analytics, Copywriting.
You have the potential to develop in Slack, Shopify, Notion, Figma.
English - Fully fluent




Responsibilities and more:

Email Clout is an email marketing agency that partners with online brands to create unique, engaging, and revenue-generating email campaigns and flows. We’re fully remote, small, and love to move fast. We hold ourselves accountable for delivering a world-class client experience and industry-shattering email performance. 




We are looking for the ideal candidate for the role of Email Marketing Account Manager, who will be responsible for the experience and performance of client accounts. In this role, you will manage and execute email marketing campaigns and flows from start to finish. This includes coming up with ideas, planning, writing copy, communicating with clients and internal teams, as well as implementation in Klaviyo. You will use Klaviyo and Figma while managing your tasks independently through Notion. Below are the key responsibilities for this role. 




- Create and manage multiple email content calendars. 

- Plan, write, and schedule email marketing campaigns and flows. 

- Collect, report, present, and analyze past data to make the best decisions for clients. 

- Conduct and lead client calls. 

- Handle difficult questions related to email performance. 

- Make design and copy changes in Figma. 

- Collaborate and manage designers and copywriters. 

- Stay up-to-date with industry trends and best practices in email marketing. 




Software stack you’ll be using: 

- Slack 

- Klaviyo 

- Figma 

- Shopify 

- Notion 




Requirements: 

- 1-2 years of experience in email marketing, preferably with experience using Klaviyo. 

- Proficiency in Figma or similar design software. 

- Excellent written and verbal communication skills. 

- Strong attention to detail and multitasking ability. 

- Familiarity with email marketing best practices and metrics. 

- Confident in communicating with clients. 

- Perfect English (spoken and written). 




Do Not Apply If:

About the Role

We are looking for fluent language professionals to help train and evaluate AI language models. This is a flexible, part-time contract role you can do entirely from home, on your own schedule. If you work with language professionally - as a writer, teacher, translator, tutor, linguist, or language specialist - this role is a strong fit.




What You'll Do

Read and evaluate AI-generated text for quality, accuracy, and fluency

Up to $7.50 USD/hr, paid weekly

Flexible hours - you set your own schedule
Part-time contract - ideal as a primary or supplemental income source




Equal Opportunity Employer:

title: Creative Technology Senior Associate
skills: Support ad technology, tech integration tests, dynamic [DCO], and omni-channel campaign production efforts across multiple brand teams, geographical locations and products (including new business and new agency tech initiatives)
Contribute to large scale programmatic, social and video campaigns across multiple formats and channels employing underlying code bases such as HTML/JS/CSS, and industry leading ad technology.

Support in the auditing and defining criteria for all functional and non-functional deliverable requirements including dynamic ad serving, data pass work streams, API/product feed integrations, asset tracking and tagging, performance outputs, creative authoring tools, scalability and quality assurance

Facilitate and cultivate relationships with our preferred platform partners, to establish development best practices and requirements, ensure continued innovation through product advancements/ways of working, and continuity of service through reporting and troubleshooting.
Encourage innovation, adoption of cutting-edge technologies, inclusion, out-of-the-box thinking, cross-team collaboration, self-organization, and diversity

Support PMG on devising strategic roadmaps and onboarding materials for the continued advancements in product development and campaign delivery, QA automation tools and process adoption through our international developer hubs, with POVs on Build, Buy, Borrow.
Become a super-user on new tools developed by PMG Product Teams.
3+ years of experience, including at least 2 years working at a Creative, AdTech and or Digital Agency
Knowledge of digital creative best practices and deliverables
Ability to write scalable and modular code to employ multi-variant testing and dynamic creative industry standards.
Digital Creative Development (front-end) or relevant ad technology background with 6+ years experience
Demonstrated ability to balance campaign objectives and time-to-market requirements without compromising product quality
Strong understanding of IAB Standards and Digital Ad Formats & specifications

Training and Certifications on PMG required DCO ad serving platforms - primary Google GMP and similar knowledge of Clinch, Celtra, Google GMP (DCM, GWD, DCS), Innovid, Smartly, Jivox, Bannerflow, Google Web Designer
Working knowledge of Project Management & DevOps tools like Airtable, JIRA, Smartsheets, Asana, Github
Self-starter with a high degree of self-motivation and initiative with the ability to build processes from the ground up and collaborate in a multi-stakeholder environment
Strong written and verbal communication skills
Detailed oriented with the ability support development team through troubleshooting and Quality assurance
Proficiency in Microsoft Office Tools (Excel, Word, PPT, Outlook, Teams)
Familiarity working with Creative Assets and Design apps such as the Adobe Creative Suite (primarily Photoshop, Illustrator, Adobe XD, Adobe After Effects)
2+ years working with internal and third-party APIs [REST, SOAP, JSON] like Open Weather Map or CRM data
1+ years Python experience or comparable programming language
Understanding of audience analytics capabilities and best practices (measurement, matching, onboarding, workflow, limitations, and workarounds, etc.) in the current environment
Experience working with excel formulas for data matching, aggregation and math
Experience with database systems, Website CMS tools queries and normalization
Basic understanding and ability to work in After Effects as needed.
Working knowledge of data factors driving project success (e.g. PII vs. anonymized data, cookies vs. people, panels vs. census, passive vs. active/survey data, fused vs. matched data, etc.)
Professional Development: Take advantage of our learning and development programs, mentorship opportunities, and career advancement support.
Generous Time Off: Enjoy generous paid time off and holiday allowances to recharge and spend time with loved ones.
Parental Leave: We provide paid parental leave to support your family during important life events.
Retirement & Pension Plans: Plan for your future with competitive retirement or pension programs, including contribution matching.
Fertility and Family Support: Access fertility benefits for all team members and their spouses.
Healthcare: Coverage and support for everyday medical expenses and routine care, tailored by geography.
Pet Insurance: Protect your pet's health and your finances.
Lifestyle Spending Accounts: Enjoy 100% company-funded accounts to promote healthy habits and well-being.
Commuter Benefits: Access support for travel and commuting needs, where available.
Annual Bonus: All employees are eligible for an annual bonus.
Volunteering Opportunities: Receive 8 give-back hours to volunteer in your local communities.
AI Enterprise License: Access AI Enterprise accounts and participate in weekly AI training sessions to empower and ensure AI safety.
description:
About the job

We are PMG

Named Ad Age Best Places to Work for 11 years in a row and Fast Company Best Workplaces for Innovators since 2022, PMG is a global independent marketing services and technology company that seeks to inspire people and brands that anything is possible. Driven by shared success, PMG leverages business strategy and transformation, creative, media, and insights—as well as its proprietary operating system, Alli—to deliver cutting-edge digital solutions and innovative media strategies.

Our team comprises over 900 employees globally, and our work for ambitious brands like Apple, Nike, Sephora, Experian, Therabody, Best Western Hotels & Resorts, Creed Fragrances, BNY, Intuit, CKE Restaurants, Travelex, and Whole Foods spans 85+ countries.

Professional proficiency in written and spoken English is required, including for resume submission and interviews.

Our Creative team is looking for Creative Technology Senior Associate to join a team of multidisciplinary developers, executing best-in-class omni-channel projects leveraging industry leading Advertising Technologies, Ad Servers and Ad builder/DCO [dynamic content optimization] tools.

Working directly with a wide range of tech partners, inter-company and external integrated teams, the Creative Technology Senior Associate will support all dynamic creative and asset automation project buildouts, delivery and integration into ad servers. You should be comfortable working directly with multi-disciplinary team leads across the PMG both in your primary office location and supported global markets.

What You'll Be Doing


Support ad technology, tech integration tests, dynamic [DCO], and omni-channel campaign production efforts across multiple brand teams, geographical locations and products (including new business and new agency tech initiatives)
Contribute to large scale programmatic, social and video campaigns across multiple formats and channels employing underlying code bases such as HTML/JS/CSS, and industry leading ad technology. 
Support in the auditing and defining criteria for all functional and non-functional deliverable requirements including dynamic ad serving, data pass work streams, API/product feed integrations, asset tracking and tagging, performance outputs, creative authoring tools, scalability and quality assurance
Facilitate and cultivate relationships with our preferred platform partners, to establish development best practices and requirements, ensure continued innovation through product advancements/ways of working, and continuity of service through reporting and troubleshooting. 
Encourage innovation, adoption of cutting-edge technologies, inclusion, out-of-the-box thinking, cross-team collaboration, self-organization, and diversity
Support PMG on devising strategic roadmaps and onboarding materials for the continued advancements in product development and campaign delivery, QA automation tools and process adoption through our international developer hubs, with POVs on Build, Buy, Borrow. 
Become a super-user on new tools developed by PMG Product Teams. 


What You'll Bring


3+ years of experience, including at least 2 years working at a Creative, AdTech and or Digital Agency 
Knowledge of digital creative best practices and deliverables
Ability to write scalable and modular code to employ multi-variant testing and dynamic creative industry standards. 
Digital Creative Development (front-end) or relevant ad technology background with 6+ years experience 
Demonstrated ability to balance campaign objectives and time-to-market requirements without compromising product quality
Strong understanding of IAB Standards and Digital Ad Formats & specifications
Training and Certifications on PMG required DCO ad serving platforms - primary Google GMP and similar knowledge of Clinch, Celtra, Google GMP (DCM, GWD, DCS), Innovid, Smartly, Jivox, Bannerflow, Google Web Designer
Working knowledge of Project Management & DevOps tools like Airtable, JIRA, Smartsheets, Asana, Github
Self-starter with a high degree of self-motivation and initiative with the ability to build processes from the ground up and collaborate in a multi-stakeholder environment
Strong written and verbal communication skills
Detailed oriented with the ability support development team through troubleshooting and Quality assurance
Proficiency in Microsoft Office Tools (Excel, Word, PPT, Outlook, Teams) 
Familiarity working with Creative Assets and Design apps such as the Adobe Creative Suite (primarily Photoshop, Illustrator, Adobe XD, Adobe After Effects)


Preferred Skills


2+ years working with internal and third-party APIs [REST, SOAP, JSON] like Open Weather Map or CRM data
1+ years Python experience or comparable programming language 
Understanding of audience analytics capabilities and best practices (measurement, matching, onboarding, workflow, limitations, and workarounds, etc.) in the current environment
Experience working with excel formulas for data matching, aggregation and math
Experience with database systems, Website CMS tools queries and normalization
Basic understanding and ability to work in After Effects as needed. 
Working knowledge of data factors driving project success (e.g. PII vs. anonymized data, cookies vs. people, panels vs. census, passive vs. active/survey data, fused vs. matched data, etc.)


What We Offer


Professional Development: Take advantage of our learning and development programs, mentorship opportunities, and career advancement support. 
Generous Time Off: Enjoy generous paid time off and holiday allowances to recharge and spend time with loved ones. 
Parental Leave: We provide paid parental leave to support your family during important life events. 
Retirement & Pension Plans: Plan for your future with competitive retirement or pension programs, including contribution matching. 
Fertility and Family Support: Access fertility benefits for all team members and their spouses. 
Healthcare: Coverage and support for everyday medical expenses and routine care, tailored by geography. 
Pet Insurance: Protect your pet's health and your finances. 
Lifestyle Spending Accounts: Enjoy 100% company-funded accounts to promote healthy habits and well-being. 
Commuter Benefits: Access support for travel and commuting needs, where available. 
Annual Bonus: All employees are eligible for an annual bonus. 
Volunteering Opportunities: Receive 8 give-back hours to volunteer in your local communities. 
AI Enterprise License: Access AI Enterprise accounts and participate in weekly AI training sessions to empower and ensure AI safety. 


Benefits vary by country, location, and geography to reflect local laws, norms, and expectations.

What Sets Us Apart

Being part of PMG means joining a company culture that’s unmatched in digital. We're dedicated to working hard to serve our employees and clients, delivering value, results, and innovation—which often requires true grit and agility. We believe in taking care of ourselves and each other to continuously improve in every way.

In alignment with our core values to be inclusive and always change for the better, PMG is committed to creating a more diverse and inclusive culture, and we are proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We believe that we only change for the better by bringing diverse perspectives to our company. PMG recruits, employs, trains, compensates, and promotes regardless of race, color, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, genetics, disability, age, or veteran status.

description:
About the job


What Makes Us Unique 



At Cloudbeds, we're not just building software, we’re transforming hospitality. Our intelligently designed platform powers properties across 150 countries, processing billions in bookings annually. From independent properties to hotel groups, we help hoteliers transform operations and uplevel their commercial strategy through a unified platform that integrates with hundreds of partners. And we do it with a completely remote team. Imagine working alongside global innovators to build AI-powered solutions that solve hoteliers' biggest challenges. Since our founding in 2012, we've become the World's Best Hotel PMS Solutions Provider and landed on Deloitte's Technology Fast 500 again in 2024 – but we're just getting started. 

 How You'll Make an Impact:



As a Customer Growth Manager (CGM) your focus will be exclusively on upselling and cross-selling to existing customers through outbound and product-led growth initiatives. You should be a dynamic and results-driven individual. 

You will build and manage a customer pipeline that aims to achieve above-quota sales performance. You will ensure that deals are of high quality with minimal post-sale fallout. 

What You Bring to the Team:


Meet and exceed ambitious targets and revenue goals set by the customer growth management team.
Develop and maintain strong relationships with key stakeholders and decision-makers.
Proactively identify upselling opportunities within the existing customer base through strategic account analysis and regular communication.
Understand the unique needs and challenges of each customer and position additional software products or features that align with their business objectives.
Prepare and deliver persuasive sales presentations, proposals, and product demonstrations that are tailored to each customer's specific needs.
Collaborate with the onboarding and product teams to ensure smooth implementation of upsold products. 
Keep abreast of industry trends, competitor activities, and market developments to identify new upselling opportunities and stay ahead of the curve.
What Sets You Up for Success:


Proven track record of success in sales, with a focus on upselling or account expansion, preferably within the hotel tech space.
Experience and/or education in hospitality operations.
Strong understanding of hospitality industry trends and challenges.
Ability to build and maintain long-term relationships with clients.
Problem-solving mindset, with a demonstrated ability to analyze customer requirements and propose tailored solutions.
Excellent communication and negotiation skills, with the ability to convey complex technical concepts to non-technical stakeholders.
Self-motivated and goal-oriented, with a relentless drive to exceed targets.
Proficiency with Salesforce, Google Business Suite, Groove, Zendesk, Canva, Generative A.I.
Willingness to travel as required ( < 10% ) 
Bonus Skills to Stand Out:


Demonstrable success in selling PMS or other hotel tech software.
Languages: English and Spanish fluency REQUIRED. Portuguese is a bonus!
 
What to Expect - Your Journey with Us 



Behind Cloudbeds' revolutionary technology is a team of redefining what's possible in hospitality. We're 650+ employees across 40+ countries, bringing together elite engineers, AI architects, world-class designers, and hospitality veterans to solve challenges others haven't dared to tackle. Our diverse team speaks 30+ languages, but we all share one language: a passion for innovation and travel. From pioneering breakthroughs in machine learning to revolutionizing how hotels operate, we're not just watching the future of hospitality unfold – we're coding it, designing it, writing it and shipping it. If you're ready to work alongside some of the brightest minds in tech who are obsessed with using AI to transform a trillion-dollar industry, this is your chance to be part of something extraordinary.

Learn more online at cloudbeds.com


Company Awards to Check Out! 


Best All-In-One Hotel Management System | HotelTechAwards (2025)
Overall 10 Best Places to Work | HotelTechAwards (2025)
Most Loved Workplace® Certified (2024) 
Top 10 People’s Choice(2024)
Deloitte Technology Fast 500 (2024)
 
Discover our Benefits:


Remote First, Remote Always 
PTO in accordance with local labor requirements
Monthly Wellness Fridays - enjoy an extra long weekend every month
Full Paid Parental Leave
Home office stipend based on country of residency
Professional development courses in Cloudbeds University
Access to professional development, including manager training, upskilling and knowledge transfer.

Everyone is Welcome - A Culture of Inclusion 



Cloudbeds is proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer that celebrates the diversity in our global team! We do not discriminate based upon race, religion, color, national origin, gender (including pregnancy, childbirth, or related medical conditions), sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, age, status as a protected veteran, status as an individual with a disability, or other applicable legally protected characteristics.

Cloudbeds is committed to the full inclusion of all qualified individuals. As part of this commitment, Cloudbeds will ensure that persons with disabilities are provided reasonable accommodations in the hiring process. We encourage deaf, hard of hearing, deaf-blind, and deaf-disabled individuals to apply. If reasonable accommodation is needed to participate in the job application or interview process or to perform essential job functions, please contact our HR team by phone at (858) 201-7832 or via email at accommodations@cloudbeds.com. Cloudbeds will provide an American Sign Language (ASL) interpreter where needed as a reasonable accommodation for the hiring processes.

To all Staffing and Recruiting Agencies: Our Careers Site is only for individuals seeking a job at Cloudbeds. Staffing, recruiting agencies, and individuals being represented by an agency are not authorized to use this site or to submit applications, and any such submissions will be considered unsolicited. Cloudbeds does not accept unsolicited resumes or applications from agencies. Please do not forward resumes to our jobs alias, Cloudbeds employees, or any other company location. Cloudbeds is not responsible for any fees related to unsolicited resumes/applications.

title: Founding Growth Lead
skills: If you've taken an app from quiet to growing and want to do it again as an owner, not an employee — message me.
description:
About the job

I'm hiring a Founding Growth Lead for TalkPilot.




TalkPilot is an AI conversation co-pilot for iOS, Mac, and Apple Watch. The product is live and it's good. Distribution is the problem — and that's the entire job.




I'm a strong builder and a weak distributor. I need the opposite of me: someone who wakes up thinking about acquisition channels, activation, and retention curves, and knows how to turn a launched app into a growing one.




What you'd own:

 → The full growth function — paid (Apple Search Ads, Meta), ASO, organic, content, lifecycle

 → Finding the one channel that actually works and pouring fuel on it

 → The numbers: CAC, install→paid conversion, retention




This is a founding role. The equity is real and meaningful. The salary is early-stage and below what a funded company pays — that's the trade for ownership. Mexico City preferred, remote possible for the right person.




If you've taken an app from quiet to growing and want to do it again as an owner, not an employee — message me.

title: Senior SEO Content Strategist
skills: Own and execute the organic growth roadmap — keyword strategy, competitive benchmarking, prioritization, and quarterly KPI targets.
Lead technical SEO: site architecture, crawlability, page speed, schema markup, internal linking, and Local SEO across CoinFlip’s kiosk footprint (Google Business Profiles).
Partner with developers and designers to ship technical improvements and prevent regressions on launches.
Maintain SEO tooling stack (GSC, GA, SEMrush/Ahrefs, Screaming Frog) and report on performance with actionable insights.
Develop and execute the content strategy across blog, landing pages, and educational guides.
Write and edit high-quality, SEO-optimized content that translates complex crypto and fintech concepts into clear, accurate, user-friendly material.
Run regular content audits; refresh, consolidate, or sunset underperforming pages.
Maintain CoinFlip’s tone of voice and ensure content meets compliance review standards.

Partner with the Communications team on data-led digital PR campaigns — supplying original research, industry data studies, and newsworthy assets designed to earn backlinks from crypto, fintech, and mainstream publications.
Source and respond to reporter requests via HARO, Qwoted, and similar platforms in coordination with Communications, producing expert commentary on a tight turnaround.
Identify backlink targets and feed a shared target publication list with Communications.
Track domain authority, referring domains, and link quality; report on link velocity and the SEO impact of earned coverage.
Partner cross-functionally with product, compliance, design, growth, and communications on launches, campaigns, and integrated initiatives.
Other duties as assigned.
5–7 years of combined SEO and content experience, ideally in fintech, crypto, or another regulated industry.
Demonstrated track record of driving organic traffic and ranking growth as both strategist and individual contributor — portfolio of published content and link-earning campaigns required.
Deep technical SEO knowledge: search algorithms, site architecture, page speed, schema markup, Core Web Vitals.
Strong writer and editor — able to produce publication-quality content on complex financial topics without sacrificing accuracy.
Hands-on experience supporting digital PR / link-building via HARO, Qwoted, or similar.
Proficiency with GSC, GA, SEMrush, Ahrefs, Screaming Frog, and a CMS (WordPress, Drupal, or similar).
Working knowledge of HTML/CSS.
Strong analytical skills; able to translate data into strategy and into clear written narratives.
Excellent communication and cross-functional collaboration skills.
Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Communications, Journalism, Business, or a related field.
Working knowledge of cryptocurrency, blockchain, and fintech.
Experience optimizing Local SEO via Google Business Profiles at scale.
Familiarity with how SEO integrates with paid media, email, and social.
Basic data visualization (Tableau, Looker, or similar) for building data-led link-earning studies.
description:
About the job

Description

CoinFlip is a global digital currency platform company, focused on providing consumers simple and secure access to buy and sell cryptocurrency. The company operates the world's largest network of cryptocurrency kiosks by transaction volume with more than 5,500 kiosks across 49 U.S. states, Puerto Rico, Canada, Australia, New Zealand, South Africa, Italy, Panama, Brazil Mexico, and Spain. CoinFlip’s digital currency kiosks make buying and selling major cryptocurrencies accessible and safe for consumers who wish to purchase their digital currency using cash. CoinFlip also operates CoinFlip Preferred, a personalized over-the-counter service that provides investors with custom, white glove support for their cryptocurrency transactions. In 2022, CoinFlip launched CoinFlip Ventures, an investment group offering coaching, funding, and networking support to early-stage crypto and web3 projects.

CoinFlip was founded in 2015 by Daniel Polotsky, Kris Dayrit, Alan Gurevich, and Ben Weiss. Headquartered in Chicago, CoinFlip placed in the top 500 on the 2021, 2022, and 2023 Inc. 5000 list, and on the 2022 and 2023 Deloitte Technology Fast 500, was named the 2021 and 2022 #1 fastest-growing company in Chicago by Crain's, ranked in Chicago Tribune’s Top Workplaces in 2021 and 2022, and was awarded the 2021 and 2022 Stevie ® Awards for Customer Service. To learn more about CoinFlip and how to get started on your digital currency journey, visit www.CoinFlip.tech.

CoinFlip is looking for a Senior SEO Content Strategist to own organic growth end-to-end — from strategy and technical SEO through content production and digital PR. This is a hands-on hybrid role for someone who can think strategically about keyword opportunities and competitive positioning, execute the technical work to capture them, write the content that ranks and converts, and partner with communications to earn the high-authority backlinks that compound it all. You’ll be the single point of accountability for organic traffic and organic-sourced conversions across CoinFlip’s web properties, partnering with product, compliance, design, growth, and communications to translate complex crypto and fintech topics into content people search for and publications cite.

Responsibilities


Own and execute the organic growth roadmap — keyword strategy, competitive benchmarking, prioritization, and quarterly KPI targets. 
Lead technical SEO: site architecture, crawlability, page speed, schema markup, internal linking, and Local SEO across CoinFlip’s kiosk footprint (Google Business Profiles). 
Partner with developers and designers to ship technical improvements and prevent regressions on launches. 
Maintain SEO tooling stack (GSC, GA, SEMrush/Ahrefs, Screaming Frog) and report on performance with actionable insights. 
Develop and execute the content strategy across blog, landing pages, and educational guides. 
Write and edit high-quality, SEO-optimized content that translates complex crypto and fintech concepts into clear, accurate, user-friendly material. 
Run regular content audits; refresh, consolidate, or sunset underperforming pages. 
Maintain CoinFlip’s tone of voice and ensure content meets compliance review standards. 
Partner with the Communications team on data-led digital PR campaigns — supplying original research, industry data studies, and newsworthy assets designed to earn backlinks from crypto, fintech, and mainstream publications. 
Source and respond to reporter requests via HARO, Qwoted, and similar platforms in coordination with Communications, producing expert commentary on a tight turnaround. 
Identify backlink targets and feed a shared target publication list with Communications. 
Track domain authority, referring domains, and link quality; report on link velocity and the SEO impact of earned coverage. 
Partner cross-functionally with product, compliance, design, growth, and communications on launches, campaigns, and integrated initiatives. 
Other duties as assigned. 


Qualifications


5–7 years of combined SEO and content experience, ideally in fintech, crypto, or another regulated industry. 
Demonstrated track record of driving organic traffic and ranking growth as both strategist and individual contributor — portfolio of published content and link-earning campaigns required. 
Deep technical SEO knowledge: search algorithms, site architecture, page speed, schema markup, Core Web Vitals. 
Strong writer and editor — able to produce publication-quality content on complex financial topics without sacrificing accuracy. 
Hands-on experience supporting digital PR / link-building via HARO, Qwoted, or similar. 
Proficiency with GSC, GA, SEMrush, Ahrefs, Screaming Frog, and a CMS (WordPress, Drupal, or similar). 
Working knowledge of HTML/CSS. 
Strong analytical skills; able to translate data into strategy and into clear written narratives. 
Excellent communication and cross-functional collaboration skills. 


Nice To Have


Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Communications, Journalism, Business, or a related field. 
Working knowledge of cryptocurrency, blockchain, and fintech. 
Experience optimizing Local SEO via Google Business Profiles at scale. 
Familiarity with how SEO integrates with paid media, email, and social. 
Basic data visualization (Tableau, Looker, or similar) for building data-led link-earning studies. 


Compensation

Expected Annual Salary: $30,000 - $50,000 USD

Working at CoinFlip means collaborating with experienced and innovative leaders who share a clear vision and a track record of success. We offer a collaborative and positive working environment where we encourage employees to balance productivity with time to recharge. Compensation is above and beyond a typical “startup” — we offer competitive salaries, performance-based incentives, and competitive benefits for full-time employees.

CoinFlip values diversity in the workplace and is an equal opportunity employer committed to providing an inclusive and accessible work environment. We thank all candidates who apply, but only those selected for an interview will be contacted.

By applying to this role, you give express consent to CoinFlip to send you informational text (SMS) messages regarding this role and the application process. You can cancel the SMS service at any time by replying "STOP" to the text message you received. If at any time you forget what keywords are supported, just reply "HELP." Message and data rates apply. If you require a special accommodation, please let us know and we’ll work with you to meet your needs.

skills: +2 years of hands-on experience with WordPress and front-end development.
